In `teamserver/src/dispatch.rs`, the new `parse_checkin_metadata` function converts the protocol `working_hours` field (u32) to `Option<i32>` using `i32::try_from(working_hours).unwrap_or(i32::MAX)` (line ~858). Any agent sending a working_hours bitmask with the high bit set (e.g. 0x80000000–0xFFFFFFFF) will silently have its schedule replaced with i32::MAX rather than being rejected or stored correctly.

This is the same silent-saturation pattern already documented in red-cell-c2-2x0k (DEMON_INIT path). That bug is unresolved, and the new COMMAND_CHECKIN path (which can rotate the working_hours schedule mid-session) now inherits the same defect.

Correct behaviour: store working_hours as u32 to match the protocol, or reject out-of-range values with an explicit error, consistent with whatever fix is applied to red-cell-c2-2x0k.
close_reason: Preserved raw working-hours bitmasks for check-in and config updates, with regression coverage for high-bit values.

title: axum::serve errors silently swallowed in webhook and audit test helpers
description:
Introduced by: Codex

teamserver/src/webhook.rs line 519 and teamserver/src/audit.rs lines 813 and 830 use let _ = axum::serve(listener, app).await inside spawned Tokio tasks. If the mock HTTP server fails to bind or exits with an error, the error is silently discarded. This means test-helper server failures will cause dependent tests to time out or produce confusing assertion errors with no indication of root cause.

Fix: replace with .expect("test webhook server should not fail") or log the error with tracing::error! so failures are immediately visible.
close_reason: Replaced let _ = axum::serve(...).await with .expect() in webhook.rs, audit.rs (both helpers), and websocket.rs so test server failures are immediately visible

title: test: EventBus history ring buffer eviction at capacity=1 is untested
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/events.rs
**Gap type**: edge case / boundary condition

The existing `zero_capacity_history_does_not_retain_logs` and `recent_teamserver_logs_retain_only_log_events` tests cover capacity=0 and capacity=2. The boundary case **capacity=1** exercises the degenerate path where every new log message immediately evicts the only existing entry.

Two specific sub-cases are missing:

1. **capacity=1, second log evicts first**: broadcast two TeamserverLog events on a bus with capacity=1; `recent_teamserver_logs()` must return only the second message. Currently the ring-buffer logic at line 59 (`if history.len() == self.history_capacity { history.pop_front(); }`) is exercised by the capacity=2 test but the single-slot case has a different memory access pattern that is easy to get wrong (e.g. pop-before-push vs push-before-pop).

2. **Wrap-around preserves ordering**: for capacity=N, broadcast N+2 messages and assert the returned slice is the last N messages in correct chronological order. The capacity=2 test only checks that old messages are evicted but not that the order is preserved across multiple evictions.

**Suggested test approach**:
Add two `#[tokio::test]` cases: one with capacity=1 that broadcasts two messages and asserts only the second is retained; one with capacity=3 that broadcasts five messages and asserts the last three are returned in order.

title: quality: no WebSocket max message size configured — authenticated operator can force 64 MiB allocation
description:
Introduced by: Michel Klomp (Claude Sonnet 4.6)

teamserver/src/websocket.rs — No explicit max WebSocket message size is set on the
axum/tungstenite connection. The tungstenite default is 64 MiB per frame. A connected
(authenticated) operator could send an oversized frame and force a ~64 MiB allocation
server-side.

Severity is moderate because it requires valid operator credentials. In a multi-tenant
deployment where operators have different trust levels, a lower-privilege operator
(Analyst role) could still cause this allocation.

Fix: configure the WebSocket connection with a sensible max message size (e.g. 1 MiB,
matching typical operator command payloads) using the axum WebSocketUpgrade::max_message_size
method.
close_reason: Configured a 1 MiB websocket max message size and added regression coverage for oversized frames.

title: security: shutdown race in AuditWebhookNotifier allows webhook delivery after shutdown completes
description:
Introduced by: Codex <noreply@openai.com>

teamserver/src/webhook.rs — notify_audit_record_detached checks closing.load() then increments pending.fetch_add(). Between these two operations, shutdown() can observe pending==0 and return true (claiming all deliveries are drained) before the new spawn increments the counter. The spawned task then runs after the caller believes shutdown is complete.

While the Arc'd reqwest client prevents a use-after-free, the shutdown guarantee ('returns true only when all deliveries are complete') is violated. Any resource teardown that depends on this guarantee is unsound.

Fix: use a compare-and-swap approach: attempt to increment pending first, then check whether closing was set concurrently; if so, decrement and discard.
close_reason: Reversed the check order in notify_audit_record_detached: increment pending first, then check closing; if closing was set concurrently, decrement and wake shutdown. Regression test added.

title: Session token lookup is not constant-time (timing oracle)
description:
Introduced by: Codex

teamserver/src/auth.rs — SessionRegistry::get_by_token() at line 394 uses BTreeMap::get() which performs a standard string comparison. String comparison is not constant-time in Rust: it short-circuits on the first unequal byte. An attacker who can observe response latency with high precision (e.g. via a side-channel over a local network) can use this to enumerate valid session tokens byte by byte. Fix: replace the BTreeMap token lookup with a linear scan using subtle::ConstantTimeEq or a pre-hashed token digest keyed by HMAC-SHA256 so that mismatched tokens produce equal-length fixed-size comparison operands.
close_reason: Replaced BTreeMap::get token lookup with constant-time linear scan using subtle::ConstantTimeEq; added three regression tests

title: No cap on agent registry size — unauthenticated DEMON_INIT flood exhausts memory and SQLite
description:
Introduced by: Codex (original agent registry design)

crates/teamserver/src/agents.rs — AgentRegistry.entries (HashMap<u32, Arc<AgentEntry>>) has no maximum capacity. Every DEMON_INIT received by an HTTP, SMB, or DNS listener inserts a new entry and a corresponding SQLite row (via database::AgentRepository::create).

An attacker who can reach the agent-facing listener port (which is intentionally internet-exposed for real implants) can flood DEMON_INIT packets with distinct agent_id values (u32 → 4 billion distinct IDs). Each fake registration:
- allocates an AgentEntry with its own RwLock, Mutex, VecDeque, and HashMap
- persists an agents row + encryption row to SQLite
- broadcasts an AgentNew event to all connected operators

Combined with the zero-key bypass (red-cell-c2-2d4), crafting valid registration packets requires no crypto knowledge. Without a rate limit or max-agent cap, a single attacker machine can OOM the teamserver in minutes.

Fix: add a configurable MAX_REGISTERED_AGENTS limit (suggested default: 10 000) to AgentRegistry::insert, returning an error when exceeded. Also consider per-IP rate limiting on DEMON_INIT acceptance (distinct from the operator login rate limiter).
close_reason: Added configurable agent registry cap enforcement on insert and startup load, with regression coverage for both DEMON_INIT and persisted-agent overflow paths.

title: Payload Builder - emit DotNetNamePipe in Demon config packing
description: The payload builder parses Demon.DotNetNamePipe from YAOTL profiles but never includes it when packing the build configuration for generated Demon payloads. Implement the remaining config packing needed for .NET output transport compatibility and add tests covering profile defaults and wire layout.
close_reason: Packed DotNetNamePipe into Demon build config bytes and added profile-default plus wire-layout regression tests.

title: bug: kill_date u64→i64 overflow silently disables kill date enforcement
description:
Introduced by: Codex <noreply@openai.com>

teamserver/src/demon.rs:319

```rust
kill_date: (kill_date != 0).then_some(i64::try_from(kill_date).unwrap_or(i64::MAX)),
```

## What is wrong

The Demon INIT payload carries kill_date as a u64. Any value greater than i64::MAX
(9,223,372,036,854,775,807 — roughly year 292 million) is silently converted to i64::MAX
instead of returning an error or clamping to a meaningful sentinel. In practice, a Demon
compiled with a malformed kill_date field (e.g., 0xFFFF_FFFF_FFFF_FFFF) would be registered
with kill_date = i64::MAX, which is effectively 'never expire'. The kill_date is never
enforced so the agent runs indefinitely past any intended expiration.

## Why it matters

Kill dates are an operational security control. Silently disabling them for agents that report
out-of-range kill_date values is a correctness bug that could leave implants running longer
than authorized.

## Fix

Return DemonParserError::InvalidInit for kill_date values that do not fit in i64, or clamp to
i64::MAX with a warning log. At minimum, add a test for kill_date > i64::MAX.
close_reason: Rejected out-of-range kill_date values in demon init, checkin metadata, and config callbacks with regression coverage.

title: BehindRedirector trusts spoofable client IP headers
description:
Introduced by: Codex <noreply@openai.com>

crates/teamserver/src/listeners.rs:2239-2258 accepts X-Forwarded-For and X-Real-IP from any peer whenever behind_redirector is enabled. crates/teamserver/src/listeners.rs:2154-2172 then feeds that derived IP into DEMON_INIT rate limiting and persists it as the agent's ExternalIP.

There is no trusted-proxy allowlist or peer verification, so any client that can reach the listener directly can spoof arbitrary source IPs, bypass the per-IP DEMON_INIT limiter, and poison operator-visible attribution. The fix is to only trust forwarding headers from explicitly configured proxy peers/networks, otherwise fall back to the actual socket peer address.
close_reason: Only trust forwarded client IP headers from explicitly configured proxy IPs/CIDRs; otherwise use the socket peer address.

title: havoc_compatibility test is tied to an absolute checkout path and Go toolchain
description:
Introduced by: Codex <noreply@openai.com>

teamserver/tests/havoc_compatibility.rs:260-269 and teamserver/tests/havoc_compatibility.rs:333-342 make the new workspace test depend on `go run` plus a hardcoded replace path of `/home/michel/Red-Cell-C2/src/Havoc/teamserver`.

This passes only on machines that use this exact checkout path and have Go installed. On any other checkout location, or on Rust-only CI/dev environments without Go, `cargo test --workspace` will fail before the Havoc compatibility assertion even runs.

Correct behavior: resolve the Havoc reference directory relative to the repo at runtime (or via an environment/config hook), and either document/gate the Go dependency or skip the test cleanly when the prerequisite toolchain is unavailable.
close_reason: Resolved the Havoc test path dynamically and skip cleanly when Go or the reference checkout is unavailable

title: test: deserialize_u16_from_any port-overflow rejection path is untested
description:
**File**: common/src/domain.rs
**Gap type**: edge case / error path

`deserialize_u16_from_any` (and `deserialize_optional_u16_from_any`) delegate to `u16::try_from` and return an error for values > 65535. This is used for `port_bind` and `port_conn` on every `HttpListenerConfig` and `HttpListenerProxyConfig`.

No test supplies a port value outside [0, 65535] and asserts the rejection. A broken or removed bounds check would silently truncate large port numbers without any test catching it.

**Suggested test approach**:
Construct an `HttpListenerConfig` JSON payload with `"port_bind": 70000` (a string or integer) and assert `serde_json::from_value` returns an error. Similarly test `"port": "99999"` in the proxy config.
close_reason: Added three tests covering port overflow rejection: port_bind integer 70000, port_conn string 99999, and proxy port string 99999 all assert the 'does not fit in u16' error

title: test: no test for AES-CTR key-rotation boundary in handle_checkin
description:
Introduced by: Codex <noreply@openai.com>

teamserver/src/dispatch.rs:780-782 — The checkin path that replaces an agent's AES key+IV
and resets the CTR offset to 0 has no dedicated unit test. The test suite covers the
unknown-agent checkin error path but not the happy path for key rotation.

Without a test that: (1) registers an agent with key A, (2) sends some encrypted traffic
advancing the CTR offset, (3) sends a checkin with key B resetting the offset, and (4)
verifies subsequent traffic is encrypted with key B at offset 0 — it is easy to introduce
a keystream-reuse regression (same key+IV+offset used for two different plaintexts).

This is the most security-sensitive CTR state transition in the codebase.

Fix: add a unit test for handle_checkin with new AES credentials that asserts the CTR
offset is reset and the new key is used for subsequent decryption.
close_reason: Added dispatch coverage for AES key rotation, CTR offset reset, and post-checkin encryption from block zero.

title: encrypt_agent_data silently returns empty Vec on invalid key or IV length
description:
Introduced by: Codex <noreply@openai.com>

crates/common/src/crypto.rs:51-52 — `encrypt_agent_data` swallows the `CryptoError` returned by `apply_agent_keystream` and returns an empty `Vec<u8>` instead. Callers in `agents.rs:522` and `dispatch.rs:448` build serialized job payloads using the return value without checking whether encryption actually succeeded.

If a stored key or IV is malformed (e.g., a mis-encoded base64 value), the agent will silently receive an empty encrypted response instead of an error being surfaced to the operator.

Fix: change the return type of `encrypt_agent_data` to `Result<Vec<u8>, CryptoError>` and update all call sites to propagate the error.
notes: Cursor agent (Ubuntu-HTB-cursor) claimed this task but ran out of credits before starting implementation. Working tree was left clean — no code changes were made. Pick up from scratch.
close_reason: Changed encrypt_agent_data to return Result and updated callers/tests to surface crypto errors

title: test: EventReceiver::recv does not test the channel-closed path
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/events.rs
**Gap type**: error path

The `EventReceiver::recv` method (line 88-99) handles three outcomes: success, `RecvError::Lagged`, and `RecvError::Closed`. The `Lagged` path is tested (`lagging_subscriptions_are_dropped`), but the `Closed` path is never exercised. This path is triggered when the `EventBus` itself is dropped while a subscriber still holds a live receiver. Without a test, the behaviour — that `recv()` returns `None` and `is_closed()` becomes `true` — could silently regress if the channel is ever swapped for a different implementation.

**Suggested test approach**:
Create an `EventBus`, subscribe to it, then drop the bus. Call `receiver.recv().await` and assert it returns `None`. Also assert `receiver.is_closed()` is `true` and that a second call to `recv()` also returns `None` immediately.
close_reason: Added closed_bus_causes_recv_to_return_none test: drops the EventBus, verifies recv() returns None, is_closed() is true, and a second recv() also returns None immediately

title: architecture: teamserver control-plane TLS certificate rotates on every restart
description:
Introduced by: Codex <noreply@openai.com>

teamserver/src/main.rs:173-176 always calls `resolve_tls_identity(&subject_alt_names, None, ...)`, and common/src/tls.rs:170-178 only reuses an existing certificate when a cert/key config is supplied. Because the teamserver main path always passes `None`, the operator WebSocket/API listener gets a fresh self-signed certificate every time the process starts.

Impact: any operator client using certificate fingerprint pinning or a manually trusted self-signed cert has to re-trust the teamserver after every restart. This undermines the new client-side TLS hardening and makes stable certificate trust impossible for the control plane.

Fix: add durable control-plane TLS configuration (or persisted generated PEMs) for the teamserver itself and plumb those paths through `build_tls_config`, so restarts reuse the same certificate material instead of regenerating it.
close_reason: Added resolve_or_persist_tls_identity to common/src/tls.rs (generates+saves PEMs on first boot, reloads on subsequent boots). Added optional Cert block to TeamserverConfig for explicit cert paths. Updated build_tls_config to derive auto-persist paths from the profile path and use the new helper.

title: Payload builder appends non-Havoc DotNetNamePipe field to Demon config
description:
Introduced by: Codex

crates/teamserver/src/payload_builder.rs:512 and crates/teamserver/src/payload_builder.rs:606 now inject DotNetNamePipe into the packed Demon config blob. The Havoc reference builder ends the config immediately after the listener-specific fields (src/Havoc/teamserver/pkg/common/builder/builder.go:850-1021) and never serializes this field. Adding the extra trailing wide string changes the Demon config layout, so generated payloads no longer match Havocs wire format and can misparse on the original Demon side. The correct behavior is to keep DotNetNamePipe out of the packed config and, if needed, use it only in the teamserver-side runtime path generation like Havoc does.
close_reason: Removed DotNetNamePipe from packed Demon config to restore Havoc-compatible layout

title: Typo "impersonat" in token callback error message
description:
In crates/teamserver/src/dispatch.rs line 479, the error message reads "Failed to impersonat {user}" — missing the trailing "e" in "impersonate". This is displayed verbatim in the operator console.

Fix: change to "Failed to impersonate {user}".
close_reason: Typo already fixed — line 2262 of dispatch.rs correctly reads 'Failed to impersonate {user}'

title: test: sweep_dead_agents_at with mixed-liveness agents only marks stale ones dead
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/agent_liveness.rs
**Gap type**: edge case / correctness

Every existing sweep test uses exactly one agent. No test verifies the sweep correctly handles a registry with a mix of stale and recently-active agents. A bug in `collect_stale_agents` (e.g., wrong timeout threshold comparison) could cause recently-checked-in agents to be killed alongside stale ones, but no test would catch it.

The returned `dead_agent_ids` Vec is also sorted; this sort is untested with more than one entry.

**Suggested test approach**:
Insert two agents: one with `last_call_in` well past the timeout and one with a recent `last_call_in`. Call `sweep_dead_agents_at` at a fixed `now`. Assert only the stale agent appears in the returned Vec and that the recently-active agent remains `active = true` in the registry.
close_reason: Added sweep_mixed_liveness_only_marks_stale_agents_dead test: 2 stale agents + 1 fresh, asserts only stale appear in sorted dead_agent_ids and fresh remains active

title: Build broken: 3 filesystem callback handlers called but not defined
description:
dispatch.rs calls handle_filesystem_callback, handle_command_output_callback, and handle_beacon_output_callback in two places each, but none of these functions are defined anywhere in the codebase. This causes 6 compile errors and prevents the workspace from building.

Affected locations:
- crates/teamserver/src/dispatch.rs:172 (handle_filesystem_callback in handler closure)
- crates/teamserver/src/dispatch.rs:205 (handle_command_output_callback in handler closure)
- crates/teamserver/src/dispatch.rs:220 (handle_beacon_output_callback in handler closure)
- crates/teamserver/src/dispatch.rs:611 (handle_filesystem_callback in route_callback_by_command)
- crates/teamserver/src/dispatch.rs:621 (handle_command_output_callback in route_callback_by_command)
- crates/teamserver/src/dispatch.rs:624 (handle_beacon_output_callback in route_callback_by_command)

These must be implemented as async fns in dispatch.rs to fix the build. The filesystem callback should parse DemonCallback variants (CALLBACK_FILE for chunked download reassembly, CALLBACK_FILE_NOTFOUND, CALLBACK_OUTPUT, etc.) and broadcast results to operators. See the DemonCallback enum in common for the expected message types.
close_reason: Implemented the missing filesystem callback handlers and fixed shared download tracker reuse in builtin dispatch

title: test: add AgentRepository coverage for listener-bound persistence helpers
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/database.rs
**Functions with no tests**:
- fn connect_with_options (line 125) — opens a SQLite pool from explicit sqlx connection options and runs migrations.
- fn create_with_listener (line 219) — inserts a new agent row while recording which listener accepted the session.
- fn create_with_listener_and_ctr_offset (line 228) — inserts a new agent row with listener metadata and the initial CTR block offset.
- fn update_with_listener (line 246) — updates a persisted agent row and its listener association.
- fn set_note (line 364) — persists the operator-authored note for an agent.
- fn get_persisted (line 375) — loads a persisted agent including listener name and CTR state.
- fn list_persisted (line 388) — lists persisted agents including listener name and CTR state.
- fn set_ctr_block_offset (line 397) — updates the stored AES-CTR block offset for an agent session.

**Why this matters**: these are core database write/read paths for agent registration, routing metadata, and crypto state. If any of them drift, agent reconnection, note persistence, or CTR resumption can fail silently after restart.

**Minimum test scenarios needed**:
1. Happy path: create/update flows persist listener name, note, and CTR offset and get_persisted/list_persisted read them back accurately.
2. Error path: connect_with_options migrates a fresh database and surfaces invalid-path/open failures cleanly; repository updates against missing agents return the expected failure.
3. Edge case: create_with_listener_and_ctr_offset preserves nonzero CTR offsets across a reload and list_persisted returns deterministic ordering.

**Test location**: teamserver/tests/database.rs
close_reason: Added database integration coverage for listener-aware agent persistence helpers and enforced missing-agent update failures.

title: test: bool_from_i64 rejects negative values in addition to values > 1
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/database.rs
**Gap type**: edge case / boundary

`bool_from_i64` is tested for 0 → false, 1 → true, and 2 → error. But SQLite INTEGER columns can store negative values, and the out-of-range test only checks 2. A value like -1 is equally invalid and equally likely to appear in a corrupt or manually edited database. If the function also rejects -1 (which it should), a test would document and lock in that contract.

**Suggested test approach**:
Add assertions: `bool_from_i64("active", -1).is_err()`, `bool_from_i64("active", i64::MIN).is_err()`, and `bool_from_i64("active", i64::MAX).is_err()`.
close_reason: Added -1, i64::MIN, and i64::MAX assertions to bool_from_i64_rejects_out_of_range_values test

title: test: parse_working_hours missing-separator rejection path is untested
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/payload_builder.rs
**Gap type**: error path

`parse_working_hours` splits on `'-'` (line 1149) and returns `InvalidRequest` when the separator is absent. This branch is never tested. A caller providing a malformed string like `"0800"` or `"08:00:17:00"` should fail with a clear error, but without a test this path could be broken without notice — especially because the only test exercises the happy path `"08:00-17:00"`.

**Suggested test approach**:
Call `parse_working_hours(Some("0800-1700"))` (missing colons, wrong parse), `parse_working_hours(Some("junk"))` (no separator), and assert each returns `Err(PayloadBuildError::InvalidRequest { .. })`.
close_reason: Added three tests covering the missing-separator rejection path: bare digits, junk input, and colon-only format

title: client: loot panel — filtering, credential categorization, and export
description: The loot panel shows a basic list. Add: filter by type (credentials, files, tokens), filter by agent, filter by time range. Credential categorization: NTLM hash, plaintext password, Kerberos ticket, certificate. File categorization: document, archive, binary. Export to CSV/JSON. Data comes from the REST API /api/loot endpoint.
close_reason: Added credential categorization (NTLM/Plaintext/Kerberos/Certificate), file categorization (Document/Archive/Binary), since/until time range filter, subcategory ComboBox, Export CSV/JSON buttons, and 14 new unit tests

title: DEMON_INIT working_hours parsed as u32 then silently saturates on i32::MAX
description:
Introduced by: Codex <noreply@openai.com>

teamserver/src/demon.rs:319-320 — parse_init_agent reads working_hours with
read_u32_be (big-endian u32) and then converts via i32::try_from(working_hours).
unwrap_or(i32::MAX).  The Havoc protocol sends working_hours as a 32-bit signed
value (Go int32); any value with bit 31 set (> 2147483647) fails the try_from
conversion and is silently replaced with i32::MAX instead of preserving the
correct sign-extended bit pattern.

An agent configured with a working-hours mask whose top bit is set — e.g. a
bitmask encoding the Sunday 11 PM slot — will arrive at the teamserver with its
schedule corrupted to 0x7FFFFFFF, causing the server to misrepresent and
potentially mis-enforce the kill window.

Fix: read the four bytes and reinterpret as i32 directly via
i32::from_be_bytes(read_fixed::<4>(...)) rather than going through u32.
close_reason: Parsed DEMON_INIT working_hours as signed big-endian i32 and added regression coverage for top-bit-set masks

title: test: http_listener_pipeline callback from unregistered agent not tested
description:
**File**: teamserver/tests/http_listener_pipeline.rs
**Gap type**: error path / security

Both listener pipeline integration tests only exercise the happy path (agent registers first, then calls back). No test verifies what the listener returns when an unknown agent_id sends a `COMMAND_GET_JOB` or `COMMAND_CHECKIN` callback without a prior `DEMON_INIT`. The expected behaviour is a 404 (agent unknown → no key material on file), but this is entirely untested.

A regression here could mean an unregistered agent gets served queued jobs intended for a different agent, leaking command payloads. It could also mean the server panics or returns 200 with empty body, masking the authentication failure.

**Suggested test approach**:
After starting a listener, send a `COMMAND_GET_JOB` envelope with a random agent_id that was never initialised. Assert the HTTP response is 404 and that no entry for that agent_id appears in the registry.
close_reason: Added pipeline coverage for unknown get-job and checkin callbacks returning 404 without registry mutation.

title: test: add integration smoke coverage for the red_cell crate root
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/lib.rs
**Functions with no tests**:
- No public functions are defined in this file, but the crate root re-exports the public `red_cell` API surface and currently has no `#[cfg(test)]` module or integration smoke test covering those exports.

**Why this matters**: the crate root is the API surface consumed by the teamserver binary and any downstream integration tests. Re-export regressions, missing feature wiring, or rustdoc/test harness breakage can slip through even when individual modules are tested in isolation.

**Minimum test scenarios needed**:
1. Happy path: add a crate-level integration smoke test that imports key `red_cell` re-exports and constructs minimal runtime components.
2. Error path: cover at least one failure-oriented public type import or constructor path so the crate root is exercised in a non-trivial way.
3. Edge case: ensure the smoke test compiles and runs with the plugin-enabled crate configuration used by `cargo test --workspace`.

**Test location**: `teamserver/tests/red_cell_public_api.rs`
close_reason: Added teamserver crate-root smoke coverage for router bootstrap and plugin error exports

title: test: SessionRegistry replaces old session when same connection_id authenticates twice
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/auth.rs
**Gap type**: edge case / error path

`SessionRegistry::insert` explicitly handles the case where a connection re-authenticates:
```rust
if let Some(previous_token) = self.token_by_connection.insert(session.connection_id, session.token.clone()) {
    self.by_token.remove(&previous_token);
}
```
This prevents orphaned sessions in `by_token` when a WebSocket connection re-uses the same `connection_id` (e.g., after a protocol-level reconnect without a new TCP connection). No test exercises this path, meaning a regression here would silently leak session entries — a memory-growth and potentially a stale-token-reuse issue.

**Suggested test approach**:
Authenticate the same `connection_id` twice with the same or different credentials. Assert that `session_count()` remains 1, the old token is no longer retrievable via `session_for_token`, and the new token is retrievable.
close_reason: Added session_registry_replaces_old_session_on_same_connection_id test: verifies session_count stays at 1, stale token is evicted, and new token is retrievable after re-authentication on the same connection_id

title: test: add unit tests for client::local_config public load/save wrappers
description:
**File**: client/src/local_config.rs
**Functions with no tests**:
- fn load (line 34) — loads persisted client preferences from the platform config path.
- fn save (line 51) — persists client preferences to the platform config path.

**Why this matters**: the current tests cover load_from and save_to, but not the public wrappers that resolve the real platform config location. A regression in path resolution or wrapper no-op behavior would leave the client unable to remember operator settings between sessions without any failing test.

**Minimum test scenarios needed**:
1. Happy path: save a populated config through the public API path resolver and load it back with the same values.
2. Error path: load returns LocalConfig::default() when the resolved file exists but contains invalid TOML.
3. Edge case: save is a no-op when no config directory is available, and does not panic.

**Test location**: inline #[cfg(test)] in client/src/local_config.rs
close_reason: Added three inline tests for the public load()/save() wrappers: happy-path round-trip via real platform config dir, default returned on invalid TOML, and no-panic guarantee. Tests use backup/restore to avoid host contamination and a mutex to prevent races.

title: teamserver: add indexes to audit log table for query performance
description: The ts_audit_log table has no indexes beyond the primary key. Audit queries filter on: operator_id, agent_id, action_type, timestamp range. Add indexes: (operator_id, timestamp), (agent_id, timestamp), (action_type, timestamp). Add the migration in database.rs and verify audit REST endpoint response times improve for large log sizes.
close_reason: Added migration 20260315140000_audit_log_indexes.sql with three composite indexes: (actor, occurred_at), (action, occurred_at), and expression index on (json_extract(details, '$.agent_id'), occurred_at). Added PRAGMA-based index existence tests and 500-row scale correctness tests for actor, action, and agent_id filter paths.

title: Operator login reveals valid usernames and skips dummy secret work
description:
Introduced by: Michel Klomp

teamserver/src/auth.rs:77-91 defines distinct UnknownUser and WrongPassword failures, and teamserver/src/auth.rs:218-225 returns UnknownUser before any password verification work when the username is absent. login_failure_message() at teamserver/src/auth.rs:440-444 then sends those distinct reasons back to the client.

Why it matters: the operator WebSocket login endpoint can be used to enumerate configured usernames remotely. The early-return path also bypasses the constant-time hash comparison entirely for unknown users, so response behavior still differs materially between "unknown username" and "known username / wrong password".

Fix: collapse the externally visible login error to a single generic authentication failure, and perform a dummy constant-time comparison against a fixed hash when the username is missing so the unknown-user path does comparable secret work before returning.
close_reason: Collapsed operator login failures to a generic error and added dummy hash comparison work for unknown usernames.

title: test: E2E test does not verify audit log entries are written during full session flow
description:
**File**: teamserver/tests/e2e_operator_agent_session.rs
**Gap type**: integration test gap

The single E2E test (`operator_session_listener_and_mock_demon_round_trip`) exercises the complete session flow (login → listener create → agent INIT → task → output) but never queries the audit log at the end to confirm that the expected entries were persisted.

Audit trail correctness is a core security feature. The database-level unit tests (teamserver/tests/database.rs) verify the audit storage layer in isolation, but no integration test confirms that the WebSocket handlers call `record_operator_action` / `record_operator_action_with_notifications` with the right parameters during a real session.

A regression where the audit call was accidentally removed from a handler path would not be caught by any existing test.

**Suggested test approach**:
After the output step in `operator_session_listener_and_mock_demon_round_trip`, query `database.audit_log().list()` and assert that records exist for at minimum: operator login (`operator.connect`), agent task dispatch (`agent.task`). Check actor, action, and agent_id fields.
close_reason: Added audit log assertions to operator_session_listener_and_mock_demon_round_trip: verifies operator.connect and agent.task entries with correct actor, action, target_id, and agent_id detail fields

title: test: LoginState::can_submit accepts whitespace-only password — undocumented behaviour
description:
**File**: client/src/login.rs
**Gap type**: edge case / security behaviour

`can_submit` checks `self.password.len() >= MIN_PASSWORD_LENGTH` (where `MIN_PASSWORD_LENGTH = 1`) but does **not** trim the password before the length check. This means a password of `"   "` (three spaces) passes validation and the form submits.

The existing test `can_submit_rejects_whitespace_only_fields` only verifies whitespace handling for `username` and `server_url` fields — it does not test the password field. The asymmetry is likely intentional (passwords may contain leading/trailing spaces) but it is not documented by a test, so a future refactor that adds `trim()` to the password check would silently change the security semantics without a failing test.

Additionally, `build_login_message` hashes the **raw** (untrimmed) password — a test that verifies this contract (that a password with surrounding spaces is hashed as-is, not trimmed) does not exist.

**Suggested test approach**:
Add a test asserting that `can_submit` returns `true` when the password is whitespace-only (documenting the intentional behaviour). Add a second test that calls `build_login_message` with a password containing leading spaces and asserts the hash matches `hash_password_sha3(" secret")` (raw value, not trimmed).
close_reason: Added can_submit_accepts_whitespace_only_password and build_login_message_does_not_trim_password tests to document intentional no-trim contracts

title: test: DnsListenerConfig JSON round-trip is not covered
description:
**File**: common/src/domain.rs
**Gap type**: existence

All other `ListenerConfig` variants have serialization round-trip tests:
- `listener_config_round_trips_with_tagged_protocol` (SMB)
- `external_listener_round_trips` (External)
- `response_body_round_trips_with_http_listener_config` (HTTP)

`ListenerConfig::Dns` has no round-trip test. Since DNS is a new transport added beyond the original Havoc protocol, any mismatched `serde` attribute (e.g. wrong field name, missing `default_dns_record_types` trigger, broken tagged enum discriminant) would go undetected.

**Suggested test approach**:
Construct a `DnsListenerConfig` with all fields populated, wrap it in `ListenerConfig::Dns`, serialize to JSON, deserialize back, and assert equality. Also assert that `record_types` defaults to `["TXT"]` when absent in the JSON payload.
close_reason: Added dns_listener_config_round_trips and dns_listener_config_record_types_defaults_to_txt_when_absent tests in common/src/domain.rs

title: E2E test fails after CTR fix: operator_session_listener_and_mock_demon_round_trip decrypts at wrong offset
description:
Introduced by: Cursor (Ubuntu-HTB-cursor, working on red-cell-c2-2bm)

crates/teamserver/tests/e2e_operator_agent_session.rs:119-120

After Cursor's CTR-tracking refactor, build_init_ack now calls registry.encrypt_for_agent(agent_id, &payload) which encrypts the ack at CTR offset N (where N = ctr_blocks_for_length(init_encrypted_len), set at demon.rs:149 during DEMON_INIT processing).

The E2E test still decrypts the ack at offset 0:
  let init_ack = decrypt_agent_data(&key, &iv, &init_bytes)?;

This produces garbage (actual [76,123,184,63] vs expected [120,86,52,18] = 0x12345678 in LE) and the test fails.

The integration tests in listeners.rs mod tests were correctly updated to use CTR-tracked decryption (advance_iv + ctr_blocks_for_length), but the E2E test in tests/e2e_operator_agent_session.rs was missed.

cargo test --workspace currently fails: 'operator_session_listener_and_mock_demon_round_trip' FAILED.

Fix: Update the E2E test to compute the init metadata length, derive ack_offset = ctr_blocks_for_length(metadata_len), and use advance_iv or decrypt_agent_data_ctr to decrypt at the correct offset. Similar pattern is already used in listeners.rs:2552-2556.
close_reason: Fixed as part of AES-CTR IV reuse fix (red-cell-c2-2bm)

title: COMMAND_CHECKIN can persist an all-zero AES key
description:
Introduced by: Michel Klomp

teamserver/src/dispatch.rs:829-868 copies AES key/IV material from COMMAND_CHECKIN straight into AgentRecord without validating it. teamserver/src/agents.rs:316-322 then persists the updated record, while teamserver/src/agents.rs:931-939 later rejects a stored all-zero AES key during encrypt/decrypt operations.

Why it matters: a malformed or malicious CHECKIN can poison the persisted transport state and brick the agent session after the update commits, turning one bad callback into a durable loss of communication.

Fix: validate rotated CHECKIN transport material before update_agent()/update_with_listener(), reject all-zero AES keys on the CHECKIN path just like DEMON_INIT already does, and add a regression test that invalid rotation attempts leave the prior crypto material intact.
close_reason: Fixed in commit 446ae04 — validate_checkin_transport_material rejects all-zero AES key before state mutation; regression test added.

title: plugins: queue_raw_agent_task generates mismatched request_id and task_id
description: In plugins.rs::queue_raw_agent_task (line 468/471), request_id and task_id are generated by two separate calls to next_request_id(), so they always have different counter values (e.g. request_id=5, task_id=6). task_id should be derived from the same counter value as request_id — e.g. let id = next_request_id(); then use id for request_id and format!("{:08X}", id) for task_id. As-is, any system that correlates a queued job by its task_id will fail.
close_reason: Fixed: queue_raw_agent_task now calls next_request_id() once and uses the same value for both request_id and task_id

title: parse_api_agent_id uses decimal for all-digit agent IDs, breaking hex-only IDs like 00000010
close_reason: Fixed parse_api_agent_id and parse_agent_id_filter to always parse as hex, matching websocket.rs. Added unit tests.

title: Trusted proxy parsing accepts attacker-controlled X-Forwarded-For hop
description:
Introduced by: Michel Klomp

teamserver/src/listeners.rs:2474-2484 — `extract_external_ip` trusts the first syntactically valid `X-Forwarded-For` entry from a trusted proxy peer. In common reverse-proxy deployments the proxy appends the real client IP to any preexisting header, so an attacker can supply a bogus leftmost hop and have Red Cell attribute the session/check-in to an arbitrary IP. This weakens DEMON_INIT per-IP throttling and poisons operator-visible source-IP metadata. Fix by only honoring a proxy-sanitized single-hop header, or by selecting the correct post-proxy hop from the right side after validating the proxy chain contract.
close_reason: Hardened trusted proxy header parsing to select the rightmost client hop behind trusted proxies and added spoofing regression tests.

title: test: add unit coverage for DemonPackage::encoded_len
description:
**File**: common/src/demon.rs
**Functions with no tests**:
- fn encoded_len (line 517) — reports the byte length that DemonPackage::to_bytes should emit for a command/request/payload tuple.

**Why this matters**: encoded_len is used to size buffers and aggregate DemonMessage serialization capacity. If it drifts from the wire format, the protocol layer can silently mis-size allocations or mask serialization bugs until a larger payload path breaks in production.

**Minimum test scenarios needed**:
1. Happy path: empty payload reports the 12-byte header-only package length.
2. Error path: non-empty payload length matches the encoded buffer length returned by to_bytes.
3. Edge case: a larger payload still reports 12 + payload.len() exactly and matches DemonMessage::to_bytes aggregation.

**Test location**: inline #[cfg(test)] in common/src/demon.rs
close_reason: Added inline unit tests covering header-only, payload-backed, and aggregated encoded_len sizing.

title: Fix clippy too-many-arguments violation in sockets.rs register_client
description: cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings fails with: error: this function has too many arguments (9/8) at crates/teamserver/src/sockets.rs:329 in the register_client method. This breaks CI clippy gates. Fix: refactor arguments into a struct (e.g. ClientRegistration) or use #[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)] with justification.
close_reason: register_client was refactored to accept a PendingClient struct (3 params), clippy now passes clean

title: test: add unit coverage for SessionActivityQuery pagination helpers
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/audit.rs
**Functions with no tests**:
- `fn limit` (line 200) — clamps session-activity page size to the supported range.
- `fn offset` (line 206) — returns the validated session-activity pagination offset.

**Why this matters**: `GET /session-activity` relies on these accessors before querying SQLite. If limit clamping or offset defaults regress, the route can over-fetch, under-fetch, or ignore caller pagination without any direct test failure today.

**Minimum test scenarios needed**:
1. Happy path: default query returns limit `50` and offset `0`.
2. Error/boundary path: oversized limit is clamped to `200`.
3. Edge case: explicit small limit and non-zero offset are preserved unchanged.

**Test location**: inline `#[cfg(test)]` in `teamserver/src/audit.rs`
close_reason: Added three inline unit tests for SessionActivityQuery::limit and ::offset covering default, clamped oversized, and explicit small/non-zero cases

title: test: format_filetime epoch boundary and invalid timestamp edge cases
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/dispatch/kerberos.rs
**Gap type**: edge case

The `format_filetime(high, low)` function has two untested paths:
1. Values at or below the epoch boundary (0x019DB1DED53E8000) must return the sentinel string "1970-01-01 00:00:00 +00:00:00" — a Kerberos ticket with an all-zero filetime (a very common placeholder) will hit this branch. No test verifies the exact sentinel string.
2. Values that produce a unix timestamp beyond what `OffsetDateTime::from_unix_timestamp` accepts will fall through to returning the raw second count as a string — this error branch is completely untested.

If the sentinel branch regresses (e.g., the comparison flips to strict <), every zero-filetime ticket will display a garbage timestamp rather than the expected placeholder, misleading operators.

**Suggested test approach**:
Call `format_filetime(0, 0)` (all zeros, well below boundary) and assert it equals the sentinel string; call `format_filetime(0x019D_B1DE, 0xD53E_8000)` (exactly at the boundary) and assert same sentinel; call `format_filetime(0x019D_B1DE, 0xD53E_8001)` (one unit above) and assert the result contains "-" (a valid date string).

title: AES-256-CTR IV reused for every agent packet — keystream reuse
description:
Introduced by: Codex

crates/teamserver/src/demon.rs:154 and crates/teamserver/src/dispatch.rs:462–470

The same key+IV pair stored at DEMON_INIT is reused as-is for every
subsequent encrypt/decrypt call for a given agent.  AES-CTR mode is a
stream cipher: it XORs each plaintext byte with a keystream derived from
(key, counter-starting-at-IV).  Reusing the same IV means the exact same
keystream is generated for every message.

Result: for any two ciphertexts C1 and C2 from the same agent session,
C1 XOR C2 = P1 XOR P2.  An observer who knows one plaintext (e.g. the
deterministic ACK body) can immediately recover the other.

Worst case is with Secure=false listeners where TLS is absent: the
keystream-reuse attack has no outer layer to stop it.

Fix: advance a per-session counter stored alongside the key/IV in
AgentEncryptionInfo, or generate a fresh nonce per message and prepend it
to the ciphertext.  Verify the chosen approach matches the Demon C agent's
counter-advance behavior before shipping.
close_reason: Per-agent AES-CTR block counter tracking implemented. ctr_block_offset in AgentEntry, encrypt_for_agent/decrypt_from_agent on AgentRegistry, updated init/callback/job flows in demon.rs+dispatch.rs+listeners.rs. All 326 tests passing, clippy clean, formatted.

title: resource: per-agent job queue has no size cap
description:
Introduced by: Michel Klomp

teamserver/src/agents.rs enqueue_job (~line 526) appends to a VecDeque<Job> without a per-agent cap. MAX_REQUEST_CONTEXTS = 10_000 protects the request-context map but not the job queue. An authenticated operator can enqueue an unbounded number of tasks for a single agent, causing monotonically growing heap allocation and eventual OOM on the teamserver.

Fix: impose a per-agent job queue depth limit (e.g. 1000 entries) and return TeamserverError::QueueFull or similar to callers, ensuring old jobs are not silently dropped.
close_reason: Added MAX_JOB_QUEUE_DEPTH=1000 constant, TeamserverError::QueueFull variant, and depth checks in enqueue_job for both direct and pivot paths. Two new tests cover at-capacity rejection and dequeue-then-accept.

title: DNS listener implementation is compiled out of release builds
description:
Introduced by: Codex

teamserver/src/listeners.rs:1479-1484 rejects DNS listeners at the manager boundary, and the actual DNS runtime code is behind #[cfg(test)] starting at teamserver/src/listeners.rs:1509 and teamserver/src/listeners.rs:2153. The tests exercise a DNS listener implementation that does not exist in production binaries.

This is deeper than payload-builder support being incomplete: even if creation gating is removed later, release builds still will not contain the runtime path. The codebase currently advertises DNS listener support in shared config/domain types and architecture docs, but production compiles it out entirely.

Fix: either ship the DNS runtime in normal builds and gate only the missing payload-generation workflow, or remove the production-facing DNS listener surface until the runtime is actually supported. Add a non-test build/start path check so release code cannot silently drift away from test-only listener implementations.
close_reason: Compiled the DNS listener runtime into normal builds, restored the manager start path, and added a manager-level start test to catch future drift.

title: test: dispatch checkin handler for unknown agent (TeamserverError::AgentNotFound) not tested
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/dispatch.rs
**Gap type**: error path

`handle_checkin` calls `registry.get(agent_id)` and returns `TeamserverError::AgentNotFound` immediately if the agent is not in the registry. Both existing checkin tests (`builtin_checkin_handler_updates_last_call_in_and_broadcasts` and `builtin_checkin_handler_refreshes_metadata_and_transport_state`) pre-register the agent before dispatching. The error path — when an unknown agent ID sends a COMMAND_CHECKIN — is never covered.

This is the dispatch-layer counterpart of the open bug red-cell-c2-s7rj (reconnect probe returns silent 200 with no warning or audit entry). Even after the HTTP-layer fix, the dispatch layer's error handling for this case remains unverified: does `dispatch` propagate the error, swallow it, or return Ok(None)?

**Suggested test approach**:
Dispatch a CommandCheckin payload for an agent_id that was never registered, then assert the result is `Err(CommandDispatchError::...)` and that no AgentUpdate event is broadcast on the event bus.
close_reason: Added dispatch test covering unknown-agent COMMAND_CHECKIN, asserting AgentNotFound propagation and no AgentUpdate broadcast.

title: HTTP listener proxy_password exposed to all operator WebSocket clients
description:
Introduced by: Codex

teamserver/src/listeners.rs:233-234 — ListenerSummary::to_operator_info() populates info.proxy_password from the listener config and sends it to every connecting operator (including Analyst-role) via send_session_snapshot() in websocket.rs. Proxy credentials should be redacted from the operator wire format.
close_reason: Redacted HTTP listener proxy_password from operator websocket payloads and added regression coverage.

title: python: rich command context — description, options, help text, history
description: RegisterCommand() in client/src/python.rs only captures command name and callback. Add: description field for help text display, options list (name, type, required, default), command history per-agent accessible from callback context. Expose via havocui.RegisterCommand(name, description, options, callback) with backward-compatible fallback for the 2-arg form.
close_reason: Added CommandOption type, options list on RegisteredCommand, per-agent command history (capped at 100), PyCommandOption pyclass, context.options/history getters, havocui.RegisterCommand with 2-arg and 4-arg forms

title: WebSocket listener edits are not written to the audit log
description:
Introduced by: Michel Klomp

crates/teamserver/src/websocket.rs:778-796 handles ListenerEdit by updating the listener and broadcasting an event or error, but it never calls log_operator_action(). The equivalent REST path records both success and failure audit entries in crates/teamserver/src/api.rs:1785-1856, so listener mutations performed through the operator WebSocket disappear from the structured audit trail. The WebSocket edit path should emit the same listener.update success/failure audit records as the REST API.
close_reason: Added listener.update audit logging to the websocket edit path and covered success/failure cases with regression tests.

title: security: API key digest lookup is not constant-time
description:
Introduced by: Michel Klomp

teamserver/src/api.rs ~line 278 — ApiKeyDigest([u8; 32]) comparison in BTreeMap::get uses the derived Ord/PartialEq impls on [u8; 32], which short-circuit on the first differing byte. While HMAC pre-processing provides pre-image resistance the byte-level comparison is a timing oracle on the HMAC digest. A high-precision remote timing attacker could distinguish different digest prefixes.

Fix: replace BTreeMap lookup with a HashMap + constant-time comparison using subtle::ConstantTimeEq, or compare with a custom wrapper that calls constant_time_eq from the subtle crate.
close_reason: Replaced BTreeMap<ApiKeyDigest,ApiIdentity> with Vec + lookup_key_ct using subtle::ConstantTimeEq; scan always visits all entries so lookup time is independent of digest prefix. Four unit tests added.

title: test: add audit/auth helper coverage for operator metadata payloads
description:
**Files**:
- teamserver/src/audit.rs
- teamserver/src/auth.rs

**Functions with no tests**:
- fn login_parameters (teamserver/src/audit.rs:519) — builds the structured audit payload for login attempts without persisting sensitive fields.
- fn as_operator_info (teamserver/src/auth.rs:133) — converts an operator presence record into the shared wire/domain representation sent to clients.

**Why this matters**: both helpers shape operator-facing and audit-facing JSON data. A regression here can leak sensitive fields, misreport operator role/presence state, or break client compatibility on inventory and audit endpoints.

**Minimum test scenarios needed**:
1. Happy path: login_parameters includes username and connection_id only, and as_operator_info preserves username, role, online, and last_seen values.
2. Error path: usernames with mixed case or unusual characters serialize without dropping fields or adding password material.
3. Edge case: as_operator_info handles offline operators with no session state while still emitting the expected optional fields.

**Test location**: inline #[cfg(test)] in teamserver/src/audit.rs and teamserver/src/auth.rs
close_reason: Added inline tests for login audit parameters and operator presence operator-info serialization.

title: security: build_tls_config in main.rs ignores profile certificate/key configuration
description:
Introduced by: Codex <noreply@openai.com>

teamserver/src/main.rs — The build_tls_config function always generates a self-signed ECDSA P-256 certificate for the operator TLS endpoint, regardless of what cert/key paths are configured in the server profile. common/src/tls.rs::resolve_tls_identity correctly implements the fallback logic (use configured cert if present, self-sign otherwise), but main.rs never calls it.

Operators who configure CA-signed certificates for the teamserver TLS channel — or who pin the certificate in their operator client — will silently receive a different (self-signed) certificate than configured. This breaks certificate pinning and defeats CA-chain validation.

Fix: in main.rs, call resolve_tls_identity() from common/src/tls.rs instead of the local build_tls_config, passing the profile's cert/key fields.
close_reason: build_tls_config already calls resolve_or_persist_tls_identity with profile.teamserver.cert (fixed in 456175e); added three tests verifying it uses configured cert paths, generates and persists on first boot, and reloads stable material on subsequent boots

title: test: LootRepository::get and list have no dedicated tests
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/database.rs
**Functions with no tests**:
- `fn get` (line 955) on `LootRepository` — fetches a single `LootRecord` by row ID; has no test verifying ID-not-found returns `Ok(None)`
- `fn list` (line 976) on `LootRepository` — global list of all loot rows across all agents; no standalone test verifies pagination independence from `list_for_agent`

**Current gap**: The existing `loot_repository_supports_insert_query_and_delete` test creates one loot record and verifies `list_for_agent`. The `get` method and global `list` method are not asserted in isolation — bugs in those SQL queries (wrong column order, missing WHERE clause) would only be caught incidentally.

**Why this matters**: `get` is used by the REST API to return a single loot item; returning wrong data or wrong `None` / `Some` status would cause silent operator data loss. `list` is used by the loot browser endpoint; an accidentally unfiltered query could leak cross-agent loot.

**Minimum test scenarios needed**:
1. `get` happy path: insert a record, call `get(id)`, assert the returned record equals what was inserted
2. `get` missing: call `get(9999)` without any prior insert, assert `Ok(None)`
3. `list` isolation: insert loot for two different agents, call `list()`, assert both records present; call `list_for_agent(agent1_id)`, assert only that agent's record returned

**Test location**: `teamserver/tests/database.rs` alongside `loot_repository_supports_insert_query_and_delete`

title: ApiRuntime rate-limit windows map grows unboundedly for inactive keys
description:
Introduced by: Cursor Agent <noreply@cursor.com>

crates/teamserver/src/api.rs:100-101 — `ApiRuntime::windows` is a `BTreeMap<String, RateLimitWindow>` that only shrinks when:
- `check_rate_limit` resets a window that has expired AND the key makes another request
- There is no background timer, no prune-on-read loop, and no eviction policy

An API key that triggers rate-limiting once and then disappears will occupy a map entry forever. Under a realistic red-team engagement with rotating keys or brief tool runs, this map accumulates one entry per unique key that was ever rate-limited. The attack surface is bounded by the number of configured API keys (not externally controllable), so OOM is unlikely in practice, but it is the same architectural flaw as the LoginRateLimiter bug (red-cell-c2-2b9) and should be fixed consistently.

Fix: add a periodic prune pass (drop expired windows) or cap total map size, consistent with how the LoginRateLimiter fix was or will be approached.
close_reason: Pruned expired REST API rate-limit windows on each check and added regression coverage.

title: External listener support is advertised but rejected at runtime
description:
Introduced by: Michel Klomp

common/src/domain.rs:19-23 and 211-219 expose External as a first-class ListenerProtocol/ListenerConfig variant, and teamserver/src/listeners.rs:1031 plus the operator mapping path accept External listener payloads. But teamserver/src/listeners.rs:1743-1777 still rejects every External listener as unsupported during create/start/restore.

Why it matters: the shared types and operator/API surface promise an External transport that the runtime cannot actually create, update, start, or restore. This is architectural drift and a misleading public contract.

Fix: either implement External listener runtime support or remove/reject the External variant at the shared API/config layer until the transport exists.
close_reason: Removed External variant from shared domain types (ListenerProtocol, ListenerConfig, ExternalListenerConfig). The HCL profile config layer retains parsing+rejection for clear user errors. All runtime handling dead code removed.

title: test: mark_stale_agent_if_unchanged skips agent that was already marked dead before mark phase
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/agent_liveness.rs
**Gap type**: error path / edge case

`mark_stale_agent_if_unchanged` has a two-condition toctou guard:
```rust
if !current.active || current.last_call_in != stale_agent.last_call_in {
    return Ok(false);
}
```

The test `sweep_toctou_guard_skips_agents_that_check_in_during_mark_phase` exercises the `last_call_in` branch (agent checked in between collect and mark), but the `!current.active` branch — where another mechanism (e.g., operator manual kill or a concurrent sweep) marks the agent dead between collect and mark — has no test.

Without this test, a future refactor could drop the `!current.active` guard and silently double-mark dead agents, creating spurious `AgentUpdate` events broadcast to operators.

**Suggested test approach**:
Insert an agent, run `collect_stale_agents` to get it in the stale list, then call `registry.mark_dead` directly to kill the agent, then call `mark_stale_agent_if_unchanged` and assert it returns `Ok(false)` without emitting another event.
close_reason: Added test sweep_toctou_guard_skips_agent_already_marked_dead_before_mark_phase covering the !current.active branch of mark_stale_agent_if_unchanged

title: test: EventReceiver::is_closed() false-path and zero-capacity EventBus history not tested
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/events.rs
**Gap type**: edge case + existence

Two untested behaviours in the `EventBus` / `EventReceiver` pair:

1. **`is_closed()` positive path**: All three existing tests only assert `is_closed()` returns `true` (after lag or drop). There is no test asserting that a freshly-created subscription returns `is_closed() == false`. A regression that initialises `receiver` as `None` by mistake would not be caught.

2. **Zero-capacity history ring-buffer**: `EventBus::new(0)` creates a ring buffer with `capacity=0`. The history-eviction branch checks `if history.len() == self.history_capacity` — with capacity 0 this triggers on the first push, making the stored count permanently 0. Whether this is intended is undocumented and untested.

**Suggested test approach**:
(1) Assert `bus.subscribe().is_closed() == false` immediately after subscribing. (2) Create an `EventBus::new(0)`, broadcast a TeamserverLog event, and assert `recent_teamserver_logs()` returns an empty vec — if this is the intended no-history mode, document it with a test.
close_reason: Added EventReceiver false-path coverage and locked in zero-history EventBus behavior without panicking.

title: audit: query_audit_log loads entire table into memory before filtering
description:
crates/teamserver/src/audit.rs:187-208 — query_audit_log calls database.audit_log().list().await? which fetches all rows from the database, converts every row to AuditRecord, reverses the entire Vec, then applies in-memory filtering and slice-based pagination.

For a busy teamserver with thousands of agent tasks logged per day, this will:
- Consume unbounded memory proportional to total audit log size
- Increase latency linearly with log size even for small page requests
- Convert and deserialize all rows even when only 1 page of 50 is needed

Fix: push filtering and pagination into SQL. The audit_log repository should expose a filtered query method that accepts WHERE clauses for actor, action, target_kind, target_id, and a JSON-extracted agent_id/command/result_status, plus LIMIT and OFFSET. COUNT(*) with the same WHERE can provide the total without fetching all rows.
close_reason: Pushed filtering and pagination into SQL via AuditLogRepository.query_filtered/count_filtered; removed in-memory filtering from query_audit_log

title: python: loot database access from Python scripts
description: Python scripts loaded via the client PyO3 runtime (client/src/python.rs) have no access to the loot database. Add a Python loot API: red_cell.get_loot(agent_id=None, loot_type=None) returning a list of loot objects with fields: id, agent_id, type, data, timestamp. Loot is fetched via the teamserver REST API. Useful for scripts that auto-process credentials or downloaded files.
close_reason: Added red_cell.get_loot(agent_id=None, loot_type=None) returning PyLootItem objects with id, agent_id, type, data, timestamp, name getters. Added LootItem.id field populated from LootID websocket event data. Includes two integration tests verifying unfiltered and filtered loot retrieval.

title: External/service bridge listeners are stubs that report Running without implementing transport
description:
Introduced by: Codex

crates/common/src/config.rs:28-30,687-695 define a Service bridge configuration, but there is no teamserver runtime path that consumes profile.service anywhere under crates/teamserver/src.

crates/teamserver/src/listeners.rs:1398-1400 starts ListenerConfig::External by spawning pending::<()>(), so the listener transitions to Running even though it never binds, accepts traffic, or bridges to any external/service agent.

crates/teamserver/src/payload_builder.rs:593-596 also rejects External listeners for Demon payload builds.

This leaves the Havoc-compatible service/external path half-exposed in config and APIs but non-functional at runtime. The fix should either implement the service bridge end to end or reject/disable the feature consistently instead of pretending it started successfully.
close_reason: Rejected unsupported external/service listeners at start and restore instead of reporting a fake running state.

title: extract_external_ip trusts agent-supplied X-Real-IP header when behind_redirector=false
description:
Introduced by: Codex <noreply@openai.com> (original HTTP listener implementation)

crates/teamserver/src/listeners.rs:2044-2062 — `extract_external_ip()`

When `config.behind_redirector` is false, the function falls back to the `x-real-ip` header value. `X-Real-IP` is conventionally injected by a reverse proxy (nginx, etc.) but in this code path, the source is the agent itself (a direct TCP connection). Any Demon agent — or an attacker spoofing one — can set `X-Real-IP: 1.2.3.4` in its HTTP request and that value becomes the recorded `external_ip` in the agent registry and audit log.

This is related to but distinct from red-cell-c2-xpe ("always 0.0.0.0"): when an agent does set this header, the stored IP is an attacker-controlled string, not the true TCP peer address. This allows phantom agents to record arbitrary IPs, polluting the operator's situational awareness.

The `X-Real-IP` fallback should be removed from the `behind_redirector=false` path. The fix for red-cell-c2-xpe (using Axum's ConnectInfo to get the actual TCP peer address) would supersede this fallback entirely.
close_reason: Fixed by 88ca055 — extract_external_ip now always uses peer.ip() when behind_redirector=false; X-Real-IP and X-Forwarded-For headers are only read when behind_redirector=true

title: test: add unit tests for ListenerRepository update and exists methods
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/database.rs (ListenerRepository, lines 670-740)
**Functions with no tests**:
- `fn update` (line 670) — updates listener config in place; no test verifies that an update to a non-existent listener returns an error or is silently ignored
- `fn exists` (line 722) — returns bool; entirely untested — the function is used by listeners.rs to detect name collisions before creation

**Why this matters**: A silent failure in `update` means a listener config change is accepted by the REST API but not persisted to SQLite. Callers assume the update was durable. If `exists` returns incorrect values it allows duplicate listener names to be registered, causing listener startup failures that are hard to diagnose.

**Minimum test scenarios needed**:
1. Happy path: create a listener, call update with a modified config, re-fetch and verify the new config is persisted.
2. Error path: call update for a listener name that was never created; verify it is a silent no-op (or returns an error, matching the current behavior).
3. Happy path: exists returns false for unknown name, true after creation, and false again after deletion.

**Test location**: `teamserver/tests/database.rs` alongside existing listener_repository tests
close_reason: Tests already implemented: listener_repository_update_persists_config_changes, listener_repository_update_is_a_noop_for_missing_listener, and listener_repository_exists_tracks_creation_and_deletion — all pass

title: test: test helper duplication across 4 integration test files creates divergence risk
description:
**File**: teamserver/tests/http_listener_pipeline.rs, teamserver/tests/mock_demon_agent_checkin.rs, teamserver/tests/e2e_operator_agent_session.rs, teamserver/tests/havoc_compatibility.rs
**Gap type**: quality / maintainability

The following helpers are copy-pasted verbatim (or near-verbatim) across all four integration test files:
- `valid_demon_init_body`
- `valid_demon_callback_body`
- `add_length_prefixed_bytes_be`
- `add_length_prefixed_utf16_be`

A protocol format change (e.g., a field reorder in the Demon metadata blob, or a length prefix endianness fix) corrected in one file would silently leave the other three files using a stale fixture. This is the most dangerous form of duplication in protocol-testing code: the tests still compile and run, they just test the wrong thing.

**Suggested test approach**:
Extract these helpers into a shared `teamserver/tests/common/demon_fixtures.rs` module (or a `tests/support/` crate). All four test files import from one canonical location. A single fix propagates everywhere.
close_reason: Extracted valid_demon_init_body, valid_demon_callback_body, add_length_prefixed_bytes_be, add_length_prefixed_utf16_be, available_port, and wait_for_listener into tests/common/mod.rs; removed local duplicates from http_listener_pipeline.rs and havoc_compatibility.rs

title: test: load_tls_identity_from_files cert-present-key-missing reports key path in error
description:
**File**: common/src/tls.rs
**Gap type**: error path

The existing `load_tls_identity_from_files_rejects_missing_paths` test checks the case where *both* files are missing — it validates that the cert path appears in the `TlsError::ReadFile` message. However there is no test for the case where the cert file exists but the *key* file is missing, which should produce a `ReadFile { path: <key_path>, .. }` error.

Without this test, a regression that swaps the cert and key error context (or always reports the cert path) would not be caught.

**Suggested test approach**:
Write the cert PEM to a temp file but do NOT write the key file. Call `load_tls_identity_from_files` and assert `TlsError::ReadFile { path, .. }` where `path` equals the key file path.
close_reason: test already present in common/src/tls.rs: load_tls_identity_from_files_rejects_missing_key_when_cert_exists writes cert to temp file, omits key file, and asserts ReadFile error path equals key_path

title: handle_pivot_callback has 9 args — fails clippy too_many_arguments
description:
crates/teamserver/src/dispatch.rs:438 — handle_pivot_callback now takes 9 parameters after the PluginRuntime argument was added (registry, events, database, sockets, downloads, plugins, agent_id, request_id, payload). Clippy's too_many_arguments lint fires (limit is 8) and -D warnings promotes it to an error, breaking 'cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings'.

Fix: introduce a DispatchContext struct (or similar) to bundle the shared state refs (registry, events, database, sockets, downloads, plugins) and pass it as a single argument, reducing the parameter count below the limit.
close_reason: Resolved while implementing the embedded PyO3 runtime: plugin tests were stabilized and pivot dispatch now uses a shared context, so cargo test and cargo clippy both pass again.

title: teamserver: audit operator disconnect and session timeout events
description: teamserver/src/audit.rs records operator login but not disconnect or timeout. Add audit records for: operator WebSocket disconnect (clean close vs error), session expiry (JWT timeout), permission denial (403 on any API endpoint). These events are needed for SOC-style audit trails on red team infrastructure.
close_reason: Added operator.disconnect kind field (clean_close/error/server_shutdown), operator.session_timeout for unauthenticated WS timeout, operator.permission_denied for RBAC-rejected WS commands, and api.permission_denied for REST 403s. query_session_activity updated to include new event types. Tests added for all paths.

title: sync_profile leaves listeners that were removed from the profile persisted and restartable
description:
Introduced by: Codex

teamserver/src/listeners.rs:572-584 only creates or updates listeners that still exist in the YAOTL profile. It never deletes persisted listeners that were removed from the profile, and teamserver/src/listeners.rs:588-596 will still restore any persisted listener whose last state is Running.

That means the profile is not authoritative on startup: removing a listener from the profile does not remove or disable it, and a previously persisted running listener can come back after restart even though the operator deleted it from configuration.

Fix: reconcile the repository against the current profile during sync by stopping and deleting listeners that are no longer declared, or explicitly mark them orphaned and prevent restore_running() from starting them. Add a startup test that removes a profile listener and verifies it does not survive the next boot.
close_reason: sync_profile now deletes listeners removed from the profile and startup tests verify they do not survive the next boot

title: build_job silently maps invalid task_id to 0 on parse failure
description: In crates/teamserver/src/websocket.rs line 600, `u32::from_str_radix(info.task_id.trim(), 16).unwrap_or_default()` silently maps any malformed or non-hex task_id string to request_id=0. If a client sends a job with an invalid task_id, the resulting callback is stored under task 0, making it unroutable and invisible to the operator. This should return an `AgentCommandError::InvalidNumericField` (the same error path used for other numeric fields like `parse_hex_u32` at line 1152) rather than silently defaulting.
close_reason: Duplicate of red-cell-c2-1xg — already fixed in commit ec7ad40. build_job now returns AgentCommandError::InvalidTaskId instead of unwrap_or_default(), with tests for empty, non-hex, and overflowing task_id values.

title: restore_running hides failed persisted listener restarts
description:
Introduced by: Codex

teamserver/src/listeners.rs:616-623 — `restore_running` now treats every `StartFailed` as non-fatal and keeps booting. That means a previously-running HTTP or DNS listener can fail to bind or initialize during restart, be silently downgraded to `Error`, and the teamserver still comes up without surfacing the availability failure to startup. New-profile listeners still abort on real start failures, so restart semantics are now inconsistent and operators can miss broken C2 surfaces after a reboot. Fix by only suppressing the explicitly unsupported external-listener case (or another narrowly-defined expected failure) and propagating genuine runtime start failures for supported transports.
close_reason: Restore now only suppresses unsupported external listeners; supported restart failures abort startup again.

title: test: SocketRelayManager add_socks_server invalid-port and duplicate-server error paths not tested
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/sockets.rs
**Gap type**: error path

`add_socks_server` has two reachable error branches that have no test:
1. `parse_port` failure (lines 155, 605-607): a non-numeric or out-of-range port string (e.g. '"abc"', '"99999"') should return `SocketRelayError::InvalidPort`. Currently only the success path is exercised in `socks_server_lifecycle_commands_track_state`.
2. Duplicate server registration (lines 169-170): adding a second SOCKS server on the same port for the same agent should return `SocketRelayError::DuplicateServer`. No test currently does this.

Without coverage, a regression in either branch would silently slip through — the manager could accept an invalid port string and panic/bind to port 0 unexpectedly, or allow duplicate entries causing state corruption.

**Suggested test approach**:
(a) Call `add_socks_server(agent_id, "not-a-port")` and assert `Err(SocketRelayError::InvalidPort { .. })`.
(b) Add two servers on port 0 for the same agent; the second call should return `Err(SocketRelayError::DuplicateServer { .. })`.
close_reason: Added unit tests covering invalid port parsing and duplicate SOCKS server registration errors in SocketRelayManager.

title: DNS listener silently drops non-C2-domain queries instead of responding with REFUSED
description:
In listeners.rs, DnsListenerState::handle_dns_packet uses the ? operator on parse_dns_c2_query, which returns None when labels don't match the configured C2 domain. This causes the whole function to return None, so no UDP response is sent to the client. The failing test (dns_listener_starts_and_responds_to_unknown_queries_with_refused) demonstrates this: it sends a query for 'something.other.domain.com' to a listener configured for 'c2.example.com' and expects a REFUSED (RCODE=5) response, but times out instead.

Fix: replace 'let c2_query = parse_dns_c2_query(&query.labels, &self.config.domain)?;' with explicit None handling that falls through to 'Some(build_dns_refused_response(query.id))' when the domain doesn't match.

This is a P1 bug because it causes a test failure and means non-C2 DNS traffic going to the listener is silently dropped instead of being refused, which can confuse legitimate DNS resolvers expecting a response.
close_reason: Returned REFUSED for non-matching DNS queries instead of dropping the packet silently

title: test: SMB listener should reject callbacks from unregistered agents
description:
**File**: `teamserver/tests/smb_listener.rs`
**Gap**: The HTTP listener has `http_listener_pipeline_rejects_callbacks_from_unregistered_agent` (line 123 of http_listener_pipeline.rs) but the SMB listener has no corresponding test.

**Why this matters**: Without this test, a regression that allows the SMB transport to accept callback packets from unknown agent IDs could go undetected. An attacker who can reach the named pipe could inject forged output or exfiltrate data by impersonating an agent ID.

**Functions under test**: `ListenerManager` SMB path → agent lookup → rejection when agent not in registry.

**Minimum test scenarios needed**:
1. Start SMB listener, complete DEMON_INIT for agent A, then send a callback packet with a different (unregistered) agent ID — expect the teamserver to close/ignore the connection without emitting an agent event on the event bus.
2. Verify that the original registered agent's state is untouched after the rejected callback.

**Test location**: `teamserver/tests/smb_listener.rs`

title: test: parse_working_hours end-before-start error path is untested
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/payload_builder.rs
**Gap type**: error path

`parse_working_hours` (line 1154) rejects working-hours ranges where the end time is not strictly after the start (`end <= start`). This error branch is never exercised by any test. Without coverage, a future refactor of the bitmask packing logic could silently break the boundary check and allow a payload to be compiled with nonsensical working hours.

**Suggested test approach**:
Call `parse_working_hours(Some("17:00-08:00"))` (end before start) and assert `InvalidRequest` is returned. Also call `parse_working_hours(Some("10:30-10:30"))` (equal times) and assert the same.
close_reason: Added parse_working_hours_rejects_end_before_start and parse_working_hours_rejects_equal_start_and_end tests covering the end<=start error branch

title: Fresh profile startup ignores non-external listener start failures
description:
Introduced by: Codex <noreply@openai.com>

teamserver/src/main.rs:232-234 in commit 65b67c4 now treats every ListenerManagerError::StartFailed from start_new_profile_listeners() as success:

    Ok(_)
    | Err(ListenerManagerError::ListenerAlreadyRunning { .. })
    | Err(ListenerManagerError::StartFailed { .. }) => {}

This was meant to degrade unsupported External listeners during boot, but StartFailed is also the error used for real listener startup failures in teamserver/src/listeners.rs:620-631 (for example HTTP/HTTPS bind errors, SMB bind failures, DNS socket bind failures, and other runtime initialization errors).
