Result: a fresh profile can now boot successfully even when a required listener failed to bind or initialize. The listener is left persisted in Error state, but main() no longer aborts, so operators get a partially started teamserver instead of an explicit startup failure. The new regression test only covers the unsupported External case and does not protect against ordinary bind failures.

Correct behavior: only suppress the specific unsupported-external degradation path (or otherwise discriminate by listener type / error cause). Ordinary listener start failures during startup should still fail boot so the configuration problem is visible immediately.
close_reason: Fresh-profile startup now only degrades unsupported external listeners; ordinary listener start failures abort boot, with regression coverage for HTTP bind failures.

title: test: LocalConfig::load silent fallback on corrupt TOML not tested
description:
**File**: client/src/local_config.rs
**Gap type**: error path / silent failure

`LocalConfig::load` calls `toml::from_str(&contents).unwrap_or_default()` — if the on-disk config is corrupt or written by an incompatible version, the parse error is silently swallowed and `LocalConfig::default()` is returned. The user loses all saved preferences without any indication.

No test exercises this path. The existing `save_and_load_round_trip_via_tempdir` test bypasses both `load()` and `save()` entirely — it manually calls `toml::to_string_pretty` and `toml::from_str` without touching the real file-system helpers.

The same issue exists for `save()`: if `dirs::config_dir()` returns `None` (which can happen on sandboxed environments or unusual OS configurations), the call silently does nothing. This path has no test coverage.

**Suggested test approach**:
Write a test that creates a tempfile, writes invalid TOML to it, calls `LocalConfig::load` (or the underlying `toml::from_str` unwrap-or-default path) with that path, and asserts the result equals `LocalConfig::default()`. Write a second test that verifies `save()` and `load()` actually round-trip when called directly (not via the manual serialize/deserialize helpers).
close_reason: Added LocalConfig tests for invalid TOML fallback, direct save/load round-trip, and missing config-dir no-op paths.

title: request_contexts HashMap in AgentRegistry is never pruned — unbounded memory growth
description:
Introduced by: Codex <noreply@openai.com>

crates/teamserver/src/agents.rs:79,286,345 — Every call to `enqueue_job` inserts a `JobContext` entry into the `request_contexts` HashMap keyed by `(agent_id, request_id)`. The entry is never removed after being read by `request_context` (called from `dispatch.rs:794`). Over a long engagement with many tasked agents, this map grows without bound, slowly consuming memory.

Fix: remove the entry from `request_contexts` inside `request_context` after it is read (change from `.get` to `.remove`), or add a bounded eviction policy (e.g., retain only the last N contexts per agent).
close_reason: Bounded request_contexts eviction (MAX 10k entries, oldest-half pruned), plus lifecycle cleanup on mark_dead/remove. All 238 tests pass.

title: client: note task sent with empty operator username
description:
Introduced by: Codex <noreply@openai.com>

In `render_note_editor` (crates/client/src/main.rs:862), `build_note_task` is called with `""` as the operator name:

    self.session_panel.pending_messages.push(build_note_task(&agent_id, &note, ""));

The AppState is already locked on lines 858-861 to call `update_agent_note`. The operator username is available as `state.operator_info.as_ref().map(|op| op.username.as_str())`. The empty string is silently forwarded in the `MessageHead.user` field, so the teamserver receives a note-update task attributed to an anonymous/blank user — breaking audit attribution for note operations.

Fix: read `operator_info.username` from the same lock used for `update_agent_note` and pass it to `build_note_task`.
close_reason: render_note_editor now forwards operator_info.username into build_note_task for note audit attribution

title: test: encode_fs_payload missing coverage for Dir, Download, Cd, Remove, Mkdir, Move, Cat, GetPwd subcommands
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/websocket.rs
**Gap type**: existence

`encode_fs_payload` handles 9 filesystem subcommands. Only two are covered by tests:
- `Copy` (covered by `build_jobs_encodes_filesystem_copy_payload`)
- `Upload` (covered by `build_jobs_splits_upload_into_memfile_chunks_and_final_fs_job`)

Seven subcommands are untested:
- `Dir` — complex 8-part semicolon-delimited argument string; each bool field has a specific encode path
- `Download` / `Cat` — base64-decode then UTF-16-encode the path argument
- `Cd` / `Remove` / `Mkdir` — single required path string, UTF-16 encoded
- `Move` — same structure as `Copy` (2-part base64 args) but different subcommand discriminant
- `GetPwd` — produces an empty payload body; no test confirms the subcommand discriminant is written

Any encoding bug in these paths (wrong byte order, missing length prefix, wrong field count check for Dir) would produce silent malformed payloads sent to the Demon agent without any test catching it.

**Suggested test approach**:
Add unit tests using `build_job` with matching `AgentTaskInfo` for each subcommand. Decode the resulting payload bytes and assert subcommand discriminant and field values.
close_reason: Added 8 unit tests covering Dir, Download, Cat, Cd, Remove, Mkdir, Move, and GetPwd encode paths in encode_fs_payload

title: Client - UI Components
description: All egui UI panels and widgets: login dialog, main window layout, session table (agent list), session graph (node visualization), command console (interactive per-agent shell), file browser, process list, loot viewer, and multi-operator chat panel.
close_reason: Completed the egui client UI set, including login, session table/graph, console workspace, file browser, process list, loot, and team chat panels.

title: test: load_tls_identity_from_files when cert exists but key is missing
description:
**File**: common/src/tls.rs
**Gap type**: error path / edge case

`load_tls_identity_from_files_rejects_missing_paths` only tests the case where BOTH files are absent (the cert path is read first, so the key is never attempted). There is no test for the case where the cert file exists but the key file is missing.

This matters because: (1) the error message in `ReadFile { path, .. }` would contain the key path, not the cert path — an easy regression to introduce without test coverage, and (2) the codepath that calls `fs::read` for the key is otherwise unreachable in tests.

**Suggested test approach**:
Write a cert PEM to a temp file, keep the key path non-existent, call `load_tls_identity_from_files` and assert `TlsError::ReadFile { path, .. }` where `path` equals the key file path.
close_reason: Added test covering cert-exists/key-missing edge case; asserts ReadFile error path equals key path

title: test: add direct handler coverage for websocket_handler
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/websocket.rs
**Functions with no tests**:
- `fn websocket_handler` (line 233) — upgrades the HTTP request, applies the operator message-size cap, and hands the socket off to the authenticated operator session handler.

**Why this matters**: this is the public Axum handler on the operator protocol path. Route-level integration tests exercise `websocket_routes()`, but they do not directly pin the handler contract around upgrade behavior, connection cleanup, and oversized-frame enforcement.

**Minimum test scenarios needed**:
1. Happy path: a request upgraded through `websocket_handler` accepts a valid login frame and increments then decrements connection tracking as the socket opens and closes.
2. Error path: an unauthenticated or malformed first frame causes the connection to close without leaving stale authenticated-count state behind.
3. Edge case: a frame larger than `OPERATOR_MAX_MESSAGE_SIZE` is rejected so the handler cannot silently accept oversized operator messages.

**Test location**: inline `#[cfg(test)]` in `teamserver/src/websocket.rs` or `teamserver/tests/<name>.rs`
close_reason: Added three focused tests for websocket_handler: connection tracking lifecycle (happy path), malformed first frame leaves no stale auth count (error path), and oversized pre-auth frame rejection (edge case). All tests pass.

title: refactor: dead _max_download_bytes parameter chain in listener state constructors
description:
Introduced by: Codex (ubuntu-c2-dev01-codex)

After the DownloadTracker refactor in fix: clean up agent downloads on teardown (c188312), the `max_download_bytes: u64` parameter is now dead in three private constructors:
- `HttpListenerState::new` (listeners.rs ~885) — renamed to `_max_download_bytes`
- `SmbListenerState::new` (listeners.rs ~973) — renamed to `_max_download_bytes`
- `DnsListenerState::new` (listeners.rs ~1913) — renamed to `_max_download_bytes`

The callers (`spawn_http_listener_runtime`, `spawn_smb_listener_runtime`, `spawn_dns_listener_runtime`) all still accept and forward `max_download_bytes: u64` through to these constructors where it is silently discarded. The limit is correctly baked into the shared `DownloadTracker` at `ListenerManager::new` time, so there is no functional bug — only unnecessary API noise.

Correct behaviour: remove the `_max_download_bytes` parameter from all three `*State::new` functions and their `spawn_*` wrappers, and remove the corresponding argument at the call sites in `ListenerManager::start_listener`.
close_reason: Removed the dead max_download_bytes parameter chain from listener state constructors and spawn wrappers.

title: Dead zero-key plaintext passthrough in encrypt/decrypt functions is unreachable and contradicts decode_crypto_material error
description:
Introduced by: Codex <noreply@openai.com>

**What is wrong:**
In `crates/teamserver/src/agents.rs`, three functions have a dead-code block immediately after calling `decode_crypto_material`:

- `encrypt_for_agent` (line 348–350)
- `encrypt_for_agent_without_advancing` (line 374–376)
- `decrypt_from_agent` (line 397–399)

Each contains:
```rust
if key.iter().all(|byte| *byte == 0) {
    return Ok(plaintext.to_vec()); // plaintext passthrough (or ciphertext passthrough for decrypt)
}
```

This code is unreachable because `decode_crypto_material` (called just above via `?`) already returns `Err(TeamserverError::InvalidAgentCrypto)` for all-zero keys, causing early return before this block. The block can never execute.

**Why it matters:**
- Dead code is confusing: a reader unfamiliar with `decode_crypto_material`'s zero-key rejection would think these functions silently skip encryption for zero-key agents (plaintext passthrough instead of error).
- The behaviour is actively wrong if somehow reached (encryption bypass vs. error). This directly contradicts the zero-key security policy established by `decode_crypto_material` and tested in `zero_key_agent_transport_is_rejected`.
- The three dead blocks should simply be removed.

**Correct behaviour:** Remove the `if key.iter().all(|byte| *byte == 0)` dead-code blocks from `encrypt_for_agent`, `encrypt_for_agent_without_advancing`, and `decrypt_from_agent`. The `decode_crypto_material` rejection is sufficient and the check is already verified by the existing unit test.
close_reason: Removed unreachable zero-key passthrough branches from agent transport helpers; zero-key rejection now relies solely on decode_crypto_material and existing tests.

title: Discord webhook profile settings are parsed and validated but never used
description:
Introduced by: Codex

crates/common/src/config.rs:34-36 and 730-745 expose WebHook.Discord settings in the supported profile schema and validate them.

There is no runtime consumer for profile.webhook anywhere under crates/teamserver/src or crates/client/src outside parsing/tests, so operators can configure Discord webhook integration that never emits notifications.

This is dead configuration surface and architectural drift: the codebase advertises a feature that the runtime does not implement. The fix should either implement webhook delivery on relevant audit/event paths or remove/reject the config until it exists.
close_reason: Implemented Discord webhook delivery on persisted operator audit events and covered it with teamserver tests.

title: DownloadTracker created fresh per route call — download state never persists
description:
In route_callback_by_command (dispatch.rs:603), a new DownloadTracker::default() is created on every call. This means any multi-chunk download reassembly state stored in the tracker would be immediately discarded at the end of each invocation — chunks would never accumulate into a complete file.

The DownloadTracker should be:
1. Passed in as a parameter from the shared instance in CommandDispatcher::with_handlers (line 131), OR
2. Stored on the shared state struct that route_callback_by_command is called from.

Using a per-call fresh tracker defeats the entire purpose of the HashMap<(u32, u32), DownloadState>. Note that route_callback_by_command at line 603 appears to be a legacy dispatch path; the handler-registration approach in with_handlers (line 131) uses the shared downloads instance. If route_callback_by_command is still active, it must share the same DownloadTracker.
close_reason: Implemented the missing filesystem callback handlers and fixed shared download tracker reuse in builtin dispatch

title: encode_pivot_job_payload and write_len_prefixed_bytes silently corrupt packets on oversized input
description:
Three length-prefix writes use `u32::try_from(len).unwrap_or_default()`, which silently emits 0 instead of an error when the slice length exceeds u32::MAX (4 GiB):
- crates/teamserver/src/agents.rs:544 (payload.len() in encode_pivot_job_payload)
- crates/teamserver/src/agents.rs:552 (inner.len() in encode_pivot_job_payload)
- crates/teamserver/src/websocket.rs:1170 (write_len_prefixed_bytes)

If this code path is ever reached with an oversized buffer, the agent receives a length field of 0 and misreads the framing of all subsequent fields, silently corrupting the protocol stream. These helpers should return a Result and propagate an error rather than defaulting to 0.
close_reason: Fixed: encode_pivot_job_payload and write_len_prefixed_bytes now return Result<_, TeamserverError::PayloadTooLarge> instead of silently writing 0 on oversized input

title: Reconcile Demon transport crypto mode with Havoc CTR reference
description: Issue red-cell-c2-499 requested AES-256-CBC helpers in crates/common, but the Havoc reference implementation currently uses AES-CTR for Demon traffic on both the teamserver and agent sides (see src/Havoc/teamserver/pkg/common/crypt/aes.go and src/Havoc/payloads/Demon/src/crypt/AesCrypt.c). Before wiring crypto into packet parsing and handshake code, verify the intended on-wire mode for Red Cell and either switch the implementation or document an intentional protocol divergence.
close_reason: Verified Havoc uses AES-CTR, switched shared agent crypto helpers to CTR, and corrected protocol docs

title: security: CHECKIN key-rotation accepted without replay/freshness protection
description:
Introduced by: Codex

teamserver/src/dispatch.rs:792-802 — handle_checkin / parse_checkin_metadata

When a COMMAND_CHECKIN payload includes a new 32-byte AES key and 16-byte IV, handle_checkin silently accepts the rotation and resets the CTR offset to zero. There is no nonce, timestamp, or challenge-response to verify the payload is fresh. The code acknowledges this with a warn! that reads 'accepting AES session key rotation from checkin payload without freshness guarantee' but takes no mitigating action.

Impact: an adversary who captures a legitimate CHECKIN packet in transit can replay it against the teamserver to rotate the session key back to a known value, then decrypt subsequent agent traffic or inject spoofed commands for any agent whose key they have replayed.

Note: this is a protocol-level gap inherited from Havoc's design. The fix options are: (a) add a server-generated challenge that the agent must sign in the CHECKIN response, (b) include a monotonic counter / timestamp that the server verifies, or (c) document the known limitation with a security note in the codebase.

This is a distinct finding from red-cell-c2-2puf (which only tracks a test gap for the key-rotation boundary).
close_reason: Documented the inherited Havoc protocol limitation with a SECURITY comment block explaining the replay risk, why a protocol-level fix is incompatible with unmodified Demon agents, and actionable operator mitigations (mTLS/VPN, treat unexpected rotations as replay indicators). Improved the warn! log message to surface the security context explicitly.

title: Client - Python API
description: Embed Python (via PyO3) in the client for operator scripting. Python script manager UI panel, custom command registration from scripts, event callbacks, and access to agent/listener objects from Python. Preserves compatibility with existing Havoc client-side Python modules.
close_reason: Python API fully implemented: embedded PyO3 runtime, havoc/red_cell/havocui module aliases, command registration, agent checkin callbacks, script manager, output capture, havocui tabs, agent/listener proxies, command queuing — all with comprehensive test coverage

title: Per-agent job queue is unbounded — authenticated operator OOM vector
description:
Introduced by: Codex

teamserver/src/agents.rs — AgentEntry::jobs (line 69) is a Mutex<VecDeque<Job>> with no maximum depth. An authenticated operator can call any task-enqueue API endpoint repeatedly without the agent ever checking in to consume jobs, growing the VecDeque without bound. With large payloads (e.g. shellcode uploads that get queued but not dispatched) the teamserver process will exhaust heap memory. The request_contexts map is capped at MAX_REQUEST_CONTEXTS=10_000 with eviction, but the job VecDeque itself has no such cap.

Fix: add a per-agent maximum job queue depth constant (e.g. 1_000) and return TeamserverError before push_back when the limit is reached.
close_reason: Added MAX_JOB_QUEUE_DEPTH=1_000 constant in agents.rs and check in enqueue_job (both direct and pivot-routed paths) returning TeamserverError::QueueFull when the limit is reached. QueueFull error variant added to database.rs. Tests cover at-capacity rejection and post-dequeue re-enqueue.

title: test: add client crate integration smoke tests for persisted login state
description:
**Files**: client/src/login.rs, client/src/local_config.rs
**Functions with no tests**:
- The client crate currently has no client/tests/*.rs integration tests.
- Public behavior around LoginFormState::new (line 34 in client/src/login.rs), LoginFormState::build_login_message (line 60), and LocalConfig persistence wrappers is only covered by inline unit tests.

**Why this matters**: the operator client’s startup/login flow depends on these pieces working together across module boundaries. Unit tests catch local logic, but they do not verify the crate-level API composition that restores saved settings and constructs the outbound login message.

**Minimum test scenarios needed**:
1. Happy path: persisted local config feeds a new login form state and produces the expected login message.
2. Error path: malformed persisted config falls back to defaults without breaking login message construction.
3. Edge case: optional fields such as CA cert and certificate fingerprint remain unset/serialized correctly in the integrated flow.

**Test location**: client/tests/login_persistence.rs
close_reason: Added client integration smoke tests covering persisted login config, invalid config fallback, and optional TLS fields.

title: Download tracker drops valid partial transfers after 15 minutes of inactivity
description:
Introduced by: Codex <noreply@openai.com>

teamserver/src/dispatch.rs:37, teamserver/src/dispatch.rs:1007-1008, teamserver/src/dispatch.rs:1064-1066, teamserver/src/dispatch.rs:1083-1094

The new partial-download cap logic hardcodes DOWNLOAD_TRACKER_STALE_TIMEOUT to 15 minutes and prunes tracked downloads before every append() and finish(). Any legitimate Demon file transfer that sits idle for more than 15 minutes is silently discarded, so the next FileWrite callback fails with "download ... was not opened" and the eventual FileClose/FileSystem close cannot persist loot or emit a completion event. Havoc compatibility does not define an inactivity timeout for active downloads, and slow or operator-paused transfers can legitimately exceed 15 minutes.

Correct behavior should avoid expiring active downloads on a fixed hardcoded timer, or make the timeout explicitly configurable and coordinated with the operator/runtime semantics so long-running transfers are not dropped mid-stream.
close_reason: Removed the fixed download inactivity timeout so partial transfers stay tracked until finish or cap enforcement.

title: test: COMMAND_CHECKIN with truncated inner payload returns parse error instead of panicking
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/demon.rs
**Gap type**: error path / protocol robustness

The callback parsing path decrypts the outer COMMAND_CHECKIN payload and then iterates over the inner sub-packages. If the inner payload is truncated (e.g., a length-prefix claims 100 bytes but only 10 remain), the parser should return a `DemonParserError` rather than panic or silently produce garbage packages. This malformed-input path is not currently tested.

A real Demon agent should never send such a packet, but an attacker who can MITM or inject an HTTP body onto the listener port could craft one. Without a test, a future refactor of the inner parsing loop could introduce an out-of-bounds panic.

**Suggested test approach**:
Build a COMMAND_CHECKIN packet where the encrypted payload decrypts to a byte sequence with a length-prefix that exceeds the remaining buffer. Parse it with `DemonPacketParser` and assert the result is `Err(DemonParserError::...)` with no panic.
close_reason: Added parse_checkin_with_truncated_inner_payload_returns_parse_error test: exercises DemonPacketParser::parse_at with a COMMAND_CHECKIN whose decrypted inner buffer has a sub-package length-prefix exceeding remaining bytes; asserts Err(DemonParserError::Protocol) with no panic

title: test: add AppState unit coverage for ListenerError message path
description:
**File**: client/src/transport.rs
**Gap type**: existence

The `OperatorMessage::ListenerError` branch (line 392) upserts a listener with an error status and pushes a chat notification. The only existing reference to `ListenerErrorInfo` in the test suite is the trivially-useless `message_variants_used_by_transport_state_reducer_are_constructible` test, which constructs the struct but makes no assertions about apply_operator_message behavior.

If this branch silently breaks (e.g., the status string format changes, or the chat push is dropped), the UI would fail to show listener error notifications without any test failure.

**Suggested test approach**:
Apply an `OperatorMessage::ListenerError` to a fresh `AppState` that already contains a listener with the same name. Assert that (1) the listener status is updated to start with "Error:", and (2) a chat message is appended. Also test the case where the listener name does not exist yet — `upsert_listener` should create a new entry.
close_reason: Added two unit tests: one verifying ListenerError updates an existing listener's status and pushes a chat message, one verifying upsert creates a new entry when no listener with that name exists

title: "Erro" truncated event type in kerberos and socket callback handlers
description:
In crates/teamserver/src/dispatch.rs, 8 error events use "Erro" instead of "Error" as the Type field sent to operator clients. The Havoc operator client relies on this string for color-coding and display formatting — "Error" (red), "Good" (green), "Info" (blue). Using "Erro" means error conditions are silently shown with no color coding.

Affected lines in dispatch.rs: 610, 635, 657, 673, 721, 772, 793, 815

Handlers affected: handle_kerberos_callback (Luid, Klist, Purge, Ptt subcommands) and handle_socket_callback (ReversePortForward start/clear, Read, Write failure branches).

Fix: replace all instances of "Erro" with "Error" in agent_response_event kind arguments in these handlers.
close_reason: Fixed all 9 truncated "Erro" → "Error" event types in kerberos callback (Luid, Klist, Purge, Ptt), socket callback (ReversePortForward start/clear, Read, Write), and filesystem cat handlers

title: audit: update_listener and delete_listener REST endpoints missing audit trail
description:
The audit trail implementation in 001245d covers listener.create, listener.start, and listener.stop for both the REST API and WebSocket paths, but update_listener (PUT /api/v1/listeners/{name}) and delete_listener (DELETE /api/v1/listeners/{name}) are not audited.

Both handlers still use _identity (underscore-prefixed, identity ignored) and contain no record_audit_entry calls. A listener deletion is a security-relevant event that must appear in the audit log.

Affected files:
- crates/teamserver/src/api.rs:929-941 (update_listener) 
- crates/teamserver/src/api.rs:958-965 (delete_listener)

Fix: change _identity to identity in both handlers and add record_audit_entry calls for success and failure paths, consistent with the pattern already used by create_listener / start_listener / stop_listener.
close_reason: Added audit trail entries to update_listener and delete_listener REST handlers with success/failure recording, consistent with existing listener audit pattern

title: test: socket callback subcommands rportfwd/write/close/connect have zero test coverage
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/dispatch.rs
**Gap type**: error path + existence

`handle_socket_callback` dispatches on 10+ DemonSocketCommand variants but only `Socket::Read` (error path) is tested. The following branches have zero test coverage:

- `ReversePortForwardAdd` — success vs failure differ in formatted message and event type (Info vs Error); neither is tested
- `ReversePortForwardList` — calls `format_rportfwd_list` which has its own parsing logic; untested
- `ReversePortForwardRemove` — conditional branch on socket_type for event emission; untested
- `ReversePortForwardClear` — success vs failure branches; neither tested
- `Write`, `Close`, `Connect` — their implementations are untested
- Invalid/unknown subcommand — should return `CommandDispatchError::InvalidCallbackPayload`; not tested

A bug in any of these branches (wrong field order, swapped success/failure, wrong event type) would ship silently.

**Suggested test approach**:
Build a payload with the appropriate subcommand prefix and field bytes, dispatch it via `CommandDispatcher`, then assert on the broadcast OperatorMessage kind and message text. Test at minimum: ReversePortForwardAdd success, ReversePortForwardAdd failure, ReversePortForwardClear success/failure, and one invalid subcommand.
close_reason: Added socket callback coverage for reverse port forward, write, connect/close, and invalid subcommand paths

title: HTTP 429 rate-limit response missing Retry-After header
description: In crates/teamserver/src/api.rs, when the rate limit is exceeded the ApiAuthError::RateLimited variant serialises retry_after_seconds into the JSON response body, but the HTTP Retry-After header (RFC 9110 §10.2.3) is never set on the response. HTTP clients, proxies, and rate-limit-aware libraries look for this header and will not back off correctly without it. Fix: in the IntoResponse implementation for ApiAuthError::RateLimited, add the Retry-After header to the response with value retry_after_seconds before returning the JSON body.
close_reason: Added Retry-After header to the HTTP 429 response in the ApiAuthError::RateLimited IntoResponse impl, with test coverage

title: HTTP listener never captures real peer IP — external_ip always 0.0.0.0
description:
Introduced by: Codex

crates/teamserver/src/listeners.rs:887,900 — Both the HTTP and HTTPS listener variants call server.serve(router.into_make_service()) instead of router.into_make_service_with_connect_info::<SocketAddr>(). Because ConnectInfo is not injected into requests, the http_listener_handler has no access to the peer socket address.

crates/teamserver/src/listeners.rs:2040 — extract_external_ip() falls back to reading the x-real-ip header, which is fully agent-controlled (spoofable), then returns '0.0.0.0' if the header is absent. In practice, all HTTP-connected agents register with external_ip = '0.0.0.0' unless a proxy sets x-real-ip.

Contrast with: DNS listener correctly uses peer.ip().to_string() from recv_from(); the websocket server uses into_make_service_with_connect_info() and ConnectInfo<SocketAddr>.

Fix: switch to router.into_make_service_with_connect_info::<SocketAddr>() in spawn_http_listener_runtime, add ConnectInfo<SocketAddr> extractor to http_listener_handler, and use the socket address as the primary external IP source (with x-forwarded-for / x-real-ip as opt-in fallbacks via behind_redirector).
close_reason: Added HTTP listener regressions for peer IP capture and trusted redirector forwarding.

title: test: add unit tests for LootRepository multi-field filtered queries
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/database.rs (LootRepository, lines 984-1018)
**Functions with partial tests**:
- `fn query_filtered` (line 985) — dynamic query builder with 9 filter fields; existing test only exercises kind, name, file_path, agent_id filters in isolation; combined multi-field filter is untested
- `fn count_filtered` (line 1004) — counts without fetching; existing test covers it but only with single-field filters

**Why this matters**: The append_loot_filters helper builds SQL by pushing bind parameters in sequence. A mis-ordered push or missing AND separator causes incorrect or insecure queries. Multi-field combinations expose bugs that single-field tests cannot detect — for example, operator_contains and pattern_contains both read from JSON sub-fields and a wrong json_extract path silently returns zero rows.

**Minimum test scenarios needed**:
1. Happy path: insert loot rows with different kinds, agents, and operators; filter by (agent_id AND kind) and verify only the matching row is returned.
2. Happy path: filter by operator_contains (JSON metadata field) and verify it matches the embedded JSON value correctly.
3. Edge case: count_filtered with multiple simultaneously active filters returns the same count as the length of query_filtered results.
4. Edge case: since/until timestamp range with no matching rows returns an empty Vec and count=0.

**Test location**: `teamserver/tests/database.rs` alongside existing loot_repository tests
close_reason: Added 4 multi-field filter tests: agent_id+kind combined, operator_contains JSON path, count==len with 4 simultaneous filters, since/until range returning empty

title: Pivot child agents are persisted with listener_name "null"
description:
Introduced by: Michel Klomp

teamserver/src/dispatch.rs:1058 calls DemonPacketParser::parse() from handle_pivot_connect_callback, and parse() hardcodes listener_name="null" in teamserver/src/demon.rs:118. Child agents registered through SMB pivot connect therefore lose their accepting-listener provenance in the registry/database, and operator snapshots later surface them as "null" via teamserver/src/websocket.rs:2350-2352.

Why it matters: pivoted agents no longer report a meaningful listener in operator state, audit context, or persisted metadata, which breaks session provenance and makes SMB pivot chains look like orphaned agents.

Fix: thread the real pivot listener/transport name through the nested DEMON_INIT path instead of using DemonPacketParser::parse(), and add an end-to-end test that the child snapshot preserves listener provenance after pivot registration.
close_reason: thread parent listener_name via parse_for_listener() in handle_pivot_connect_callback; AgentNew event now uses the real listener_name; updated existing test and added snapshot test to verify child preserves listener provenance

title: auth: error message has typo "exits" instead of "exist"
description: auth.rs line 89: AuthenticationFailure::UnknownUser returns "User doesn't exits" — should be "User doesn't exist". The typo is surfaced directly to the operator login UI and audit log. Introduced in commit 8664e86 by michel@woodenshoe.org. P3 severity.
close_reason: Corrected the unknown-user authentication error text and updated the auth message test.

title: plugins: silent mutex poison recovery in install_as_active/active() can mask panics
description: In crates/teamserver/src/plugins.rs lines 171-188, both install_as_active() and active() recover a poisoned Mutex by calling poisoned.into_inner(). A poisoned mutex means another thread panicked while holding the lock, leaving the guarded value in an unknown state. Silently recovering it means the plugin runtime could be left in a corrupted state with no indication. The correct approach is to treat a poisoned lock as a fatal error (return Err or panic with a clear message), not silently ignore it. This could cause subtle, hard-to-debug correctness issues.
close_reason: install_as_active() and current() now return PluginError::MutexPoisoned instead of silently recovering via into_inner(). All callers propagate the error.

title: test: unbounded spin loop in socket_connect_and_close_callbacks test can hang CI
close_reason: Wrapped the socket lifecycle test polling loop in a 5s timeout so CI fails fast instead of hanging.

title: payload: dynamic MinGW/NASM toolchain discovery and version verification
description: teamserver/src/payload_builder.rs has hardcoded toolchain paths for MinGW gcc and NASM. Add: runtime discovery via PATH and common install locations (/usr/bin, /usr/local/bin, /opt/mingw), version verification (gcc --version, nasm --version) with minimum version requirements, clear error messages when toolchain is missing or outdated. Store discovered paths in PayloadBuilderService config at startup.
close_reason: Added runtime toolchain discovery (PATH + /usr/bin, /usr/local/bin, /opt/mingw), version verification (GCC >= 10.0, NASM >= 2.14) with clear error messages, and startup info logging of discovered paths/versions

title: websocket: execute_agent_task has triplicated job-queuing block
description: In websocket.rs::execute_agent_task (lines 633-654), the three branches (plugin command returns false, plugin present but no command matched, no plugin runtime) each contain an identical 5-line block: build_jobs -> queue all -> return count. This should be extracted into a shared path to avoid divergence. If one branch is fixed (e.g. error handling change) the others are silently missed.
close_reason: Deduplicated triplicated job-queuing block into a single shared path with a handled_by_plugin boolean

title: read_smb_frame allocates unbounded heap buffer from untrusted length field
description:
In listeners.rs, read_smb_frame (line 1173) reads a 32-bit length field directly from the SMB pipe stream and immediately allocates 'vec![0_u8; payload_len]' without any upper-bound check. A malicious or malfunctioning client can send a length of up to u32::MAX (~4 GB), triggering a multi-gigabyte heap allocation that will likely panic or OOM-kill the teamserver process.

Fix: add a sanity cap before the allocation. A reasonable limit is the maximum Demon packet size (e.g. 16 MiB or whatever is used for the HTTP path). Return an io::Error(InvalidData) if payload_len exceeds that cap, which will cause handle_smb_connection to log a warning and close the connection cleanly.

Location: crates/teamserver/src/listeners.rs, read_smb_frame function, line ~1184-1187.
close_reason: Rejected oversized SMB frame lengths before allocation and added regression coverage.

title: wait_for_callback_drain has TOCTOU race that causes spurious full-timeout delay
close_reason: Fixed the shutdown Notify TOCTOU race by registering waiters before checking state and added a regression test for immediate callback drain.

title: assembly_dispatch.rs: listener setup boilerplate copy-pasted 4 times
description:
Introduced by: Claude (dev01, Ubuntu-C2-dev01-claude)

Four test files copy-paste the same HttpListenerConfig setup block, differing only in listener name:
- teamserver/tests/assembly_dispatch.rs: 4 identical 20-field blocks
- teamserver/tests/output_dispatch.rs: 4 identical 20-field blocks
- teamserver/tests/screenshot_dispatch.rs: 1 block (http_listener helper)
- teamserver/tests/socks5_relay.rs: 1 block (dispatch_http_listener helper)

A change to any listener field (e.g. adding a new required field) requires 10 edits across 4 files.

All files have a start_server() pattern — a shared http_listener_config(name: &str, port: u16) -> ListenerConfig helper in tests/common/ would absorb all boilerplate.

Correct fix: move the HttpListenerConfig construction into tests/common/mod.rs as a reusable helper.

title: test: SessionActivityRecord::try_from error paths for non-operator-prefixed action not tested
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/audit.rs
**Gap type**: error path

`SessionActivityRecord::try_from(AuditLogEntry)` returns `Err(TeamserverError::InvalidPersistedValue)` when:
1. The `action` field does not have the `"operator."` prefix (line 250-255) — should fail clearly rather than silently producing a wrong `activity` label.
2. The `id` field is `None` (line 258-263) — same pattern as the already-tested `AuditRecord::try_from` id-missing case.

The existing tests only exercise the `AuditRecord` conversion path. The `SessionActivityRecord` conversion has no tests at all — an incorrect strip/prefix logic change would go unnoticed.

**Suggested test approach**:
(1) Call `SessionActivityRecord::try_from` with an entry whose `action` is `"agent.task"` (no operator prefix) and assert the returned error contains the action name. (2) Call with `id: None` and assert `TeamserverError::InvalidPersistedValue`.
close_reason: Added SessionActivityRecord::try_from tests for invalid action prefixes and missing ids

title: teamserver: persist ctr_block_offset to SQLite and reload on startup
description: The CTR offset fix (red-cell-c2-9pf) describes the symptom but not the implementation. AgentEntry.ctr_block_offset must be written to the ts_agents table on every update and read back in AgentRegistry::load(). Without this, all agents lose comms after any teamserver restart. Schema change: add ctr_block_offset INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 to ts_agents. Update AgentRegistry::insert and encrypt_for_agent/decrypt_from_agent to persist the updated offset after each use.
close_reason: ctr_block_offset persisted to ts_agents via migration 20260311121000, written in AgentRepository and reloaded in AgentRegistry::load

title: WebSocket auth path logs live session tokens
description:
Introduced by: Codex <noreply@openai.com>

crates/teamserver/src/websocket.rs:322-326 logs the authenticated session token at info level immediately after authentication.

Those tokens are the bearer secrets used to identify authenticated operator sessions. Any stdout collector, file appender, or centralized log sink can replay them to impersonate operators. The fix is to stop logging the token value entirely; keep only non-secret identifiers such as username and connection_id, or log a one-way fingerprint if correlation is required.
close_reason: Removed the authenticated websocket session token from the operator connect log entry.

title: DEMON_INIT does not reject all-zero AES IV
description:
Introduced by: Codex

teamserver/src/demon.rs — parse_init_agent() at line 244 checks that the AES key is not all-zero before calling decrypt_agent_data(), but does not apply the same check to the IV. An adversary that sends a DEMON_INIT packet with a non-zero key but an all-zero 16-byte IV produces a deterministic CTR keystream rooted at block 0 with no IV entropy. This weakens the effective security of the session to key-only protection. The all-zero IV guard should mirror the all-zero key guard already present. Fix: add iv.iter().all(|b| *b == 0) check immediately after the key check and return DemonParserError::InvalidInit("all-zero AES IV is not allowed").
close_reason: Added all-zero IV guard in parse_init_agent() mirroring the existing key guard; returns InvalidInit('all-zero AES IV is not allowed'). Added test parse_rejects_plaintext_zero_iv_init.

title: Fresh profile external listeners still abort teamserver startup
description:
Introduced by: Codex

teamserver/src/main.rs:80-81 always calls start_new_profile_listeners() during boot. That helper then starts every Created listener from the loaded profile and propagates any start failure (teamserver/src/main.rs:214-234). In teamserver/src/listeners.rs:1473 and teamserver/src/listeners.rs:1489-1496, External listeners still hard-fail because no runtime exists.

The restore path for persisted Running external listeners was hardened previously, but a fresh profile that declares an External listener will still make the whole teamserver fail to boot instead of degrading that listener to Error and continuing.

Fix: treat unsupported profile-declared external listeners the same way restore_running() now treats start failures: persist an Error state, broadcast/log the failure, and continue booting the rest of the server. Add a startup test that includes an External listener in the profile and asserts the teamserver stays up while that listener is marked Error.
close_reason: Fresh profile external listeners now degrade to Error during startup without aborting teamserver boot; regression test added.

title: test: deserialize_bool_from_any rejects integer values outside {0,1}
description:
**File**: common/src/domain.rs
**Gap type**: error path

`deserialize_bool_from_any` has an explicit error branch for integer values that are neither 0 nor 1 (line 435):
```rust
_ => Err(de::Error::custom(format!("invalid boolean number \`{value}\`")))
```
No test exercises this path. The function is used for the `Active` and `Elevated` fields on every `AgentInfo` deserialized from the Havoc operator protocol. A broken validation here would silently treat unexpected integer values as booleans at parse time.

**Suggested test approach**:
Construct an `AgentInfo` JSON payload with `"Active": 2` (or another value outside {0,1}) and assert `serde_json::from_value` returns an error containing 'invalid boolean number'.
close_reason: added test exercising the integer outside {0,1} error branch via AgentRecord with Active: 2

title: AgentRegistry ignores ctr_offset and reuses the base IV for every agent packet
description:
Introduced by: Codex

teamserver/src/agents.rs:382-425 keeps a persisted ctr_offset field, but encrypt_for_agent, encrypt_for_agent_without_advancing, and decrypt_from_agent all call AES-CTR with the original IV and never derive or apply the stored block offset. teamserver/src/agents.rs:1302-1388 then locks this behavior in tests by asserting repeated encryptions are identical and that set_ctr_offset does not affect the keystream.

Why it matters: the same keystream is reused across every packet for a given agent session, so XORing any two ciphertexts reveals the XOR of their plaintexts whenever agent traffic is exposed off-TLS. It also makes ctr_offset dead state that suggests synchronization exists when it does not.

What the fix should do: either remove ctr_offset entirely and document the compatibility tradeoff everywhere it appears, or restore a real counter-offset model consistently across encrypt/decrypt/ack paths and update the Havoc-compatibility tests to prove the intended wire behavior.
close_reason: Implemented offset-aware AES-CTR handling for agent transport, persisted block advancement, and updated transport/integration tests.

title: No brute-force protection on WebSocket operator login
description:
Introduced by: Codex <noreply@openai.com>

crates/teamserver/src/websocket.rs:229-357 — The WebSocket authentication handler (`handle_authentication`) has no rate limiting, failed-attempt counter, or delay. An attacker can connect, attempt a login, disconnect on failure, and immediately reconnect — repeating at network speed to brute-force operator passwords.

The REST API has per-key rate limiting (`ApiRateLimit`) but the WebSocket path does not.

Fix: maintain a per-source-IP (or global) failed-login counter backed by a sliding-window or token-bucket, and add a short (e.g., 2 s) delay before responding to failed login attempts.
close_reason: Added per-IP LoginRateLimiter with sliding 60s window (5 max failures), 2-second delay on failed auth, ConnectInfo IP extraction, and tests

title: crypto.rs: ctr_blocks_for_length uses manual div_ceil — clippy lint failure
description:
Introduced by: Cursor (Ubuntu-HTB-cursor, working on red-cell-c2-2bm)

crates/common/src/crypto.rs, function ctr_blocks_for_length (uncommitted local change):

    let blocks = (data_len + AES_BLOCK_LEN - 1) / AES_BLOCK_LEN;

clippy::manual_div_ceil fires under `-D warnings`, causing the workspace to fail to compile with clippy.

Fix: replace with `data_len.div_ceil(AES_BLOCK_LEN)` per the clippy suggestion. This is a Rust 1.73+ stable API.
close_reason: Fixed as part of AES-CTR IV reuse fix (red-cell-c2-2bm)

title: demon.rs: build_init_ack test uses stale sync API — cargo test fails to compile
description:
Introduced by: Cursor (Ubuntu-HTB-cursor, working on red-cell-c2-2bm)

crates/teamserver/src/demon.rs:641-653 — test build_init_ack_encrypts_agent_identifier

build_init_ack was refactored from:
  pub fn build_init_ack(agent_id: u32, encryption: &AgentEncryptionInfo) -> Result<Vec<u8>, DemonParserError>
to:
  pub async fn build_init_ack(registry: &crate::AgentRegistry, agent_id: u32) -> Result<Vec<u8>, DemonParserError>

The test still calls the old signature build_init_ack(0xAABB_CCDD, &encryption) and uses #[test] instead of #[tokio::test]. This causes two compile errors:
  E0308: wrong argument count/types
  E0599: .expect() called on a Future, not a Result

Result: cargo test --workspace fails to compile the lib test binary. All 385 tests in the teamserver crate are unreachable.

Fix: Update the test to use a test registry, change to #[tokio::test], and call build_init_ack(&registry, agent_id).await.
close_reason: Fixed as part of AES-CTR IV reuse fix (red-cell-c2-2bm)

title: Implement WebSocket command handlers (agent tasking)
description: In crates/teamserver: handle operator commands for agent interaction. AgentTask: validate agent exists, check operator RBAC permission, enqueue job in agent's job queue, broadcast AgentTask event to all operators. AgentKill. AgentNote (add note to agent). AgentRemove. All with proper RBAC checks.
close_reason: Implemented operator WebSocket handlers for agent task, note, kill, and remove commands with RBAC checks and registry updates

title: QA runner: cargo test fails without RUSTFLAGS — python3.12-dev not installed
description:
The QA review environment is missing `python3.12-dev` (libpython3.12.so dev symlink). Running `cargo test --workspace` fails with:

  rust-lld: error: unable to find library -lpython3.12

The workaround is to run with:
  RUSTFLAGS="-L /usr/lib/python3.12/config-3.12-x86_64-linux-gnu" cargo test --workspace

Fix: install `python3.12-dev` on the QA/dev machines, OR add a `.cargo/config.toml` at the repo root with:
  [build]
  rustflags = ["-L", "/usr/lib/python3.12/config-3.12-x86_64-linux-gnu"]

All 476 tests pass once the library path is provided. This is a CI/environment configuration issue, not a code bug.
close_reason: Added a repo-level .cargo/config.toml with the Python 3.12 linker path for QA hosts missing python3.12-dev linker defaults.

title: test: AuditRecord::try_from returns InvalidPersistedValue when id is None
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/audit.rs
**Gap type**: error path

`SessionActivityRecord::try_from` has a test for the `id = None` case (`session_activity_record_requires_id`). The identical error branch in `AuditRecord::try_from` — which returns `Err(TeamserverError::InvalidPersistedValue { field: "id", ... })` when `entry.id` is `None` — has no test. Database rows should always have an `id` assigned by SQLite, but if they somehow don't, silent success (with the wrong id) would be worse than a clear error. Without a test this branch is dead coverage.

**Suggested test approach**:
Construct an `AuditLogEntry` with `id: None` and valid other fields, call `AuditRecord::try_from`, and assert `Err(TeamserverError::InvalidPersistedValue { field: "id", .. })`.
close_reason: Added audit_record_requires_id test covering the id=None error path in AuditRecord::try_from

title: process: Claude did not close red-cell-c2-30fe after committing the fix
description:
Introduced by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Commit 446ae04 (feat(dispatch): reject all-zero AES key in COMMAND_CHECKIN) fully implements the fix described in red-cell-c2-30fe and adds the required regression test. However, the agent never ran `br close red-cell-c2-30fe` or committed a chore: close line, so the issue remains stuck in IN_PROGRESS in the tracker.

Impact: the issue backlog is inaccurate; future agents may attempt to re-implement work that is already done.

Fix: close red-cell-c2-30fe as resolved, pointing at commit 446ae04 as the completion artifact.
close_reason: red-cell-c2-30fe was already closed (pointing at commit 446ae04); no further action needed

title: test: Profile::validate DNS listener validation paths are untested
description:
**File**: common/src/config.rs
**Gap type**: error path

The `Profile::validate` method contains three DNS listener validation checks that have zero test coverage:
- `listener.name.trim().is_empty()` → pushes error `Listeners.Dns.Name must not be empty`
- `listener.domain.trim().is_empty()` → pushes error `Listeners.Dns "X" must define Domain`
- `listener.port_bind == 0` → pushes error `Listeners.Dns "X" must define a PortBind greater than zero`

Every other listener type (HTTP, SMB, External) has at least one test exercising its validation checks. The DNS listener was added as a new feature and its validation was never tested. A bug in these checks (e.g., an incorrect field reference) would be invisible.

**Suggested test approach**:
Create a `rejects_invalid_dns_listener_configuration` test that builds a profile with a DNS listener where name, domain, and port_bind are all invalid (empty/zero), then asserts that all three validation errors appear in the result.
close_reason: Added rejects_invalid_dns_listener_configuration test covering all three DNS validation error paths (empty name, empty domain, zero port_bind)

title: test: parse_checkin_metadata rejects agent ID mismatch and truncated payloads
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/dispatch/checkin.rs
**Gap type**: error path / protocol parsing

`parse_checkin_metadata` (private to the module) has two explicit error paths with no tests:
1. Payloads shorter than `CHECKIN_METADATA_PREFIX_LEN` (48 bytes) must return `InvalidCallbackPayload` — truncated input is a common fuzzing surface.
2. When the agent ID embedded in the payload mismatches the outer envelope's agent ID, the function must return an error — this mismatch would silently corrupt agent state if the check were removed.

Without tests, regressions in either early-return condition would be invisible. Because this is the gate before any agent metadata is updated, both paths are in the security-relevant trust boundary.

**Suggested test approach**:
Drive `parse_checkin_metadata` directly (or via a test helper that builds a minimal CHECKIN payload): supply a 47-byte payload to trigger the truncation error, then supply a full payload with a mismatched agent ID to trigger the mismatch error, asserting the correct `CommandDispatchError` variant and message in each case.

title: test: operator_agent_info with orphan agent produces empty pivot fields
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/agent_events.rs
**Gap type**: edge case

The `operator_agent_info` function has two PivotInfo-dependent output fields: `pivots.parent` (Option<String>) and `pivot_parent` (String). When the agent is a root (no parent), `pivot_parent` falls back to `unwrap_or_default()` → empty string, and `pivots.parent` is None. When children is empty, `pivots.links` is an empty Vec. No test covers this combination.

Without a test here, a future refactor could incorrectly populate `pivot_parent` (e.g., using a sentinel value) without any test failure. The Havoc client relies on this field being an empty string for non-pivot agents to display the session tree correctly.

**Suggested test approach**:
Call `operator_agent_info` with `PivotInfo { parent: None, children: vec![] }`, then assert `info.pivot_parent.is_empty()`, `info.pivots.parent.is_none()`, and `info.pivots.links.is_empty()`.
close_reason: test operator_agent_info_with_no_parent_or_children_leaves_pivot_fields_empty already implemented and passing

title: test: deserializing an unknown EventCode numeric value must return a serde error
description:
**File**: common/src/operator.rs
**Gap type**: error path

The `numeric_code!` macro generates a `Deserialize` impl that calls `from_u32`, which returns `None` for unrecognized values and maps this to a serde error. There is no test that sends a numeric event code that has no corresponding `EventCode` variant (e.g., 0xFF or 0x99) and asserts the deserialization returns `Err`. Without this test, a future refactoring of the macro could silently start accepting arbitrary codes or panicking on unknown values, and no test would catch the regression.

This is security-relevant: operator messages arrive over WebSocket connections and the event code is the first dispatch key. A malformed or malicious client could send unexpected codes.

**Suggested test approach**:
Build a raw JSON message with `"Head": {"Event": 255}` (or any value not in the `EventCode` enum) and assert that `serde_json::from_value::<OperatorMessage>(…)` returns `Err`. Repeat for a valid `EventCode` paired with an unknown `SubEvent` (e.g., event=1 / sub_event=0xFF) to cover the catch-all arm at line 1025.

title: Teamserver - Authentication & RBAC
description: Operator authentication (SHA3-256 password hashing, session management) and role-based access control system. Operators should have differentiated permissions (e.g. admin, operator, analyst/read-only). This is a new feature beyond what Havoc provides.
close_reason: Implemented operator authentication, session tracking, runtime operator persistence, and RBAC enforcement across WebSocket and REST surfaces; verified with full workspace checks.

title: Implement Demon payload builder
description: In crates/teamserver: wrap the Demon agent compilation pipeline in Rust. Given a listener config and Demon options (sleep, jitter, sleep obfuscation technique, injection settings, output format), invoke MinGW and NASM to compile a Demon binary. Stream build output to operator. Deliver compiled EXE/DLL/shellcode bytes back via WebSocket. Validate compiler toolchain availability at startup.
close_reason: Implemented a validated Demon payload builder with WebSocket streaming, artifact delivery, and startup toolchain checks

title: perf: agent.checkin audit write blocks hot callback path — should be background task like agent.dead
description:
Introduced by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

teamserver/src/dispatch.rs:871-890 — handle_checkin()

The agent.checkin audit write (record_operator_action) is awaited inline in handle_checkin(), which runs on every agent callback. For a deployment with 1000 agents checking in every 5 seconds, this is 200 DB writes/second. Since SQLite serialises writes, this creates a concurrency bottleneck on the hot path.

By contrast, the agent.dead audit write in agent_liveness.rs:140-161 is correctly done in a background tokio::spawn, explicitly to avoid adding await points between the event broadcast and socket pruning.

The same pattern should be applied here: wrap the record_operator_action call in tokio::spawn in handle_checkin so that the audit write does not delay the checkin response back to the agent.
close_reason: Wrapped record_operator_action in tokio::spawn in handle_checkin(); updated unit test to yield_now twice before querying audit log, matching the agent.dead pattern from agent_liveness.rs

title: Implement AES-256-CBC crypto for agent communication
description: In crates/common: implement AES-256-CBC encryption/decryption for Demon agent communications. Each agent has a unique 32-byte key and 16-byte IV. Use the aes + cbc crates (RustCrypto). Functions: encrypt_agent_data(key, iv, plaintext) -> Vec<u8>, decrypt_agent_data(key, iv, ciphertext) -> Result<Vec<u8>>. Key generation for new agents. Write unit tests verifying interoperability with known Havoc-produced ciphertext.
close_reason: Implemented AES-256-CBC agent crypto helpers in crates/common with encryption, decryption, key generation, and unit tests.

title: Set up egui client binary and app skeleton
description: In crates/client: main.rs entry point for red-cell-client. eframe/egui app setup with window configuration (title, size, dark theme). App state struct holding: connection status, operator info, list of agents, listeners, loot, chat messages. Placeholder panels for all UI sections. CLI args: --server <url> for teamserver address.
close_reason: Implemented the red-cell-client egui app skeleton, CLI server argument, and placeholder panels.

title: DNS listener spawns an unbounded task for every UDP packet
description:
Introduced by: Michel Klomp

teamserver/src/listeners.rs:2294-2305 — the DNS runtime clones the packet and calls `tokio::spawn` for every received datagram with no semaphore, join set, or backpressure. A spoofed UDP flood can therefore create unbounded tasks and heap allocations long before protocol validation rejects the payload, which turns the DNS listener into an easy CPU/memory exhaustion target. Fix by processing packets inline on the receive loop or by gating spawned work behind a bounded concurrency limit and dropping/refusing excess load.
close_reason: Processed DNS UDP packets inline on the receive loop and added burst coverage to avoid unbounded spawned work under flood traffic.

title: DEMON_INIT with agent_id=0 accepted — reserved/sentinel ID not blocked
description:
Introduced by: Claude Sonnet 4.6

teamserver/src/demon.rs:155-183 — parse_at_for_listener accepts any agent_id value from the network without validating that it is non-zero. A DEMON_INIT packet with agent_id=0 in both the outer transport header and the encrypted metadata will pass all checks (weak-key check, duplicate-registration check, header-vs-payload mismatch check) and be registered as a legitimate agent with the sentinel ID 0x00000000.

Agent ID 0 is a natural sentinel/error value in many contexts. Accepting it means that error-handling paths that return 0 as a default agent ID could incorrectly match a registered agent. Additionally, if an attacker sends an init with agent_id=0, subsequent reconnect probes or callbacks from legitimate agents whose ID resolves to 0 through any arithmetic error would be incorrectly associated with the attacker-registered agent.

The test parse_rejects_init_with_zero_header_id_and_different_payload_id (demon.rs:1332) only tests the case where header ID (0) differs from payload ID (0xAAAABBBB), not where both are 0.

Fix: Add an explicit check in parse_at_for_listener: if envelope.header.agent_id == 0 return DemonParserError::InvalidInit.
close_reason: Added zero agent_id check in parse_at_for_listener; added regression test for both-zero case

title: SocksServerHandle::shutdown ignores graceful shutdown channel, always aborts task
description:
Introduced by: Codex

crates/teamserver/src/sockets.rs:499-501 — The SocksServerHandle struct stores a oneshot::Sender<()> in its 'shutdown' field, and the spawned accept loop uses a tokio::select! to listen for this channel. However, the shutdown() method never sends on the channel:

    fn shutdown(&self) {
        self.task.abort();  // always aborts, never sends shutdown_tx
    }

The Drop impl does send on it, but also calls task.abort(). The net result is that graceful shutdown via the channel is never used — tasks are always abruptly aborted, which could leave the TCP listener in an uncleaned state.

Fix: have shutdown() send on the oneshot channel instead of (or before) aborting, so the accept loop can terminate cleanly.
close_reason: Already fixed in HEAD: SocksServerHandle::shutdown sends the graceful shutdown signal and is covered by a regression test.

title: Teamserver - REST API
description: HTTP REST API on the teamserver for automation and external tooling integration. OpenAPI spec, endpoints for agents (list/show/kill/interact), listeners (CRUD), loot/credentials, and jobs. Separate authentication from WebSocket (API keys or JWT). New feature beyond Havoc.
close_reason: Completed REST credentials and jobs endpoints with OpenAPI coverage, API tests, and queue snapshot support.

title: Implement screenshot, SOCKS5, and Kerberos commands
description: In crates/teamserver: COMMAND_SCREENSHOT (receive screenshot bytes, save to loot, notify operators), COMMAND_SOCKET/SOCKS5 proxy (relay TCP connections through the agent - requires async bidirectional streaming), COMMAND_KERBEROS (roasting, delegation attacks - parse and format results for operators).
close_reason: Implemented screenshot loot persistence, SOCKS5 relay handling, and Kerberos/socket command support

title: Teamserver - Listeners
description: C2 listener implementations: HTTP/HTTPS (profile-configured, custom URIs/headers/user-agents), SMB named pipe listener, and listener lifecycle management (create, start, stop, persist to DB). DNS listener planned as follow-on after HTTP and SMB.
close_reason: Completed listener lifecycle, persistence, HTTP/HTTPS, SMB, and verification.

title: DNS listener upload accepts chunks before agent is registered — unauthenticated upload buffering
description:
Introduced by: Claude Sonnet 4.6

teamserver/src/listeners.rs:1951-2000 — The DNS listener handle_upload() method buffers incoming chunks (up to DNS_MAX_PENDING_UPLOADS=1000 sessions, DNS_MAX_UPLOAD_CHUNKS=256 chunks each, ~39 bytes per chunk) before the assembled Demon packet is passed to process_demon_transport(). Rate limiting for DEMON_INIT is applied after assembly, not during chunk accumulation.

An unauthenticated source can:
1. Open 1000 concurrent agent_id slots simultaneously (with total=256 each).
2. Each slot holds up to 256×39 = ~10KB of chunk data in memory.
3. Total worst-case: 1000 × 10KB = ~10MB held in memory across 256,000 DNS queries.

While DNS_MAX_PENDING_UPLOADS limits the number of upload sessions, a single IP can saturate all 1000 slots because upload sessions are keyed by agent_id (u32), which can be freely chosen. There is no per-IP limit on how many upload sessions a single source can open.

Fix: Track per-IP upload session counts and enforce a per-source cap (e.g., 5-10 concurrent uploads per source IP) before accepting the first chunk.
close_reason: Added DNS_MAX_UPLOADS_PER_IP=10 cap; peer_ip stored in DnsPendingUpload; try_assemble_upload counts same-IP sessions and rejects if at limit; new test dns_upload_rejects_new_session_when_per_ip_limit_reached verifies the fix

title: test: add unit tests for audit::query_session_activity activity filter validation
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/audit.rs (query_session_activity, lines 407-460)
**Functions with partial tests**:
- `fn query_session_activity` (line 407) — the activity filter uses BTreeSet::retain to narrow down allowed action labels; the empty-set guard (line 425) returns early with an empty page when the requested activity filter matches nothing; this branch is tested for completely unknown activities but NOT for the case where a valid activity substring matches no entries

**Why this matters**: The session-activity endpoint is the main audit trail for operator sessions. If the empty-set guard fires incorrectly due to a mis-formatted action prefix (operator.{activity}), every filtered query silently returns 0 results — the operator audit UI appears empty without an error.

**Minimum test scenarios needed**:
1. Happy path: insert operator.connect entries; query with activity='connect'; verify only those entries appear.
2. Happy path: query with activity='chat' returns only operator.chat entries even when operator.connect also exists.
3. Error path: query with activity='nonexistent_action_xyz' triggers the empty-set guard and returns {total:0, items:[]}.
4. Edge case: query with no activity filter includes all five session event types in the results.

**Test location**: inline `#[cfg(test)]` in teamserver/src/audit.rs alongside existing query_session_activity tests

title: test: add integration test for build_router routing /api/v1 sub-paths through JSON not-found handler
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/app.rs
**Functions with partial tests**:
- `build_router()` (line 123) — 3 existing tests verify fallback returns empty 404, /api/v1 root returns 200, /havoc prefix is routed. None verify that a non-existent path *under* /api/v1 (e.g., `/api/v1/nonexistent`) goes through the JSON not-found handler in `api_routes` rather than the plain empty-body fallback

**Why this matters**: `api_routes` installs a `.fallback` handler that returns a JSON body `{"error": {"code": "not_found", ...}}`. But the nesting order in `build_router` determines which fallback wins. If the route nesting is misconfigured, API clients hitting unknown /api/v1 paths get an empty 404 body instead of a structured JSON error, breaking client error parsing.

**Minimum test scenarios needed**:
1. `GET /api/v1/nonexistent` via `build_router` → 404 with JSON body containing `error.code == "not_found"`.
2. `GET /api/v1` (root info endpoint, no auth required) still returns 200 OK (regression guard).
3. `GET /missing` (outside api/v1) still returns 404 with empty body.

**Test location**: inline `#[cfg(test)]` in teamserver/src/app.rs, extending the existing test module

title: test: format_net_group_descriptions with empty list, single row, and name-width padding
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/dispatch/network.rs
**Gap type**: existence / edge case

`format_net_group_descriptions` (used for both `DemonNetCommand::LocalGroup` and `DemonNetCommand::Group`) has no tests. The existing issue `red-cell-c2-upwv` covers `format_net_sessions`, `format_net_shares`, and `int_to_ipv4`, but this formatter was missed.

Key cases:
1. Empty input: must return an empty string (early return guard). If removed, the header line would be emitted for an empty group list.
2. Single group: verify column header + separator + one data row are all present.
3. Long group name: the `group_width` padding is computed from the max of 5 and the longest name. With a name shorter than 5 chars, padding should still be at least 5; verify the output columns align correctly.

**Suggested test approach**:
Call `format_net_group_descriptions` with `&[]` (assert empty string), a single `("Admins".to_owned(), "Local administrators".to_owned())` entry, and an entry with a 20-character group name, asserting the expected column layout in each case.

title: Common Crate (shared types, protocol, crypto)
description: Implement the shared Rust crate used by both the teamserver and client. Contains: Demon binary protocol types, AES-256-CBC crypto, JSON WebSocket message types for operator communication, TLS certificate generation, and HCL/YAOTL config parsing. This is a foundational dependency for all other crates.
close_reason: Shared common crate is implemented and passes workspace checks

title: protocol: process_path field always aliased to process_name in operator_agent_info
description:
Introduced by: Codex <noreply@openai.com>

teamserver/src/agent_events.rs — In the OperatorAgentInfo construction, process_path is set to agent.process_name.clone() (same as process_name). AgentRecord has no separate process_path field. Havoc clients display process_path as the full binary path (e.g., C:\Windows\System32\explorer.exe) in the agent table; they receive the short name instead.

Fix: add a process_path field to AgentRecord populated from DemonInit metadata, and populate OperatorAgentInfo::process_path from it.
close_reason: Added process_path field to AgentRecord populated from the full UTF-16 binary path in DemonInit/CheckIn metadata. Added DB migration, wired through database layer (INSERT/UPDATE/SELECT), fixed agent_events.rs to use agent.process_path instead of agent.process_name, updated all test fixtures, and added regression test.

title: consistency: DEFAULT_MAX_DOWNLOAD_BYTES defined twice with different types (u64 vs usize)
description:
Introduced by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

teamserver/src/listeners.rs:56 — const DEFAULT_MAX_DOWNLOAD_BYTES: u64 = 512 * 1024 * 1024
teamserver/src/dispatch.rs:38  — const DEFAULT_MAX_DOWNLOAD_BYTES: usize = 512 * 1024 * 1024

Same semantic constant, same value, but different types and two separate definitions. If these values ever diverge, a download accepted by the listener layer could be rejected by the dispatcher (or vice versa), silently breaking large-file transfers.

Fix: define the constant once in teamserver/src/lib.rs (or common/) with a single canonical type and import it in both files.
close_reason: Defined DEFAULT_MAX_DOWNLOAD_BYTES once in teamserver/src/lib.rs as u64; removed duplicate definitions from listeners.rs and dispatch.rs; updated call sites to use the canonical constant directly.

title: OperatorConfig derives Debug — plaintext password exposed in any debug log
description:
Introduced by: Claude Sonnet 4.6

common/src/config.rs:436-444 — OperatorConfig derives Debug with a plaintext password field:



The Profile struct embeds OperatorConfig via operators.users, and Profile itself derives Debug. Any call that formats a Profile or OperatorConfig with {:?} — including future tracing instrument spans, error messages, or panic messages — will emit operator plaintext passwords to logs or stderr.

Similarly, HttpListenerProxyConfig at config.rs:615 also derives Debug with an optional password field.

Fix: Replace #[derive(Debug)] with a manual impl that redacts the password field, or add #[debug(skip)] / #[sensitive] annotations. This matches the pattern already used for AgentEncryptionInfo in domain.rs:276.
close_reason: Replaced #[derive(Debug)] with manual impl fmt::Debug on OperatorConfig and HttpListenerProxyConfig, redacting password fields to prevent plaintext leakage into logs

title: agent_new_event duplicated between listeners.rs and dispatch.rs — HTTP/SMB/DNS agents lose pivot chain info
description:
Introduced by: Codex

Two separate private functions named agent_new_event exist:
- crates/teamserver/src/listeners.rs:907 — 3-arg version (listener_name, magic_value, agent)
  Uses AgentPivotsInfo::default() — always emits empty pivot chain.
- crates/teamserver/src/dispatch.rs:2450 — 4-arg version with real PivotInfo.

process_demon_transport (listeners.rs) calls the 3-arg version, so any agent that registers via the HTTP, SMB, or DNS listener will broadcast an AgentNew event with no pivot information, even if it is a pivot agent routed through the SMB listener.

This means the operator console will always show these agents as having no parent/children, regardless of actual topology.

Fix: move agent_new_event (and the helper operator_agent_info) into common/domain.rs or a shared listeners/dispatch helper, and have both callers use the shared function with real PivotInfo. The 3-arg variant in listeners.rs should be removed.
close_reason: Added regression coverage for shared AgentNew pivot metadata and verified pivot connect events preserve parent links.

title: test: all-zero IV in DemonInit is not covered by any test
description:
Introduced by: Codex <noreply@openai.com>

teamserver/src/demon.rs — There is a test verifying that an all-zero AES key is rejected, but no test documents or verifies the behavior for an all-zero AES IV. Given the security issue (all-zero IV not rejected), the desired behavior should be explicitly tested regardless of which direction the fix goes.

Fix: add a unit test for parse_init_agent with a valid key and all-zero IV, asserting it returns an error (once the validation is added per red-cell-c2-ude5).
close_reason: All-zero IV is rejected and tested via parse_rejects_plaintext_zero_iv_init (added in commit 6770ac9 alongside the fix)

title: Implement process commands (COMMAND_PROC)
description: In crates/teamserver: handle all COMMAND_PROC (0x1010) sub-commands: process list (ps), process kill, process inject, token impersonation for spawn. Parse binary subcommand and parameters. Serialize job for agent. Parse callback output and format for operator display.
close_reason: Implemented Havoc-compatible process tasking/callback handling for proc list/create/kill, shellcode inject, and token impersonation.

title: DNS listeners are implemented but cannot be used for payload generation
description:
Introduced by: Codex

crates/teamserver/src/listeners.rs:1401-1410 and 2035-2110 implement DNS listener startup and request handling, matching the project architecture goal of a DNS listener.

crates/teamserver/src/payload_builder.rs:598-601 rejects ListenerConfig::Dns with "DNS listeners are not supported for Demon payload builds".

That means the advertised DNS transport can be created and started on the teamserver, but the built-in payload generation path cannot emit a Demon configured to use it. The feature is therefore incomplete from an operator workflow perspective.

The fix should add DNS packing support to the payload builder or clearly gate the listener feature until payload generation exists.
close_reason: Gated DNS listeners at create/start time until Demon payload generation supports DNS transport, and preserved DNS protocol coverage with lower-level runtime tests.

title: Websocket tests flaky under full-suite parallel load — 5s frame timeout fires
description:
Introduced by: Claude Sonnet 4.6

teamserver/src/websocket.rs — read_operator_message() helper uses a 5-second tokio::time::timeout to receive the next WebSocket frame (line 4540). Under the full test suite run (686+ tests running in parallel), this timeout fires consistently for three tests:

  • websocket::tests::websocket_listener_commands_broadcast_and_persist_state
  • websocket::tests::websocket_listener_edit_records_audit_trail
  • websocket::tests::permission_denied_audit_recorded_when_analyst_sends_privileged_command

All three tests pass reliably when run in isolation (cargo test -p red-cell --lib -- websocket::tests). Failure is therefore due to resource contention under parallel test load, not logic errors.

The root cause is shared: the 5s hard-coded deadline does not account for OS scheduling delays and CPU saturation when hundreds of Tokio tasks are competing for threads.

Fix options:
  1. Increase the timeout in read_operator_message() to 15–30 seconds to survive full-suite load.
  2. Annotate the three slow tests with #[tokio::test(flavor = "current_thread")] to pin them to a single-threaded executor and avoid scheduling interference.
  3. Add #[serial_test::serial] (serial_test crate) to serialise the websocket integration tests that bind a port and exchange multiple frames.

Observed at: 2026-03-16 QA run — cargo test --workspace exit code 1, 3 failures, 683 passed.
close_reason: Increased read_operator_message test helper timeout from 5s to 30s to survive full-suite parallel load

title: Implement HTTP route for agent callbacks
description: In crates/teamserver: Axum route(s) for Demon agent HTTP callbacks. POST handler on configurable URIs (from listener profile). Read raw body bytes, validate minimum size, check magic bytes before decryption. Return 404 for invalid requests (operational security). Integrate with listener registry so only routes for active listeners accept traffic. Must handle both HTTP and HTTPS.
close_reason: Implemented HTTP(S) Demon callback validation with decoy 404 handling for malformed traffic.

title: WebSocket agent removals bypass structured audit logging
description:
Introduced by: Michel Klomp

crates/teamserver/src/websocket.rs:930-941 dispatches AgentRemove and crates/teamserver/src/websocket.rs:1179-1199 deletes the agent and broadcasts the event, but neither path records a success or failure audit entry. Agent tasking, listener lifecycle actions, and REST mutations are audited; permanent agent deletion over the operator WebSocket is not. This leaves a gap in the structured audit trail for one of the highest-impact destructive actions. The AgentRemove path should log success/failure with agent_id and operator context before mutating state.
close_reason: Added structured success and failure audit entries for WebSocket agent deletion, including operator and agent context.

title: client: note task sent with empty operator username
description:
Introduced by: Codex <noreply@openai.com>

In `render_note_editor` (crates/client/src/main.rs:862), `build_note_task` is called with `""` as the operator name:

    self.session_panel.pending_messages.push(build_note_task(&agent_id, &note, ""));

The AppState is already locked on lines 858-861 to call `update_agent_note`. The operator username is available as `state.operator_info.as_ref().map(|op| op.username.as_str())`. The empty string is silently forwarded in the `MessageHead.user` field, so the teamserver receives a note-update task attributed to an anonymous/blank user — breaking audit attribution for note operations.

Fix: read `operator_info.username` from the same lock used for `update_agent_note` and pass it to `build_note_task`.
close_reason: render_note_editor now forwards operator_info.username into build_note_task for note audit attribution

title: Implement HCL/YAOTL config parsing in common crate
description: Parse the existing Havoc .yaotl profile format (HCL-based) in Rust. Use the hcl-rs crate. Define strongly-typed Rust structs for the full profile schema: Teamserver (Host, Port, Build), Operators (username/password pairs), Demon defaults (Sleep, Jitter, Injection settings), Listener configs (HTTP, SMB, External). Write unit tests with the existing havoc.yaotl profile.
close_reason: Implemented typed YAOTL profile parsing in red-cell-common with Havoc fixture tests.

title: Implement HTTP/HTTPS listener (profile-configured)
description: In crates/teamserver: full HTTP/HTTPS C2 listener. Profile-configurable: custom URIs, HTTP headers (e.g. fake server headers), user-agent filtering, fake 404 for non-agent requests, custom response bodies. HTTPS with auto-generated or profile-specified TLS cert. Bind to configurable host:port. Integrate with Axum router. Support multiple simultaneous HTTP listeners on different ports.
close_reason: Implemented Axum-backed HTTP/HTTPS listener runtime with filtering, fake 404 handling, response customization, and TLS bootstrap

title: test: dispatch/util.rs basename and arch-label functions have no tests
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/dispatch/util.rs
**Gap type**: existence

Three pure utility functions in this file have zero tests:

1. `basename(path: &str) -> String` — strips the directory prefix from Windows or POSIX paths.
