Attribution: Codex (a849a947 — feat: capture agent loot and credentials)
close_reason: Fixed in dispatch/mod.rs:1352 — replaced unwrap_or_default() with unwrap_or(i64::MAX) so oversized credential content preserves maximum representable size instead of silently recording 0

title: test: login rate limiter lockout is never tested end-to-end
description:
**File**: teamserver/tests/e2e_operator_agent_session.rs
**Gap type**: existence / security

The `LoginRateLimiter` is instantiated in `TeamserverState` for every test server, but no integration test exercises the path where repeated failed logins actually trigger a lockout or a perceptible delay increase.  The existing `wrong_password_receives_error_and_connection_closes` test makes exactly one bad-password attempt; it never verifies that a second, third, or Nth failed attempt from the same IP is eventually rate-limited or blocked.  A regression that silently disables the limiter (e.g. always returning `allowed`) would pass every existing test.

**Suggested test approach**:
Fire N consecutive bad-password WebSocket login attempts from the same address, then assert either (a) the Nth attempt receives a different error response, or (b) the time between attempts grows measurably, or (c) additional attempts are outright refused — whatever the current limiter contract guarantees.

title: SocksServerHandle::shutdown ignores graceful shutdown channel, always aborts task
description:
Introduced by: Codex

crates/teamserver/src/sockets.rs:499-501 — The SocksServerHandle struct stores a oneshot::Sender<()> in its 'shutdown' field, and the spawned accept loop uses a tokio::select! to listen for this channel. However, the shutdown() method never sends on the channel:

    fn shutdown(&self) {
        self.task.abort();  // always aborts, never sends shutdown_tx
    }

The Drop impl does send on it, but also calls task.abort(). The net result is that graceful shutdown via the channel is never used — tasks are always abruptly aborted, which could leave the TCP listener in an uncleaned state.

Fix: have shutdown() send on the oneshot channel instead of (or before) aborting, so the accept loop can terminate cleanly.
close_reason: Already fixed in HEAD: SocksServerHandle::shutdown sends the graceful shutdown signal and is covered by a regression test.

title: test: DemonInit replay (same agent_id sent twice) behavior is unspecified and untested
description:
**File**: teamserver/tests/http_listener_pipeline.rs
**Gap type**: edge case / security

No test sends a valid DemonInit packet for an already-registered agent_id a second time. The handler could: (a) silently overwrite the existing agent's keys, (b) reject the re-init with an error, or (c) register a duplicate row. None of these behaviors are verified.

This matters for security: an adversary who can observe the agent_id (which appears in plaintext in the DemonEnvelope header) could send a new DemonInit with a key they control, effectively hijacking the agent session if the server accepts re-registration without validating that the old key is known.

**Suggested test approach**:
After a successful first INIT, send a second DemonInit with the same agent_id but different key/IV material. Assert: (1) the response code is 404 or another rejection code, (2) the agent registry still contains the original agent with the original key, and (3) no duplicate agent appears in `list_active()`.
close_reason: Added http_listener_pipeline_rejects_duplicate_init_preserves_original_key: verifies second DemonInit with same agent_id returns 404, original AES key/IV unchanged, no duplicate in list_active()

title: test: add unit tests for AgentRepository::set_ctr_block_offset error path
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/database.rs (AgentRepository, lines 425-441)
**Functions with no inline tests**:
- `fn set_ctr_block_offset` (line 425) — updates the AES-CTR keystream block offset for an agent; the error branch (rows_affected == 0 when agent not found) is untested; this is the same pattern as update_agent_ctr_block_offset (line 486) which also has the AgentNotFound guard

**Why this matters**: The CTR offset is the per-agent AES-256-CTR keystream position. If a stale or fabricated agent_id is silently accepted by `set_ctr_block_offset` (because rows_affected is not validated correctly), the teamserver will not notice the offset divergence. This causes every subsequent decryption to fail without a clear error, effectively bricking agent communication silently.

**Minimum test scenarios needed**:
1. Happy path: insert an agent, call set_ctr_block_offset with a non-zero offset, re-fetch the persisted offset and verify it matches.
2. Error path: call set_ctr_block_offset for an agent_id that was never inserted; verify TeamserverError::AgentNotFound is returned.
3. Edge case: call set_ctr_block_offset with offset=0 on a registered agent; verify the update is applied without error.

**Test location**: `teamserver/tests/database.rs` alongside existing agent_repository tests

title: Implement in-memory agent registry
description: In crates/teamserver: thread-safe in-memory agent registry using DashMap or tokio::sync::RwLock<HashMap>. Supports: insert new agent, get agent by ID, list all active agents, update agent fields, mark agent as dead, get/set per-agent AES key+IV, per-agent job queue (VecDeque<Job> behind a Mutex). Sync with DB on changes. Write unit tests for concurrency.
close_reason: Implemented a DB-backed in-memory agent registry with AES state, job queues, and concurrency tests

title: python: expect() in production path of parse_havocui_register_command_request
description:
Introduced by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 (Ubuntu-C2-dev02-claude)

client/src/python.rs:2065 in parse_havocui_register_command_request.

The call positional.last().cloned().expect("last checked above") is guarded by is_some_and() on the preceding line so it cannot actually panic. However it still violates the AGENTS.md rule: no expect() in production paths.

Fix: restructure to if let Some(cb) = positional.last().filter(|v| v.is_callable()) to call .last() only once and eliminate the expect().
close_reason: Replaced is_some_and + expect() with if let Some(cb) = positional.last().filter(|v| v.is_callable()) to eliminate expect() in production path

title: Implement Demon packet parser and AES handshake
description: In crates/teamserver: parse incoming Demon binary packets. Read 12-byte header (Size, Magic=0xDEADBEEF, AgentID). For new agents (DEMON_INIT): handle key exchange to establish per-agent AES-256-CBC key+IV, store in agent registry. For existing agents: look up key by AgentID, decrypt payload, return parsed command ID + request ID + data bytes. All using types from common crate.
close_reason: Implemented Havoc-compatible Demon packet parsing, init registration, and callback decryption.

title: transport tests: unused import ChatCode left after chat→EventLog refactor
description:
Introduced by: Ubuntu-C2-dev02-claude

`client/src/transport.rs:3187` has:
```rust
use red_cell_common::operator::{ChatCode, ChatUserInfo};
```
`ChatCode` is no longer used in the test module after the `chat_messages` → `EventLog` refactoring in commit `0541c6b`. The symbol is used in the main production code (line 2979) but the per-test `use` statement was not trimmed. `cargo test` emits a compiler warning:

  warning: unused import: `ChatCode`

This will become a -D warnings error if the test targets are ever compiled with clippy --tests.

Fix: remove `ChatCode` from the test-module import and keep only `ChatUserInfo`.
close_reason: Removed unused ChatCode from test-module import in client/src/transport.rs

title: Workspace fails to compile: api.rs references items only in git stash
description:
The working tree has `crates/teamserver/src/api.rs` (agent REST endpoints) that references:
- `AgentRegistry::list()` — method only in git stash (not committed, not in working tree)
- `crate::websocket::execute_agent_task` — function not yet pub(crate) in committed code
- `AgentCommandError` — enum not yet pub(crate) in committed code
- `ToSchema` impls for `AgentInfo`, `AgentEncryptionInfo`, `AgentTaskInfo` — derives only in stash

Running `cargo check --workspace` produces 21 compiler errors. The stash contains the prerequisite changes but has not been applied.

Fix: apply the stash (`git stash pop`) before committing the api.rs additions. All pieces need to land in the same commit (or the stash changes committed first).
close_reason: Resolved by commits ebfbbfe and dc4ae40 — work was committed and build passes cleanly.

title: test: add unit test for pivot::inner_demon_agent_id binary envelope parser
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/dispatch/pivot.rs
**Functions with no tests**:
- `fn inner_demon_agent_id` (line 265) — extracts agent_id from a raw Demon envelope byte slice by calling DemonEnvelope::from_bytes; used to identify the inner agent in pivot chains

**Why this matters**: This function is the entry point for pivot chain routing. An error here causes the teamserver to reject or misroute all pivoted agent traffic, breaking the entire SMB/TCP pivot subsystem. The `DemonProtocolError` error path (invalid magic, truncated header) needs a test to ensure the correct error propagates rather than a panic.

**Minimum test scenarios needed**:
1. Happy path: construct a minimal valid DemonEnvelope byte slice → assert returned agent_id matches
2. Error path: pass empty slice → assert DemonProtocolError returned (not panic)
3. Error path: pass bytes with wrong magic → assert DemonProtocolError

**Test location**: inline `#[cfg(test)]` in teamserver/src/dispatch/pivot.rs OR teamserver/tests/pivot_dispatch.rs
close_reason: Added 3 inline unit tests for inner_demon_agent_id: happy path, empty-slice error, wrong-magic error

title: completeness: DNS listener has domain model and config parsing but no runtime implementation
description:
Introduced by: Codex <noreply@openai.com>

teamserver/src/listeners.rs — ListenerConfig::Dns is accepted by profile parsing (common/src/domain.rs) and passes profile validation, but the ListenerManager start/stop/restart dispatch has no handler for the Dns variant. Attempting to start a DNS listener will either reach an unimplemented branch or silently fail.

AGENTS.md lists DNS listener as a planned feature, but there is no open tracking issue and no stub implementation that clearly marks the DNS listener as not-yet-implemented.

Fix: add an explicit match arm for ListenerConfig::Dns in ListenerManager that returns Err(ListenerError::NotImplemented) with a clear message, and add a feature tracking issue.
close_reason: DNS listener already has a full runtime implementation via spawn_dns_listener_runtime. The ListenerManager::spawn_listener_runtime dispatch has a complete ListenerConfig::Dns arm. All four CI checks pass. Issue was resolved by prior commits (fix: allow DNS listener management flows, fix: gate unsupported dns listener starts).

title: test: Profile::parse and from_file error paths untested
description:
**File**: common/src/config.rs
**Gap type**: error path

`Profile::parse`, `Profile::from_reader`, and `Profile::from_file` all return `Result<Self, ProfileError>` but only the success paths are tested. The following error conditions have no coverage:

1. **Malformed HCL** — `Profile::parse("{completely invalid hcl]"))` should return `ProfileError`. The deserialization error variant is never exercised.
2. **Nonexistent file** — `Profile::from_file("/nonexistent/path.yaotl")` should return a `ProfileError::Io` (or equivalent). The error-handling branch in `from_file` is never exercised.
3. **Missing required top-level block** — input that is valid HCL but omits the required `Teamserver` or `Operators` block should return a deserialisation error, not silently succeed with default values.

Without error-path tests, a refactor that accidentally changes which error variant is returned, or starts `unwrap`ing instead of propagating, would not be caught.

**Suggested test approach**:
Add three tests: (1) pass a syntactically invalid HCL string to `Profile::parse` and assert `is_err()`; (2) call `Profile::from_file` with a path that does not exist and assert the error; (3) pass valid HCL that is missing the `Teamserver` block and assert deserialisation fails.
close_reason: Added three tests covering ProfileError::Parse (malformed HCL), ProfileError::Read (nonexistent file), and deserialization failure (missing Teamserver block)

title: Implement operator audit trail
description: In crates/teamserver: persist all operator actions to ts_audit_log table. Log: operator username, timestamp, action type, agent ID (if applicable), command, parameters, result status. Audit every: agent task, listener create/start/stop, operator connect/disconnect, login attempt (success and failure), loot access. Expose via REST GET /audit with filtering/pagination.
close_reason: Implemented structured operator audit logging with REST filtering and pagination.

title: Implement listener lifecycle management
description: In crates/teamserver: listener manager for creating, starting, stopping, and deleting listeners. ListenerManager struct holds active listener handles (tokio::task::JoinHandle). Methods: create(config), start(name), stop(name), delete(name), list(). Persist state to DB. Handle errors (port already in use, etc). Expose to WebSocket command handlers and REST API.
close_reason: Implemented persisted listener lifecycle management with REST and WebSocket integration

title: Fix operator-facing typos in process/injection/token callback messages
description:
Multiple user-visible strings in crates/teamserver/src/dispatch.rs contain typos:
- Line 398: 'Successful killed process: {pid}' → 'Successfully killed process: {pid}'
- Line 431: 'Successful injected shellcode' → 'Successfully injected shellcode'
- Line 477: 'Successful impersonated {user}' → 'Successfully impersonated {user}'
- Line 479: 'Failed to impersonat {user}' (missing 'e') → 'Failed to impersonate {user}'
These strings appear in the operator UI so correct grammar matters. The test assertions on lines 1517 and 1534 encode the typos and must be updated alongside the fix.
close_reason: Fixed all four typos in dispatch.rs callback messages (Successful→Successfully, impersonat→impersonate) and updated matching test assertions

title: Testing Infrastructure
description: Full test suite: unit tests for common crate (protocol parsing, crypto, config), unit tests for teamserver core, integration tests that spin up a real teamserver with a mock Demon agent checking in, and E2E tests with mock agent plus mock operator client connecting. Tests run in CI.
close_reason: Testing infrastructure complete: CI workflow restored, crypto round-trip tests confirmed, listener lifecycle tests (13), SOCKS5 relay integration tests (12), SMB listener integration tests (5)

title: transport: AppState::tls_failure not cleared on successful connect
description:
Introduced by: Ubuntu-C2-dev02-claude (feat(client): TLS error UX, commit 1e8d8c6)

AppState::tls_failure is written in run_connection_manager when a TLS cert error is detected but is never cleared when a subsequent connection attempt succeeds.

In main.rs the failure is propagated back to LoginState whenever an error_message is present:
  let tls_failure = snapshot.tls_failure.clone();
  ...
  login_state.set_tls_failure(failure);

Scenario that triggers it:
1. Connection fails with TLS cert error → AppState::tls_failure set
2. User trusts cert → reconnects successfully → AppState::tls_failure still set
3. Server shuts down or session is disrupted (non-TLS reason)
4. Client returns to Login phase — stale tls_failure is copied to LoginState
5. Login UI shows the TLS certificate error panel even though the new disconnect had nothing to do with TLS

Fix: clear AppState::tls_failure when the WebSocket connection is established (e.g., at the top of the Ok(socket) branch in run_connection_manager, before entering the send/recv loops).
close_reason: Clear tls_failure at top of Ok(socket) branch in run_connection_manager so stale TLS error is not shown after a successful reconnect

title: test: concurrent encrypt_for_agent calls on same agent may race on CTR counter
description:
**File**: teamserver/tests/ctr_offset_persistence.rs
**Gap type**: edge case / concurrency / crypto

All existing CTR-offset tests call `encrypt_for_agent` sequentially for one agent at a time.  There is no test that fires two or more concurrent `encrypt_for_agent` calls for the *same* agent and verifies:
1. No two calls use the same block offset (two-time-pad collision).
2. The persisted CTR offset reflects the total blocks consumed by all calls combined.

A race condition in the counter increment (read-modify-write without holding the lock for the DB write) could cause two calls to encrypt at the same offset, which silently produces decryptable but wrong ciphertext — the worst kind of crypto failure.

**Suggested test approach**:
Spawn N concurrent tasks each calling `registry.encrypt_for_agent(agent_id, payload)` and collect all returned ciphertexts; assert all are pairwise distinct and that the final `ctr_offset` equals the sum of blocks consumed by all N calls.

title: payload: service EXE and staged shellcode payload templates
description: payload_builder.rs currently supports EXE, DLL, ReflectiveDLL, Shellcode. Add: Windows Service EXE template (ServiceMain entry point, SCM registration), staged shellcode (small stager that downloads and executes full payload from C2), raw position-independent shellcode for injection. Add corresponding template files under a payloads/templates/ directory following the existing Demon build system.
close_reason: Added StagedShellcode (HTTP stager EXE + payloads/templates/MainStager.c), RawShellcode (DllLdr.x64.bin loader variant, x64-only), and payloads/templates/MainSvc.c reference template. Updated PayloadBuilderInner with dllldr_x64_template and stager_template fields, added build_staged_payload/compile_stager/build_stager_defines, 15 new tests.

title: Implement REST API framework and OpenAPI spec
description: In crates/teamserver: REST API infrastructure. Mount /api/v1/ routes on the Axum router. API key authentication middleware (keys configurable in profile). Define OpenAPI spec (using utoipa crate). Rate limiting. JSON error responses. Versioning strategy. This lays the groundwork for all REST endpoint implementations.
close_reason: Implemented the versioned REST API framework with API-key auth, rate limiting, OpenAPI docs, and JSON errors.

title: Implement WebSocket command handlers (listener management)
description: In crates/teamserver: handle operator commands over WebSocket for listener management. ListenerNew: validate config, create listener, persist to DB, broadcast ListenerNew event. ListenerStart/Stop: lifecycle management. ListenerDelete. All responses broadcast to all connected operators so multi-operator sessions stay in sync.
close_reason: Completed listener WebSocket lifecycle handling with multi-operator sync coverage

title: Implement REST endpoints: loot, audit log, and operators
description: In crates/teamserver: REST endpoints for: GET /loot (list credentials/files/screenshots with filtering), GET /loot/:id (download specific loot item), GET /audit (operator activity log with pagination and filtering by operator/agent/time), GET /operators (list connected operators - Admin only), POST /operators (create new operator - Admin only).
close_reason: Implemented REST loot listing/download, admin operator endpoints, and audit time filtering.

title: agent_new_event duplicated between listeners.rs and dispatch.rs — HTTP/SMB/DNS agents lose pivot chain info
description:
Introduced by: Codex

Two separate private functions named agent_new_event exist:
- crates/teamserver/src/listeners.rs:907 — 3-arg version (listener_name, magic_value, agent)
  Uses AgentPivotsInfo::default() — always emits empty pivot chain.
- crates/teamserver/src/dispatch.rs:2450 — 4-arg version with real PivotInfo.

process_demon_transport (listeners.rs) calls the 3-arg version, so any agent that registers via the HTTP, SMB, or DNS listener will broadcast an AgentNew event with no pivot information, even if it is a pivot agent routed through the SMB listener.

This means the operator console will always show these agents as having no parent/children, regardless of actual topology.

Fix: move agent_new_event (and the helper operator_agent_info) into common/domain.rs or a shared listeners/dispatch helper, and have both callers use the shared function with real PivotInfo. The 3-arg variant in listeners.rs should be removed.
close_reason: Added regression coverage for shared AgentNew pivot metadata and verified pivot connect events preserve parent links.

title: Initialize Cargo workspace
description: Create the Cargo workspace root Cargo.toml at the repo root. Set up the workspace members: crates/teamserver, crates/client, crates/common. Create placeholder lib.rs/main.rs for each crate. Update .gitignore for Rust build artifacts (target/). Set up rustfmt.toml and clippy.toml with project-wide lint settings. Rust edition 2024.
close_reason: Initialized the Cargo workspace with placeholder crates and shared lint configuration.

title: Duplicate SHA3-256 password hash implementation in auth.rs vs common/crypto.rs
description:
Introduced by: Codex <noreply@openai.com> (feat: implement operator authentication)

crates/teamserver/src/auth.rs:277-286 — `auth::hash_password()`
crates/common/src/crypto.rs:134-145 — `crypto::hash_password_sha3()`

Both functions compute SHA3-256 of a password and return a lowercase hex string. Both produce identical output (verified by the same test vector: `hash('password1234') == '2f7d3e77...')`). The `auth` module uses its own local implementation instead of the common crate's public API.

This violates the DRY principle and creates a maintenance hazard: if one implementation is changed (e.g., to add a salt, PBKDF2 rounds, or encoding change), the other silently stays diverged. Since both functions produce identical output today, no bug exists yet, but this is an architecture drift from the principle that `crates/common` owns shared crypto primitives.

Fix: delete `auth::hash_password` and replace all call sites with `red_cell_common::crypto::hash_password_sha3`.
close_reason: Removed the duplicate teamserver SHA3-256 helper and switched auth call sites to red_cell_common::crypto::hash_password_sha3.

title: test: format_ticket_flags bit-field output for zero, single-bit, and multi-bit combinations
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/dispatch/kerberos.rs
**Gap type**: edge case / quality

`format_ticket_flags(flags: u32)` extracts 16 named flags from bits 16–31 and appends a hex representation. The function is displayed directly in operator output for every Kerberos ticket in `klist`. No unit tests exist for it.

Three cases are unverified:
1. All-zero flags — should produce " (0x0)" with no flag names.
2. A single known flag bit set (e.g., bit 16 = "name_canonicalize") — should include just that name plus hex.
3. Multiple flags set simultaneously — the names must appear in the correct order with space separation.

If the bit-position array were shifted by one index during a maintenance change, the flag names displayed would be off by one, silently producing incorrect Kerberos ticket information for every klist operation.

**Suggested test approach**:
assert!(format_ticket_flags(0).contains("(0x0)")); assert!(format_ticket_flags(1 << 16).contains("name_canonicalize")); assert the hex suffix is always present regardless of which flags are set.

title: test: decode_working_hours handles zero, non-zero, and sign-bit-set inputs correctly
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/dispatch/checkin.rs
**Gap type**: edge case

`decode_working_hours` maps a raw `u32` protocol bitmask to `Option<i32>`:
- `0` → `None` (working hours disabled)
- any non-zero value → `Some(i32::from_be_bytes(raw.to_be_bytes()))`

The function is called from both `parse_checkin_metadata` (checkin.rs) and `output.rs` (DEMON_INFO callback), and its output controls when the agent will execute tasks. There are no unit tests at all.

Key cases to verify:
- `0u32` → `None` (disabled, must not be treated as active bitmask)
- `0b101010u32` → `Some(42i32)` (representative non-zero value from integration tests)
- `0x8000_0000u32` (high bit set) → sign bit should be preserved in the `i32` conversion without a panic or wrap

**Suggested test approach**:
Add a small `#[cfg(test)]` module in checkin.rs and test all three cases with `assert_eq!(decode_working_hours(input), expected)`.

title: test: no test for CTR offset persistence and recovery after simulated teamserver restart
description:
Introduced by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

teamserver/src/agents.rs — AgentRegistry::load() reloads per-agent CTR offsets from SQLite.
teamserver/src/database.rs — update_agent_ctr_block_offset() persists the offset.

There is no test that:
1. Registers an agent, sends N encrypted messages (advancing the CTR offset)
2. Drops the in-memory registry and reloads it from SQLite
3. Verifies that the re-encrypted messages continue from the correct offset (not reset to 0)

Without this test, a regression in the persist-before-mutate ordering or the reload path would produce a CTR keystream collision (two-time-pad) after every restart, silently re-using keystream material.

Fix: add an integration test in teamserver/tests/ that exercises the full save-reload-encrypt cycle and asserts block offset continuity across the simulated restart. This directly tests the fix for red-cell-c2-3qbl.
close_reason: Added integration test in teamserver/tests/ctr_offset_persistence.rs exercising the full save-reload-encrypt cycle across a simulated registry restart (3 test cases: nominal multi-message offset, zero offset round-trip, exact block boundary)

title: test: evict_oldest_windows with target_size=0 and with empty map edge cases are untested
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/rate_limiter.rs
**Gap type**: edge case

`evict_oldest_windows` has a short-circuit guard: `if windows.len() <= target_size { return; }`. Two boundary cases are not tested:

1. **Empty map with target_size=0**: `windows.len() == 0 <= 0` triggers the early return. If the guard accidentally used `<` instead of `<=`, this case would compute `to_remove = 0 - 0 = 0` (no underflow due to usize), so the bug would be masked. A dedicated test asserting the map is unchanged confirms the guard fires.

2. **target_size=0 with a non-empty map**: Should evict all entries. Not tested. This is a legitimate use case if a site wants to completely flush its rate-limiter windows.

3. **Ties in window_start**: When multiple entries share the same `window_start`, the sort is non-deterministic. A test asserting that at least the correct *count* is evicted (not caring about which of the tied entries survive) would document the contract.

**Suggested test approach**:
Add three tests: empty map + target_size=0 (no-op), non-empty map + target_size=0 (all evicted), and multiple entries with identical window_start where only count is asserted.

title: test: read_socks_connect_request unsupported command and unknown address type are untested
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/sockets.rs
**Gap type**: error path

`read_socks_connect_request` handles several rejection cases that have no direct unit tests:
1. SOCKS command != CONNECT (e.g. BIND=2, UDP ASSOCIATE=3) — must send `SOCKS_REPLY_COMMAND_NOT_SUPPORTED` and return `Unsupported` error
2. Unknown address type (atyp not in {1,3,4}) — must return `InvalidData` error
3. IPv6 address type (SOCKS_ATYP_IPV6=4) reads 16 bytes — never tested

Without these tests a refactoring could accidentally remove the BIND rejection guard or change the byte layout, silently breaking protocol compliance with no failing test to catch it.

**Suggested test approach**:
Use the loopback TCP pair helper to write crafted byte sequences (wrong command byte, unknown atyp, IPv6 address), call `read_socks_connect_request` on the stream, and assert the returned error kind and that the correct refusal reply bytes were sent on the wire.
close_reason: All three missing test cases already implemented: rejects non-CONNECT command with SOCKS_REPLY_COMMAND_NOT_SUPPORTED and Unsupported error, rejects unknown atyp with InvalidData error, accepts IPv6 address type reading 16 bytes correctly. All tests pass.

title: test: RBAC enforcement on WebSocket message types has no integration test
description:
Introduced by: Codex <noreply@openai.com>

teamserver/src/rbac.rs — The RBAC system correctly defines role permissions (Analyst cannot TaskAgents, ManageListeners, etc.), but there are no integration tests that send a restricted-role message over a WebSocket connection authenticated as Analyst and verify it is rejected.

The unit tests in rbac.rs cover role definitions and permission lookups, but do not test that the WebSocket handler actually enforces RBAC at the message routing layer.

Fix: add integration tests in e2e_operator_agent_session.rs or a new test file that: (1) authenticate as Analyst role, (2) send a TaskAgents-category message, (3) verify the server returns an authorization-denied response and does not execute the task.
close_reason: Added four integration tests in e2e_operator_agent_session.rs: analyst_cannot_send_agent_task_message, analyst_cannot_create_listener, operator_cannot_send_admin_message, and analyst_can_send_chat_message_without_disconnection. These test the WebSocket handler's RBAC enforcement at the message routing layer.

title: test: filesystem_subcommand rejects unknown subcommand strings without a test
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/websocket.rs
**Gap type**: error path

`filesystem_subcommand` parses a string (either numeric or named) into a `DemonFilesystemCommand` variant and returns `AgentCommandError::UnsupportedFilesystemSubcommand` for unrecognized strings. This error path is not tested.

Without a test, a refactor that accidentally removes the catch-all arm, or a string-matching bug (e.g. case sensitivity, typo), would produce no failing test — the operator client would send an unrecognised filesystem command and the agent task would fail silently.

**Suggested test approach**:
Call `build_job` with `command_id` set to the numeric value of `CommandFs` and `sub_command` set to an unknown string like `"cat_dog"`, and assert the result is `Err(AgentCommandError::UnsupportedFilesystemSubcommand { .. })`.
close_reason: Added test asserting build_job returns UnsupportedFilesystemSubcommand for unknown sub_command string 'cat_dog' with CommandFs command_id

title: test: add unit tests for ConnectionStatus label/color/detail/placeholders
description:
**File**: `client/src/transport.rs`
**Functions with no tests**:
- `ConnectionStatus::label` (line 203) — returns a display string for each connection state variant
- `ConnectionStatus::color` (line 213) — returns an egui::Color32 for each state (grey/yellow/green/red)
- `ConnectionStatus::detail` (line 222) — returns `Some(message)` for Retrying/Error, `None` for others
- `ConnectionStatus::placeholders` (line 193) — returns a fixed array of all five status variants used for UI preview

**Why this matters**: If a new variant is added to `ConnectionStatus` without updating these match arms, the omission is silent — no exhaustiveness check catches a wrong `color()` or an incorrect `detail()` return. A typo in `label()` would silently show wrong text in the operator UI.

**Minimum test scenarios needed**:
1. Happy path: `ConnectionStatus::Connected.label()` == "Connected", `ConnectionStatus::Disconnected.label()` == "Disconnected"
2. Detail: `ConnectionStatus::Retrying("x".to_owned()).detail()` == `Some("x")`, `Connected.detail()` == `None`
3. Placeholders: `ConnectionStatus::placeholders()` has length 5 and contains all variants
4. Color: `Connected.color() != Disconnected.color()` (colors are distinct per state group)

**Test location**: inline `#[cfg(test)]` in `client/src/transport.rs`

title: test: parse_checkin_metadata weak-key validation is only integration-tested, no isolated unit test for validate_checkin_transport_material
description:
File: teamserver/src/dispatch/checkin.rs
Gap type: error path / isolation

The validate_checkin_transport_material function rejects all-zero AES keys and IVs from COMMAND_CHECKIN payloads. It is covered indirectly via two large integration tests in dispatch/mod.rs (builtin_checkin_handler_rejects_all_zero_rotated_aes_key_without_mutating_state and the IV equivalent), but these tests exercise the full dispatch chain including AgentRegistry I/O and event broadcasting.

There is no isolated unit test for validate_checkin_transport_material itself. This matters because:
1. The function has two independent rejection conditions (weak key, weak IV) and neither is tested in isolation from the rest of the checkin flow.
2. A future refactor that moves or inlines this validation would need to trace through the heavy integration test to verify the logic still runs.
3. Partially-zeroed keys (e.g., a key with only the last byte non-zero) are NOT rejected by is_weak_aes_key but may be cryptographically weak -- this boundary is untested.

Suggested test approach:
Add a #[cfg(test)] block in checkin.rs with direct calls to validate_checkin_transport_material using all-zero key, all-zero IV, and a valid key/IV pair, asserting Ok(()) or Err as appropriate.

title: Implement agent checkin handler (DEMON_INIT)
description: In crates/teamserver: handle DEMON_INIT (command 99) - the initial agent registration. Parse agent metadata from checkin payload: hostname, username, domain, IPs, PID, PPID, process name, arch, OS version, elevation status, sleep/jitter config. Create AgentInfo, store in registry and DB, broadcast AgentNew event to all operators. Return success response to agent.
close_reason: Handled DEMON_INIT callbacks by persisting agents, returning init acks, and broadcasting AgentNew events.

title: test: handle_config_callback — only WorkingHours key is tested; 11 other keys have zero coverage
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/dispatch/output.rs
**Gap type**: existence / edge case

`handle_config_callback` dispatches over all 12 `DemonConfigKey` variants. Two tests exist (`builtin_config_and_mem_file_handlers` and `builtin_config_handler_preserves_high_bit_working_hours`), both covering only `WorkingHours`. The following variants are completely untested:

| Key | Why it matters |
|-----|---------------|
| `MemoryAlloc` / `MemoryExecute` | Reads a u32 and formats a confirmation string — format regression invisible |
| `InjectSpawn64` / `InjectSpawn32` | Reads a UTF-16 path — incorrect encoding handling or truncated path would be silent |
| `KillDate` with raw=0 | Clears kill_date on the agent; the "KillDate was disabled" code path is never exercised |
| `KillDate` with raw≠0 | Sets kill_date; a timestamp parse error would be reported to operator differently than expected |
| `ImplantSpfThreadStart` / `InjectSpoofAddr` | Reads two strings (module + symbol) separated — wrong separator or order would be invisible |
| `ImplantCoffeeVeh` / `ImplantCoffeeThreaded` / `ImplantVerbose` | Reads a bool and formats via `bool_string` — success/failure text never validated |
| `ImplantSleepTechnique` / `InjectTechnique` | Reads a u32; format string untested |
| **Unknown key value** | Should propagate `InvalidCallbackPayload` — not tested |

**Suggested test approach**:
Add a parameterized test or individual tests dispatching a CommandConfig payload for each key. For keys that mutate agent state (KillDate, WorkingHours), assert the registry reflects the update. For message-only keys, assert the broadcast event contains the expected confirmation text.

title: test: assert Display output format for ProfileError and ProfileValidationError
description:
**File**: common/src/config.rs
**Functions with no tests**:
- `ProfileError::Parse` (line 328) — Display: "failed to parse YAOTL profile: {0}" — format string untested
- `ProfileError::Read` (line 333) — Display: "failed to read YAOTL profile from {path}: {source}" — path interpolation untested
- `ProfileValidationError` (line 344) — Display: "profile validation failed: {errors joined with "; "}" — join format and separator untested

**Why this matters**: Error messages are surfaced directly to operators in the teamserver startup path. A broken Display implementation (e.g. wrong separator, missing path field) would produce confusing output when a config file is malformed or missing, making operator on-call diagnosis harder. The `thiserror` macro makes these easy to break accidentally with attribute edits.

**Minimum test scenarios needed**:
1. `ProfileError::Parse` — parse garbage input, call `.to_string()`, assert it starts with "failed to parse YAOTL profile:"
2. `ProfileError::Read` — call `Profile::from_file` on a missing path, call `.to_string()`, assert it contains the path string
3. `ProfileValidationError` with multiple errors — call `.to_string()`, assert errors are separated by "; " and prefixed with "profile validation failed: "

**Test location**: inline `#[cfg(test)]` in `common/src/config.rs`
close_reason: Added three inline tests: profile_error_parse_display_format, profile_error_read_display_contains_path, profile_validation_error_display_join_format — all assertions verified against exact Display strings

title: test: deserialize_agent_id rejects numeric agent ID that does not fit in u32
description:
**File**: common/src/domain.rs
**Gap type**: error path

The `deserialize_agent_id` helper has a branch for JSON numbers:
```rust
StringOrU64::Number(value) => u32::try_from(value).map_err(|_| {
    de::Error::custom(format!("agent identifier `{value}` does not fit in u32"))
}),
```
This overflow/truncation path is completely untested. A JSON payload with `"AgentID": 4294967296` (2^32) should produce a deserialization error, but there is currently no test that exercises this.

If this check were accidentally removed or changed to a silent truncation (`value as u32`), no test would catch it. A misidentified agent could receive commands intended for a different agent.

**Suggested test approach**:
Build an otherwise-valid `AgentRecord` JSON payload with `"AgentID": 4294967296` (or `"AgentID": 18446744073709551615` for u64::MAX) and assert that `serde_json::from_value::<AgentRecord>` returns an error containing `"does not fit in u32"`.
close_reason: Added test covering the numeric overflow path in deserialize_agent_id; verifies 2^32 and u64::MAX both produce 'does not fit in u32' error

title: test: AgentEncryptionInfo rejects invalid base64 for AES key and IV fields
description:
**File**: common/src/domain.rs
**Gap type**: error path

The `AgentEncryptionInfo` struct uses custom serde helpers `deserialize_base64_to_zeroizing_bytes` for both `AESKey` and `AESIv` fields. These fields carry the per-agent session keys loaded from the database and from DEMON_INIT packets. There is no test that supplies corrupt or non-base64 bytes and verifies a clean deserialization error.

Without this test, a future regression in the deserializer (or a storage corruption scenario that puts garbage bytes in the DB) could silently produce wrong key material — or worse, panic — instead of returning an actionable error that the caller can handle or log.

The existing `agent_info_deserializes_mixed_havoc_shapes` test only exercises the happy path with valid base64 strings.

**Suggested test approach**:
Construct a JSON payload with `AESKey: "%%%not-base64%%%"` and assert that `serde_json::from_value::<AgentRecord>(payload)` returns an error whose message contains the word 'base64' or 'invalid'. Repeat for `AESIv`. Also test an empty string (`""`) for each to verify it is accepted as empty bytes (or rejected if that is the intended contract).

title: windows_version_label and basename duplicated between demon.rs and dispatch/checkin.rs
description: Two helper functions are duplicated between teamserver/src/demon.rs and teamserver/src/dispatch/checkin.rs: (1) windows_version_label / checkin_windows_version_label — near-identical match tables mapping (build, minor, major) to Windows version strings; (2) basename — strip directory prefix from a Windows path. Both helpers belong in red_cell_common or a shared dispatch utility module to avoid drift. Duplication was introduced when dispatch.rs was split into per-command-family modules in commit b6211d9 (Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6).
close_reason: Moved windows_version_label, basename, process_arch_label, and windows_arch_label into teamserver::dispatch::util; removed duplicates from demon.rs and dispatch/checkin.rs

title: maintainability: dispatch.rs is ~9 800 lines — split into per-command-family modules
description:
Introduced by: Claude Sonnet 4.6

teamserver/src/dispatch.rs (9 837 lines)

dispatch.rs is by far the largest file in the codebase (~9 800 lines vs the next-largest at ~6 200). It contains handlers for every Demon command family (filesystem, process, token, network, socket, kerberos, screenshot, transfer, inline-execute, pivot, etc.) plus the dispatcher infrastructure, download tracker, loot credential capture, and all handler tests.

This creates several practical problems:
- A single merge conflict anywhere in the file blocks work on unrelated handlers
- Reviews are impractical — a 9 000-line diff is impossible to reason about
- Test isolation is nonexistent: a regression in the filesystem handler can mask a socket handler bug
- Adding a new command handler requires navigating thousands of lines of unrelated code

Fix: split into a dispatch/ directory with a mod.rs for the infrastructure (CommandDispatcher, DownloadTracker, LootContext) and one file per command family (e.g., dispatch/filesystem.rs, dispatch/process.rs, dispatch/token.rs, dispatch/socket.rs). The pub API surface of dispatch.rs should remain unchanged.
close_reason: Split 10k-line dispatch.rs into dispatch/ directory with 12 per-command-family modules. Public API unchanged, all 627 tests pass.

title: test: havoc_compatibility test silently skips and returns Ok() when Havoc source is absent
description:
Introduced by: Codex <noreply@openai.com>

teamserver/tests/havoc_compatibility.rs — havoc_compatibility_skip_reason() returns Some(reason) and all tests return Ok(()) when RED_CELL_HAVOC_TEAMSERVER_DIR is unset. CI environments without the Havoc source tree pass all compatibility tests trivially with zero coverage of the Demon protocol.

Protocol regressions — wrong byte order, wrong command IDs, wrong encryption parameters — would go undetected in CI.

Fix: convert the skip to a test::ignore annotation with a clear skip message, or gate the havoc_compatibility tests behind a Cargo feature flag that must be explicitly enabled. At minimum, emit a cargo test warning (not Ok) when the tests are skipped.
close_reason: Gated havoc_compatibility.rs behind #\![cfg(feature = "havoc-compat")]. Without the feature tests are excluded entirely (can't silently pass). With the feature enabled but Go missing the test panics with a clear diagnostic. Feature documented with run instructions in Cargo.toml.

title: tests: no wiring tests for emit_agent_registered (listeners.rs) and emit_task_created (websocket.rs) plugin event dispatch
description:
Introduced by: Ubuntu-C2-dev01-claude (feat(plugins) commit e4ac5d1)

Unit tests were added for the PluginRuntime emit_* methods directly (emit_agent_registered_invokes_registered_callbacks, emit_task_created_invokes_registered_callbacks), but no integration/wiring tests verify that the plugin events fire when the actual code paths are exercised:

1. teamserver/src/listeners.rs:1284 — emit_agent_registered is called inside process_demon_transport after a DemonInit succeeds. No test sends a DemonInit and asserts the registered callback fires.

2. teamserver/src/websocket.rs:1247 — emit_task_created is called inside execute_agent_task. No test calls execute_agent_task and asserts the task_created callback fires.

Without wiring tests a future refactor could silently disconnect the emit calls and no test would catch it.
close_reason: Added two wiring tests: process_demon_transport_fires_plugin_agent_registered_event in listeners.rs and execute_agent_task_fires_plugin_task_created_event in websocket.rs. Also added pub(crate) test helpers (swap_active, register_callback_for_test, install_api_module_for_test) and a shared PLUGIN_RUNTIME_TEST_MUTEX to ensure cross-module test serialisation.

title: test: handle_sleep_callback agent-not-found error path is untested
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/dispatch/output.rs
**Gap type**: error path

`handle_sleep_callback` calls `registry.get(agent_id).await.ok_or(TeamserverError::AgentNotFound)` before mutating the agent. The happy path (agent exists) is tested by `builtin_sleep_ppid_and_assembly_handlers_update_state_and_broadcast`, but the error path — dispatching a CommandSleep callback for an agent ID that is not registered — is never exercised.

This matters because:
- If the error-propagation path is broken the handler could panic or silently succeed instead of returning an error to the dispatcher.
- The agent-not-found error is the standard sentinel that distinguishes a race condition (agent died mid-flight) from a protocol bug; operators rely on correct error propagation for debugging.

**Suggested test approach**:
Dispatch a CommandSleep payload to a dispatcher whose registry has no agents, and assert that `dispatch()` returns `Err(CommandDispatchError::TeamserverError { .. })` containing the agent-not-found variant. Also confirm that no AgentResponse event was broadcast.

title: transport.rs: TLS certificate verification completely bypassed
description:
Introduced by: Codex

In `crates/client/src/transport.rs`, the `build_insecure_tls_connector` function uses a `DangerousCertificateVerifier` that accepts any TLS certificate without any validation (line ~817-828). The `verify_server_cert` implementation always returns `Ok(ServerCertVerified::assertion())` unconditionally.

There is no alternative path for operators to provide a CA certificate or pin the server certificate. This leaves operator credentials (username + SHA3-256 hashed password) and all C2 traffic vulnerable to man-in-the-middle attacks on untrusted networks.

Expected fix: support at minimum one of:
1. Certificate pinning (operator stores a fingerprint of the teamserver cert on first connection)
2. Custom CA certificate path in the client config
3. `--accept-invalid-cert` flag that must be explicitly opted into rather than being the only option
close_reason: Replaced unconditional TLS bypass with TlsVerification enum: CertificateAuthority (default), CustomCa, Fingerprint pinning, and explicit DangerousSkipVerify. Added CLI flags --accept-invalid-certs, --ca-cert, --cert-fingerprint with config file fallbacks. 10 new tests covering all modes.

title: Implement TLS certificate generation
description: In crates/common: self-signed TLS certificate generation for the teamserver HTTPS listener and WebSocket server. Use rcgen crate to generate RSA/ECDSA certs at runtime (same approach as Havoc). Also support loading existing PEM certs from profile config. Integrate with rustls/tokio-rustls for use in Axum.
close_reason: Added shared TLS identity generation, PEM loading, and rustls/tokio-rustls integration in crates/common.

title: security: API key HMAC secret derived from two UUIDv4 values — reduced entropy (244 vs 256 bits)
description:
Introduced by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

teamserver/src/api.rs:349-357 — generate_key_hash_secret() builds a 32-byte HMAC secret by concatenating two UUIDv4 values.

UUIDv4 carries only 122 bits of randomness (6 bits encode version/variant). Two concatenated UUIDs give at most ~244 bits of entropy rather than the 256 bits a [u8; 32] could hold. This is not exploitable in practice but is inconsistent with the rest of the codebase which uses getrandom/OsRng directly for key generation (see generate_agent_crypto_material()).

Fix: use OsRng or getrandom::fill() to populate the 32-byte secret directly, matching the pattern already used for agent key material.
close_reason: replaced UUID concatenation with getrandom::fill() for full 256-bit entropy

title: test: negotiate_socks5 with zero methods advertised is untested
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/sockets.rs
**Gap type**: edge case

`negotiate_socks5` reads `n_methods` (a u8 count) and then reads that many method bytes. If `n_methods = 0` — a legal but unusual SOCKS5 handshake — the method bytes slice is empty, `methods.contains(&SOCKS_METHOD_NO_AUTH)` is false, and the function should return a `PermissionDenied` error after sending the `[5, 0xFF]` rejection. This path is not tested.

While a client sending zero methods is broken by definition, a malformed or adversarial client could do so. Without a test, a regression that, for example, sends `[5, 0]` (accepting no-auth) when there are zero methods would go undetected and allow unauthenticated SOCKS5 connections from clients that did not even advertise a method.

**Suggested test approach**:
Send a SOCKS5 greeting of `[5, 0]` (version=5, n_methods=0, no method bytes). Call `negotiate_socks5`. Assert the result is an `Err` with kind `PermissionDenied`. Read the 2-byte response from the client side and assert it is `[5, 0xFF]` (version=5, NO ACCEPTABLE METHODS).

title: test: deserialize_u16_from_any rejects port numbers greater than 65535
description:
**File**: common/src/domain.rs
**Gap type**: edge case / error path

`deserialize_u16_from_any` is used for the `port_bind` field in `HttpListenerConfig`, `DnsListenerConfig`, and the `port` field in `HttpListenerProxyConfig`. It accepts both numeric and string inputs by delegating to `deserialize_u64_from_any` and then calling `u16::try_from`.

There are no tests for the out-of-range case: a port value of 70000 or 99999 (valid as u64, invalid as u16) should return a descriptive serde error. Without this test, a misconfigured profile with an invalid port could silently truncate the value or cause a confusing downstream failure rather than a clear parse error.

Also untested: string input `"0"` (should succeed, yielding port 0), and a string with surrounding whitespace like `" 8080 "` (the trim() call should handle it, but this is never exercised).

**Suggested test approach**:
Deserialize a JSON object for `HttpListenerConfig` with `port_bind: 70000` and assert the result is `Err`. Repeat with `port_bind: "70000"`. Also verify `port_bind: 0` succeeds and `port_bind: " 443 "` parses to 443.

title: loot export CSV: no sanitization for spreadsheet formula injection
description:
Introduced by: Claude Sonnet 4.6

client/src/main.rs:3881 — `csv_field` (and the export pipeline that calls it) does not sanitize field values that begin with spreadsheet formula prefix characters (`=`, `+`, `-`, `@`).

Loot data is adversary-controlled: credential names, file names, source paths, and preview content all originate from the compromised target host. A target-side attacker can craft a value like `=EXEC("malware.exe")` in any of these fields. When an operator exports loot to CSV and opens the file in Excel or LibreOffice Calc without disabling macros/external content, the formula executes in the operator's environment.

Fix: prepend a single-quote `'` (or wrap in a quoted zero-width space) to any field value whose first non-whitespace character is `=`, `+`, `-`, or `@`, and add a test asserting the sanitization.
close_reason: Prepend single-quote to formula-trigger chars (=+-@) in csv_field; tests added for all four triggers, whitespace-prefixed values, combined trigger+quote, and safe values.

title: correctness: transport state setters mutate memory before SQLite commit
description:
Introduced by: Codex <noreply@openai.com>

teamserver/src/agents.rs:415-421 (`set_ctr_offset`) updates the in-memory `ctr_block_offset` before awaiting `repository.set_ctr_block_offset(...)`. teamserver/src/agents.rs:472-487 (`set_encryption`) overwrites the in-memory AES key/IV before awaiting `repository.update_with_listener(...)`.

Impact: if the SQLite write fails, both methods return an error but leave the live registry mutated. The in-memory transport state then disagrees with durable state, so a restart or reconnect can resume with stale CTR/key material while the running process uses different values. That is exactly the kind of memory-vs-database drift the rest of the agent registry has been hardened against.

Fix: make these updates atomic from the caller perspective by persisting first and only then mutating memory, or by rolling the in-memory mutation back on persistence failure. Add regression coverage that injects repository write failures and asserts no partial mutation remains.
close_reason: Persist-before-mutate ordering fixed in set_ctr_offset and set_encryption; regression tests added that inject pool failure and assert no partial in-memory mutation.

title: Client - Core Infrastructure
description: Core egui client binary: application setup, WebSocket client connection to teamserver (with TLS), operator authentication flow, JSON protocol handler and event processing loop. Foundation for all UI components.
close_reason: Core egui client binary complete: application setup with AppPhase state machine, WebSocket client with TLS (CA/custom-CA/fingerprint/skip-verify), operator authentication flow, JSON protocol handler with full OperatorMessage dispatch, and async event processing loop. Foundation for all UI components is in place; dependent issues (1nri, 1uz7, 2uo8, 1j7n) are now unblocked.

title: test: Admin role performing admin-only operations has no positive integration test
description:
**File**: teamserver/tests/e2e_operator_agent_session.rs
**Gap type**: existence / RBAC

The RBAC integration tests only cover *rejection* paths: Analyst cannot task, Analyst cannot create listener, Operator cannot send admin messages.  There is no test verifying that an Admin-role user *can* successfully perform Admin-gated operations (e.g. AgentRemove, operator management).

Without a positive test, a regression that accidentally upgrades all denial checks to deny-all would still pass every existing RBAC test.  The Admin acceptance path is the complement of the denial tests and must be independently verified.

**Suggested test approach**:
Log in as the `admin` user from `multi_role_profile()`, send an `AgentRemove` or operator-management message, and assert the server does NOT close the connection (i.e., no Close frame arrives within the assertion window).

title: fake_404_response leaks x-havoc: true header, fingerprinting the C2 server
description:
Introduced by: Codex <noreply@openai.com>

teamserver/src/listeners.rs:927 — fake_404_response() unconditionally adds an 'x-havoc: true' response header to every 404 response returned by the HTTP listener. This response is sent for all non-matching requests (wrong method, URI, user-agent, required headers) as well as for unknown agent probe attempts. Any attacker scanning for C2 infrastructure can fingerprint Red Cell listener instances by sending any HTTP request and checking for this header.

Fix: remove the x-havoc header from fake_404_response(), or make it configurable via the listener profile so operators can explicitly opt in.
close_reason: Removed x-havoc: true header from fake_404_response(); added test assertion to verify absence of the header

title: no tests for pivot connect/disconnect failure paths
description:
Introduced by: Codex <noreply@openai.com>

teamserver/src/dispatch/pivot.rs — handle_pivot_connect_callback, handle_pivot_disconnect_callback

Both failure branches (success == 0) are untested. There are no unit or integration tests that exercise the pivot connect or disconnect failure responses.

Current test coverage for pivot dispatch only covers the success path (via the e2e tests and mock agent tests). The error paths are completely dark.

Fix: add unit tests for:
- handle_pivot_connect_callback with success=0 verifying an Error event is broadcast
- handle_pivot_disconnect_callback with success=0 verifying an Error event is broadcast
close_reason: Tests already present and passing: pivot_connect_callback_failure_broadcasts_error_event and pivot_disconnect_callback_failure_broadcasts_error_event in dispatch::tests verify Error event is broadcast on success==0 for both connect and disconnect failure paths

title: test: DNS ListenerConfig variant has no serde round-trip test
description:
**File**: common/src/domain.rs
**Gap type**: round-trip / edge case

`ListenerConfig` is a tagged enum with three variants: Http, Smb, and Dns. The test suite has round-trip tests for Http (via `listener_config_reports_name_and_protocol`) and Smb (via `listener_config_round_trips_with_tagged_protocol`), but not for Dns.

`DnsListenerConfig` has a `record_types` field with a custom default (`default_dns_record_types` returns `["TXT"]`). This field is easy to accidentally break or mis-tag. A round-trip test would also verify that `{"protocol":"dns", ...}` deserializes back to `ListenerConfig::Dns` correctly.

Without this test, a rename or serde attribute regression on `DnsListenerConfig` would not be caught until a DNS listener is created in production.

**Suggested test approach**:
Construct a `ListenerConfig::Dns` value with all fields populated (including explicit `record_types`), serialize to JSON, deserialize back, and assert the two are equal. Also verify that omitting `record_types` from the JSON restores the default `["TXT"]`.

title: test: no test verifying SOCKS5 server only binds to localhost and rejects non-localhost connections
description:
Introduced by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

teamserver/src/sockets.rs — SOCKS5 relay binds to 127.0.0.1 only and accepts NO_AUTH SOCKS5 method.

The localhost-only binding is the entire access control for the SOCKS5 tunnel. There is no test verifying that a connection from a non-localhost address is rejected, or that the port is not advertised on external interfaces.

On a multi-user teamserver host, any local process with knowledge of the SOCKS5 port could route arbitrary TCP traffic through an agent's tunnel without operator credentials.

Fix: add an integration test that:
1. Starts the SOCKS5 server
2. Verifies it is only reachable on 127.0.0.1 (not 0.0.0.0)
3. Documents the no-authentication design decision as a known, intentional security boundary
close_reason: Added socks5_server_binds_to_localhost_only test: verifies bind address is 127.0.0.1, rejects connections on non-loopback interfaces, and documents NO_AUTH as an intentional security boundary

title: workflow: fix for red-cell-c2-3qq0 left uncommitted in git stash
description:
Introduced by: Ubuntu-C2-dev02-claude

The fix for DEMON_INIT all-zero AES IV rejection (red-cell-c2-3qq0) was implemented in `teamserver/src/demon.rs` but was never committed or pushed. The work (38 additions: IV validation in `parse_init_agent` and a regression test `parse_rejects_plaintext_zero_iv_init`) is sitting in the local git stash (`stash@{0}: WIP on main: 08aff95 chore: claim red-cell-c2-3qq0`).

Per AGENTS.md mandatory workflow: 'Work is NOT complete until `git push` succeeds'. The agent session ended without landing the work.

The fix itself looks correct:
- Adds `if iv.iter().all(|byte| *byte == 0)` guard with tracing warn
- Returns `Err(DemonParserError::InvalidInit("all-zero AES IV is not allowed"))`
- Regression test verifies rejection and confirms agent not registered in state

**Action required**: Pop the stash, commit, and push. Issue `red-cell-c2-3qq0` should then be closeable.
close_reason: Fix was committed and pushed in commit 6770ac9 — stash issue resolved.

title: test: normalize_server_url error paths have zero coverage
description:
**File**: client/src/transport.rs
**Gap type**: error path

`normalize_server_url` has exactly one test — the happy path (appending `/havoc/` to a bare WSS URL). The function has four distinct failure branches and two normalisation behaviours that are completely untested:

1. **Unsupported scheme** — passing `http://` or `https://` or `ftp://` should return `TransportError::UnsupportedScheme`. No test verifies this.
2. **Malformed URL** — a string that is not a valid URL (e.g. `"not a url"`) should return `TransportError::InvalidUrl`. No test verifies this.
3. **Missing host** — a URL like `wss:///path` that parses but has no host should return `TransportError::MissingHost`. No test verifies this.
4. **`/havoc` without trailing slash** — `wss://host/havoc` should normalise to `.../havoc/`. No test verifies this.
5. **Custom path preserved** — `wss://host/custom/path` should be passed through unchanged. No test verifies this.

Without these tests, regressions in URL validation would go undetected. The transport layer is the first security boundary — accepting an `http://` URL would silently send operator credentials in cleartext.

**Suggested test approach**:
Add five tests for the above cases: each calls `normalize_server_url` with the specified input and asserts either the correct `Err` variant or the expected normalised string.
close_reason: Added 6 tests covering unsupported scheme (http, https), malformed URL, /havoc trailing-slash normalisation, and custom path preservation. MissingHost branch is unreachable for ws/wss with the url crate.

title: correctness: kill_date u64→i64 overflow silently becomes i64::MAX in 3 locations
description:
Introduced by: Codex <noreply@openai.com>

teamserver/src/demon.rs:319, teamserver/src/dispatch.rs:870, teamserver/src/dispatch.rs:2222

All three sites read kill_date as u64 from the protocol and convert with:
  (kill_date != 0).then_some(i64::try_from(kill_date).unwrap_or(i64::MAX))

If an agent sends kill_date > i64::MAX (~292 years from Unix epoch), the conversion fails
silently and the stored kill_date becomes i64::MAX (year ~292 billion). Kill-date eviction
logic will never fire, so the agent becomes effectively immortal regardless of operator
intent.

Fix: return an error (CommandDispatchError::InvalidCallbackPayload) when kill_date exceeds
i64::MAX, or clamp with a warning. The current silent fallback hides a protocol violation.
close_reason: Rejected kill_date values above i64::MAX in init, checkin, and config paths with regression coverage.

title: Project Setup & Infrastructure
description: Initialize the Cargo workspace, set up directory structure, configure CI/CD pipeline, and establish the foundational project scaffolding for the Red Cell C2 Rust rewrite. This epic must be completed before any other development work begins.
close_reason: Completed foundational workspace scaffolding and enforced green quality gates.

title: tests: SOCKS5 relay integration tests
description: No tests exist for the SOCKS5 proxy relay (sockets.rs). Add tests covering: SOCKS5 CONNECT handshake (IPv4, IPv6, domain), data relay between client and target, relay teardown on agent disconnect, write_client_data error path (currently silently dropped per red-cell-c2-2d3), and the SocksServerHandle::shutdown path (red-cell-c2-4vu). Use tokio test runtime with in-process TCP listeners as targets.
close_reason: Added teamserver/tests/socks5_relay.rs: 12 tests covering server lifecycle, SOCKS5 CONNECT handshake (IPv4/domain/IPv6), data relay via finish_connect+write_client_data, teardown on remove_agent

title: test: Profile::from_file rejects missing profile path with ProfileError::Read
description:
**File**: common/src/config.rs
**Gap type**: error path

The `Profile::from_file` method maps filesystem errors to `ProfileError::Read` which carries the path and the source `io::Error`. This error variant is never tested — all `from_file` tests use a path that successfully resolves.

A regression here (e.g., the `Read` variant being replaced with a generic `Parse` panic or a missing path context) would go undetected.

**Suggested test approach**:
Call `Profile::from_file` with a path inside a fresh `tempfile::TempDir` that was never written. Assert that the result is `Err(ProfileError::Read { path, .. })` where `path` matches the non-existent file path.
close_reason: Added test asserting ProfileError::Read carries the correct path using tempfile::TempDir

title: Implement JSON WebSocket message types (operator protocol)
description: In crates/common: define all Rust structs (with serde Serialize/Deserialize) for the JSON-over-WebSocket operator protocol. Message structure: { Head: { Event, User, Timestamp }, Body: { SubEvent, Info } }. All event types: InitConnection, Login, ListenerNew, ListenerStart, ListenerStop, AgentNew, AgentUpdate, AgentTask, AgentResponse, Chat, BuildPayload, etc. Mirror the existing Havoc event system. Write unit tests for JSON round-tripping.
close_reason: Implemented Havoc-compatible operator WebSocket message types and round-trip tests in red-cell-common.

title: test: resolve_logging_config_with_override with profile=None and a non-empty RUST_LOG override is untested
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/logging.rs
**Gap type**: edge case

`resolve_logging_config_with_override` handles three filter-directive sources in priority order: RUST_LOG env override > profile level > debug/info default. The existing tests cover:
- profile=None, debug=true, override=None → "debug" + Pretty
- profile=None, debug=false, override=None → "info" + Json
- profile=Some, debug=false, override=None → profile level
- profile=Some, debug=false, override=Some → override wins
- profile=Some, debug=false, override=Some(whitespace) → falls back to profile level

Missing: **profile=None, debug=false, override=Some("trace")** — should produce filter_directive="trace" with Json format (because debug_logging=false determines the format when there is no profile). This combination is the most common production scenario for operators who set RUST_LOG without a Logging block in their profile. If the None-profile + override path were accidentally broken, none of the existing tests would catch it.

**Suggested test approach**:
Add a test calling `resolve_logging_config_with_override(None, false, Some("trace".to_owned()))` and assert filter_directive=="trace", format==LogFormat::Json, file==None.

title: dispatch/transfer: let _ = downloads.finish() silently discards DB error on download completion
description:
Introduced by: Claude Sonnet 4.6

teamserver/src/dispatch/transfer.rs:110 — The download completion path discards errors from downloads.finish():

    let _ = downloads.finish(agent_id, file_id).await;

downloads.finish() calls remove_locked() which removes the in-memory tracking state. However, if the underlying DB write fails (e.g., SQLite I/O error), the download is removed from memory but the database row may not be updated. The operator will see the download as still in-progress in the database view while the in-memory state says it is complete, causing a persistent memory/database drift.

Similarly, dispatch/socket.rs:160 and 167 discard errors from sockets.close_client() and sockets.finish_connect(), which could leave socket state inconsistent between memory and the event bus.

Fix: Log these errors with tracing::warn! rather than discarding them. The operations should not be made fatal (returning Err) since they are in callback handlers that must continue processing, but silent discard prevents any operator visibility into stuck downloads/sockets.
close_reason: Replaced let _ = ... with warn! logging for downloads.finish() (None case), sockets.close_client() (Err case), and sockets.finish_connect() (Err case) to surface state drift to operators

title: test: add integration tests for dispatch/output — mark_agent_dead_and_broadcast and handle_demon_info_callback
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/dispatch/output.rs
**Functions with no tests**:
- `fn mark_agent_dead_and_broadcast` (line 170) — marks agent status as dead in DB and broadcasts exit event to all operators; called on exit/kill-date callbacks
- `fn handle_demon_info_callback` (line 195) — processes demon metadata update (PID, integrity, username, hostname, OS version) and updates agent record in DB
- `fn handle_job_callback` (line 263) — processes running job list callback, stores job table in DB
- `fn handle_sleep_callback` (line 333) — processes sleep change acknowledgment
- `fn handle_config_callback` (line 360) — processes agent config dump

**Why this matters**: mark_agent_dead_and_broadcast is the sole mechanism for operators to learn an agent has died. A bug here (e.g. DB write fails silently, broadcast skipped) means dead agents appear alive in the UI indefinitely. handle_demon_info_callback updates the agent record that all subsequent commands depend on.

**Minimum test scenarios needed**:
1. mark_agent_dead_and_broadcast: agent exists → DB status = dead, broadcast event emitted
2. handle_demon_info_callback: valid metadata payload → DB agent record updated with new PID/user/host
3. handle_job_callback: multi-job payload → correct job rows broadcast
4. Error path: handle_demon_info_callback with truncated payload → error returned, no partial write

**Test location**: teamserver/tests/output_dispatch.rs (integration test with in-memory DB)
close_reason: Added 4 integration tests in teamserver/tests/output_dispatch.rs: exit callback marks agent dead + broadcasts AgentUpdate/AgentResponse, DemonInfo MemAlloc broadcasts response, CommandJob List broadcasts formatted table, truncated DemonInfo payload returns HTTP error with no broadcast

title: test: format_memory_protect, format_memory_state, format_memory_type fallback to hex for unknown values
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/dispatch/process.rs
**Gap type**: edge case / correctness

Three formatting functions in process.rs — `format_memory_protect`, `format_memory_state`, and `format_memory_type` — each handle a set of known Windows constants and fall through to `format!("0x{other:X}")` for unrecognized values. None of these fallback paths have tests.

In practice the Demon agent can return memory protection flags composed of multiple flags ORed together (e.g., PAGE_GUARD | PAGE_READWRITE = 0x104). The single known-value tests in the process-memory handler integration test only exercise exact known values. An unknown or combined value would silently produce a hex output; if the format string were accidentally changed to lowercase ("{:x}"), the casing of all fallback hex values would change without a test catching it.

**Suggested test approach**:
assert_eq!(format_memory_protect(0x01), "PAGE_NOACCESS"); assert_eq!(format_memory_protect(0x104), "0x104"); assert_eq!(format_memory_state(0x3000), "0x3000"); assert_eq!(format_memory_type(0x9999), "0x9999").

title: test: session_token rejects non-Bearer Authorization scheme without explicit test
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/rbac.rs
**Gap type**: error path / edge case

The `session_token` function handles two header names: `Authorization` and `x-session-token`. For `Authorization`, it expects the value to start with `"Bearer "` (including the trailing space). If the value does not start with that exact prefix — e.g., `Basic dXNlcjpwYXNz`, `Token abc`, or `Bearerxxx` (no space) — `strip_prefix("Bearer ")` returns `None` and the function returns `Err(AuthorizationError::InvalidAuthorizationHeader)`.

This specific path has **no test**. The existing tests cover: empty Bearer token, non-UTF-8 Authorization header, empty x-session-token, non-UTF-8 x-session-token. None test a syntactically valid but wrong-scheme Authorization header value.

A future refactor that changes the prefix check (e.g., accidentally making it case-insensitive, or accepting `Bearer` without the trailing space) would not be caught by the existing suite — an attacker could potentially use a differently-cased or differently-formatted header to probe behavior.

**Suggested test approach**:
Build a request with `Authorization: Basic dXNlcjpwYXNz`. Call `AuthenticatedOperator::from_request_parts`. Assert the error is `AuthorizationError::InvalidAuthorizationHeader`. Also test `Authorization: Bearertoken` (no space) for the same result.

title: audit: OperatorMessage::ListenerRemove WebSocket path missing audit trail
description:
The WebSocket dispatch_operator_command function gained audit logging for ListenerNew and ListenerMark in 001245d, but OperatorMessage::ListenerRemove (crates/teamserver/src/websocket.rs:597-607) has no audit calls on either the success or failure branch.

When an operator removes a listener over the WebSocket protocol, no audit record is written. This is inconsistent with the REST DELETE which is also unaudited (see red-cell-c2-3kc).

Fix: add log_operator_action calls in both branches of the ListenerRemove match arm, using action label 'listener.delete', consistent with the pattern already used for ListenerNew/ListenerMark in the same function.
close_reason: Added log_operator_action calls on both success and failure branches of the ListenerRemove WebSocket handler, using 'listener.delete' action label consistent with ListenerNew/ListenerMark. Two new tests verify audit records are written.

title: test: notify_audit_record_detached silently drops when notifier is closing
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/webhook.rs
**Gap type**: edge case

`notify_audit_record_detached` checks `delivery_state.closing` at the top and returns early without spawning a task when the notifier is in the closing state. This guard is not tested.

Without this test, a refactor that removed the early-return guard (or moved it after the `fetch_add`) would cause tasks to be spawned after `shutdown()` returns, leaving dangling background HTTP requests. The existing shutdown tests verify that in-flight requests drain correctly, but none verify the drop-when-closing path.

**Suggested test approach**:
Start a notifier, call `shutdown(Duration::ZERO)` to set the closing flag, then call `notify_audit_record_detached` and assert that the `pending` counter remains at zero (no task was spawned) and that no payload arrives at the mock webhook server.
close_reason: Test already implemented in commit 6378c9d — notify_detached_is_dropped_when_closing asserts pending=0 and no HTTP delivery after shutdown()

title: completeness: os_build is always empty in operator_agent_info
description:
Introduced by: Codex <noreply@openai.com>

teamserver/src/agent_events.rs — os_build is hardcoded to String::new() in the OperatorAgentInfo construction. The DemonInit parser does decode the Windows build number (e.g., 22000 for Win11), but it is not stored in AgentRecord and therefore cannot be surfaced to operators. Havoc clients use os_build to distinguish Windows 10 from Windows 11 and to show patch level.

Fix: add an os_build field to AgentRecord, populate it from DemonInit parsing, persist it to SQLite, and include it in OperatorAgentInfo.
close_reason: Added os_build: u32 to AgentRecord, populated from DemonInit and CommandCheckin parsers, persisted via migration 20260315130000_agent_os_build.sql, surfaced in OperatorAgentInfo and ApiAgentInfo

title: SMB/DNS listener accept loops miss shutdown signal between iterations
close_reason: Pinned SMB/DNS shutdown futures, made shutdown notifications latch after initiation, and added regression tests.

title: test: send_socks_connect_reply has no test for well-formed DOMAIN reply
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/sockets.rs
**Gap type**: error path / edge case

`send_socks_connect_reply` uses a different serialization path for `SOCKS_ATYP_DOMAIN` than for IPv4/IPv6: it emits a 1-byte length prefix (`address.len() as u8`) before the address bytes, while IPv4/IPv6 extend_from_slice without a prefix. The only existing test exercises the *failure* path where the domain exceeds 255 bytes (`send_socks_connect_reply_rejects_oversized_domain_addresses`). There is no test for the success path where a valid, short domain name is echoed back.

If the length prefix byte were accidentally omitted or mispositioned, the SOCKS5 reply for any domain-addressed connection would be malformed, causing every hostname-targeted relay to fail silently. The client would receive garbled bytes and either fail to parse the reply or misinterpret the port.

**Suggested test approach**:
Call `send_socks_connect_reply` with `atyp=SOCKS_ATYP_DOMAIN`, `address=b"example.com"`, `port=443`, and `reply=SOCKS_REPLY_SUCCEEDED`. Read the full response from the peer socket and assert: `[5, 0, 0, 3, 11, b'e', b'x', …, 1, 187]` where byte 4 is `11` (the length prefix), bytes 5-15 are ASCII `example.com`, and the final two bytes are `443` big-endian.

title: Unauthenticated DEMON_INIT reconnect probe desyncs agent AES-CTR counter
description:
Introduced by: Codex <noreply@openai.com> (feat: add demon packet parser)

crates/teamserver/src/listeners.rs:976-985 / crates/teamserver/src/demon.rs:136-137

The Demon protocol's reconnect probe is a DEMON_INIT packet with an empty payload (20 bytes total: 12-byte header + 4-byte command_id + 4-byte request_id). The parser returns `ParsedDemonPacket::Reconnect` for this shape.

`process_demon_transport` then calls `build_init_ack(registry, header.agent_id)` if the agent_id is known. `build_init_ack` calls `registry.encrypt_for_agent()`, which **advances the teamserver's CTR block offset by 1** (4 bytes → 1 block).

**Attack**: Any attacker who can reach the HTTP listener port and knows a registered agent's 32-bit agent_id (visible in plaintext in every Demon packet header when TLS is not used) can send crafted 20-byte reconnect probes to repeatedly advance the server's CTR counter for the target agent, while the real agent's counter stays unchanged. After N forged probes, the server encrypts responses at offset N while the agent expects offset 0. All subsequent comms are silently broken — the agent receives garbage and cannot communicate.

No knowledge of the agent's AES key is required. The agent_id is the only secret.

**Fix**: Reconnect probe handling should not advance the CTR counter. The ACK for a reconnect should either use offset 0 (if the agent is confirming it still holds the correct key) or require the probe to carry a keyed proof-of-knowledge before advancing state. Alternatively, require a valid HMAC/proof before treating a packet as an authenticated reconnect.
close_reason: Reconnect ACKs now use a non-mutating CTR preview path so unauthenticated probes cannot desync agent transport state.

title: Implement REST endpoints: agents and listeners
description: In crates/teamserver: REST endpoints. Agents: GET /agents (list all), GET /agents/:id (details), DELETE /agents/:id (kill), POST /agents/:id/task (queue a job). Listeners: GET /listeners, POST /listeners (create), PUT /listeners/:name/start, PUT /listeners/:name/stop, DELETE /listeners/:name. Full RBAC checks on each.
close_reason: Implemented RBAC-protected REST endpoints for agents and listeners in crates/teamserver

title: test: add integration tests for dispatch/screenshot and dispatch/socket callback handlers
description:
**Files**: teamserver/src/dispatch/screenshot.rs, teamserver/src/dispatch/socket.rs
**Functions with no tests**:
- `fn handle_screenshot_callback` (screenshot.rs line 18) — receives raw PNG/BMP bytes from agent, stores as loot record, broadcasts download-complete event to operators
- `fn handle_socket_callback` (socket.rs line 9) — routes SOCKS5 socket data callbacks (open, read, write, close) to the relay layer
