description:
**File**: teamserver/src/dispatch/network.rs
**Gap type**: edge case / correctness

The `int_to_ipv4(value: u32)` function converts a native u32 (received from the Demon agent as little-endian bytes on the wire) into a dotted-decimal IPv4 string by calling `value.to_le_bytes()`. If the byte-order interpretation were to flip (e.g., changed to `to_be_bytes()`), all IP addresses displayed in reverse-port-forward and socket callbacks would be printed backwards, with no test catching it.

This function is called in `handle_socket_callback` for rportfwd add, remove, and list events — all operator-facing. A regression here would render all displayed addresses unreadable.

**Suggested test approach**:
Call `int_to_ipv4(0x0100_007F)` and assert it equals "127.0.0.1" (0x7F=127 is byte 1 if big-endian, byte 0 if little-endian); call `int_to_ipv4(0xC0A8_0101)` and assert "1.1.168.192" (the little-endian interpretation of 192.168.1.1). One assertion is enough to pin down which endian convention is in use.

title: Implement job queue and COMMAND_GET_JOB handler
description: In crates/teamserver: handle COMMAND_GET_JOB (command 1) - agent polling for work. Dequeue all pending jobs from the agent's job queue. Serialize jobs into the Demon binary response format (command ID + params per job). If no jobs, return empty response. Handle COMMAND_CHECKIN (100) heartbeat: update agent LastCallIn timestamp in registry and DB, broadcast AgentUpdate event to operators.
close_reason: Implemented queued job draining, Demon job responses, and checkin heartbeat updates

title: test helper: available_port() has TOCTOU race — port released before listener can bind it
description:
Introduced by: Claude (Ubuntu-C2-dev02-claude)

In teamserver/tests/common/mod.rs, the `available_port()` helper (line 141-143) works by binding an OS-assigned port to get the number, then returning that number — the TcpListener is implicitly dropped as the function returns, releasing the port before the caller passes it to `ListenerManager::create()` (or `SocketRelayManager::add_socks_server()`). Any other process or concurrent test can grab that port in the window between the drop and the bind by the listener under test.

`available_port_excluding()` (line 147-158) has the same problem: it calls `drop(listener)` explicitly before returning, which makes the race window even more obvious.

Correct behaviour: keep the `TcpListener` alive (held by the caller) so the port stays reserved until the component under test binds it, then drop it immediately before calling start. Return both the port number and the guard, or use `SO_REUSEPORT` semantics if the OS supports it.

Affected tests: all tests in listener_lifecycle.rs, socks5_relay.rs, and smb_listener.rs (http_listener_pipeline.rs and others) that call `common::available_port()`.
close_reason: available_port() and available_port_excluding() now return (u16, TcpListener). Callers hold the guard until immediately before start()/add_socks_server() binds the port, eliminating the race window. The port-in-use test reuses the guard directly instead of a second bind.

title: correctness: listener stop errors silently discarded during ListenerManager::shutdown
close_reason: replaced let _ = with warn! log on stop failure during shutdown

title: test: CHECKIN AES key rotation rejection must preserve original session key
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/dispatch/checkin.rs
**Gap type**: error path / security

The `handle_checkin` function contains a critical security guard (lines 29–51): when a CHECKIN payload carries different AES key/IV material than the registered session key, the new key is silently discarded and the original is kept. This prevents replay attacks where an adversary who captures a CHECKIN frame could push the session key to a value they control.

This property has no unit or integration test. If the `if key_rotation { ... updated.encryption = existing.encryption.clone(); }` block were accidentally deleted or the condition inverted, no test would catch it.

**Suggested test approach**:
Register an agent via a mock DEMON_INIT, then send a COMMAND_CHECKIN callback payload containing a different (attacker-controlled) AES key/IV. After dispatch, assert that the agent's stored `encryption.aes_key` and `encryption.aes_iv` are unchanged from the original DEMON_INIT values.

title: test: operator_agent_info serialized JSON field names match Havoc wire protocol
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/agent_events.rs (serialized via common/src/operator.rs AgentInfo)
**Gap type**: round-trip / protocol compatibility

`operator_agent_info` builds an `OperatorAgentInfo` struct that is serialized to JSON and sent directly to Havoc operator clients over WebSocket. The Havoc client expects specific field names such as `NameID`, `MagicValue`, `SleepDelay`, `Jitter`, `ProcessPID`, etc.

The existing tests in `agent_events.rs` assert on Rust struct field values (e.g., `info.magic_value == "deadbeef"`), but do NOT assert on the serialized JSON output. A `#[serde(rename = ...)` attribute change or a field rename in `AgentInfo` would silently break Havoc compatibility — all Rust assertions would still pass but the operator client would receive unknown keys and display nothing.

This is especially risky because `AgentInfo` in `operator.rs` has many fields with Havoc-specific rename attributes. Any one of them is a potential compatibility regression.

**Suggested test approach**:
Call `operator_agent_info(...)` with a known `AgentRecord`, then call `serde_json::to_value(&info)` and assert that the resulting JSON object contains keys `"NameID"`, `"MagicValue"`, `"SleepDelay"`, `"Jitter"`, `"ProcessPID"`, `"ExternalIP"` with the expected string values.

title: security: all-zero IV accepted during DemonInit without rejection
description:
Introduced by: Codex <noreply@openai.com>

teamserver/src/demon.rs — parse_init_agent correctly rejects all-zero AES keys, but performs no equivalent check on the AES IV. An adversarially crafted DemonInit with a valid key and all-zero IV is accepted. Combined with the keystream-reuse issue (IV is static per-agent), an all-zero IV produces the weakest possible keystream for that agent slot.

Fix: add the same all-zero guard to the IV field that already exists for the key field in parse_init_agent, and add a corresponding test.
close_reason: all-zero IV guard already added to parse_init_agent alongside matching test parse_rejects_plaintext_zero_iv_init in commit 6770ac9

title: External listener transport remains a declared but unimplemented stub
description:
Introduced by: Codex

teamserver/src/listeners.rs:1555
teamserver/src/listeners.rs:1559
teamserver/src/listeners.rs:1562

External listeners are still exposed as a first-class transport in the shared config and operator surface, but every start path hard-fails with a not implemented error. That leaves the architecture and operator UX claiming support for a connector type that cannot do any work. The fix should either implement the external service-bridge runtime or remove or deprecate the transport from the supported surface until it exists.
close_reason: Rejected unsupported External listeners during profile validation and listener creation/sync; legacy persisted entries still fail safely on restore.

title: test: add unit tests for LoginState::set_tls_failure and tls_failure clearing
description:
**File**: client/src/login.rs
**Functions with no tests**:
- `fn set_tls_failure` (line 98) — stores a TlsFailure on the login state for UI display
- `fn set_connecting` (line 103) — clears tls_failure when a new attempt starts (the clearing of tls_failure is untested)

**Why this matters**: If `set_tls_failure` silently breaks (e.g. field rename, logic change) the TLS pinning failure dialog will not appear — the UI will show no indication that the cert fingerprint was rejected, causing silent MITM vulnerability exposure. The `set_connecting` clearing of `tls_failure` is equally untested: if a second login attempt fails to clear a stale TLS failure the UI will show a stale cert warning.

**Minimum test scenarios needed**:
1. Happy path: call `set_tls_failure(failure)` → `state.tls_failure` is `Some(failure)`
2. State transition: call `set_tls_failure(...)` then `set_connecting()` → `state.tls_failure` is `None`
3. Edge case: call `set_tls_failure(...)` then `set_error(...)` → tls_failure is unchanged (set_error does not clear it)

**Test location**: inline `#[cfg(test)]` in `client/src/login.rs`
close_reason: Added three inline tests: set_tls_failure stores failure, set_connecting clears tls_failure, set_error does not clear tls_failure

title: DNS upload assembler accepts untrusted chunk total from client, no upload session cap
description:
In listeners.rs, try_assemble_upload (line 1473) accepts a u16 total value directly from the client DNS query and uses it as the expected chunk count, storing it in DnsPendingUpload::total without validation. An attacker can:
1. Send an upload query with total=65535 and seq=0 for a fabricated agent_id. This creates a DnsPendingUpload entry that will never complete (65535 chunks required) and stays in the uploads map for 120 seconds (DNS_UPLOAD_TIMEOUT_SECS).
2. Repeat with thousands of distinct fake agent_ids without triggering the cleanup (retain is only called on the next incoming upload query). Between upload bursts, stale entries accumulate in the uploads Mutex<HashMap> without being pruned.
3. The total field is also not validated for consistency across chunks of the same agent_id — subsequent packets with a different total are silently ignored since the entry already exists, but adversarial early packets can set total to an unexpected value for a legitimate agent.

Fix: (a) Add a reasonable cap on total (e.g. 256 or 1024 chunks max), returning 'err' immediately if exceeded. (b) Add a periodic background cleanup task (e.g. tokio::spawn with interval) instead of relying on caller-triggered retain. (c) Add a max uploads map capacity check (e.g. 1000 concurrent sessions).

Location: crates/teamserver/src/listeners.rs, try_assemble_upload, lines 1473-1516 and handle_dns_packet line 1381.
close_reason: Rejected oversized and inconsistent DNS upload sessions, added periodic stale-session cleanup, and capped concurrent pending uploads.

title: test: add unit tests for network formatting helpers (format_net_sessions, format_net_shares, int_to_ipv4)
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/dispatch/network.rs
**Functions with no tests**:
- `fn format_net_sessions` (line 142) — renders SMB session table with dynamic column widths; empty input returns empty string
- `fn format_net_shares` (line 168) — renders SMB share table with dynamic column widths; empty input returns empty string
- `fn format_net_group_descriptions` (line 195) — renders group/description table; empty returns empty string
- `fn int_to_ipv4` (line 211) — converts little-endian u32 to dotted-quad IPv4 string

**Why this matters**: Column-width calculation with `max().unwrap_or()` chains have a known failure mode when data contains very long entries that exceed header width — a bug here would misalign operator UI output silently. int_to_ipv4 endianness is critical for IP display accuracy.

**Minimum test scenarios needed**:
1. format_net_sessions: empty slice → empty string; single row → header + row; row shorter than header width → column still min-width
2. format_net_shares: similar shape as above
3. int_to_ipv4: 0x0101A8C0 → "192.168.1.1"; 0x00000000 → "0.0.0.0"
4. format_net_group_descriptions: multiple rows with varying widths → assert alignment

**Test location**: inline `#[cfg(test)]` in teamserver/src/dispatch/network.rs

title: Implement operator authentication
description: In crates/teamserver: operator login flow. Operators defined in HCL profile (username + plaintext password, hashed at load time with SHA3-256 to match Havoc). WebSocket handshake: client sends {User, Password_SHA3}. Server validates against profile. On success: issue session token (UUID or JWT), associate with WebSocket connection. Reject unknown users or wrong passwords. Write unit tests.
close_reason: Implemented SHA3-based operator login, session tokens, and WebSocket auth flow with tests

title: Implement token, injection, and shell commands
description: In crates/teamserver: handlers for COMMAND_TOKEN (steal/impersonate/revert), COMMAND_INJECT_DLL (inject DLL into remote process with shellcode), shell command execution, run (execute with output capture), and spawn (new process with token). Serialize jobs correctly per Demon binary protocol.
close_reason: Implemented COMMAND_INJECT_DLL and COMMAND_SPAWNDLL encoder+callback handlers, extended proc_subcommand for Modules/Grep/Memory, added parse_memory_protection and optional_u32 helpers. 13 new tests covering all new handlers and encoders.

title: test: add unit tests for ListenerProtocol::as_str and Display
description:
**File**: `common/src/domain.rs`
**Functions with no tests**:
- `ListenerProtocol::as_str` (line 41) — returns "http", "smb", or "dns" for each variant
- `ListenerProtocol` Display impl (line 50) — delegates to `as_str`; controls how the protocol appears in database records and log output

**Why this matters**: `as_str()` is used directly in teamserver database writes (`database.rs:659,673`) to persist the protocol label. If a new variant is added and `as_str` returns the wrong string, the database will store an incorrect protocol label that is then unrecognisable on read-back. No existing test checks the actual string values returned.

**Minimum test scenarios needed**:
1. `ListenerProtocol::Http.as_str()` == "http"
2. `ListenerProtocol::Smb.as_str()` == "smb"
3. `ListenerProtocol::Dns.as_str()` == "dns"
4. `format!("{}", ListenerProtocol::Http)` == "http" (Display delegates to as_str)

**Test location**: inline `#[cfg(test)]` in `common/src/domain.rs`

title: test: GET /webhooks (get_webhook_stats) endpoint has no tests
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/api.rs
**Gap type**: existence

The `get_webhook_stats` handler (line 2623) is registered at `GET /webhooks` and returns `WebhookStats` (which includes an optional `discord` field with a failure count). There are zero tests for this endpoint.

Two meaningful paths exist:
1. Webhooks disabled (no Discord URL configured in profile): response should have `discord: null`.
2. Webhooks enabled: response should have `discord.failures` as a non-negative integer.

Without tests, a serialization change (e.g., renaming the `discord` key or removing the field) would not be caught before deployment.

**Suggested test approach**:
1. With the default test profile (no Discord webhook URL), issue `GET /webhooks` with a valid API key and assert 200 + `body["discord"] == null`.
2. Optionally, construct an `AuditWebhookNotifier` with a Discord URL configured and assert `body["discord"]["failures"] == 0` (or similar).

title: test: add unit tests for dispatch/transfer format helpers (byte_count, transfer_progress_text, transfer_state_name)
description:
**File**: `teamserver/src/dispatch/transfer.rs`
**Functions with no tests**:
- `fn transfer_progress_text` (line 379) — formats download progress as a percentage string (handles zero-total edge case)
- `fn transfer_state_name` (line 387) — maps a u32 state code to a human-readable name; returns "Unknown" for unrecognised values
- `fn byte_count` (line 396) — converts a byte count to a human-readable string with SI unit suffix (B, kB, MB, GB, TB)

**Why this matters**: These pure formatting functions are called in the transfer list output rendered for operators. A rounding error in `byte_count` or a wrong string in `transfer_state_name` would silently corrupt the file-transfer status table shown to operators — no other code validates the output.

**Minimum test scenarios needed**:
1. `byte_count`: 0 → "0 B"; 999 → "999 B"; 1000 → "1.00 kB"; 1_000_000 → "1.00 MB"; 1_000_000_000_000 → "1.00 TB"
2. `transfer_progress_text`: total=0 → "0.00%"; progress=50, total=100 → "50.00%"; progress=1, total=3 → "33.33%"
3. `transfer_state_name`: state=1 → "Running"; state=2 → "Stopped"; state=3 → "Removed"; state=99 → "Unknown"

**Test location**: inline `#[cfg(test)]` in `teamserver/src/dispatch/transfer.rs`
close_reason: Added 13 inline unit tests for byte_count, transfer_progress_text, and transfer_state_name covering all required scenarios

title: test: database.rs AgentRepository::update and list_active have no inline tests
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/database.rs
**Gap type**: existence

`database.rs` is 1696 lines with dozens of repository methods across `AgentRepository`, `ListenerRepository`, `LootRepository`, `AuditLogRepository`, `OperatorRepository`, and `AgentResponseRepository`. The inline test module contains only 3 tests covering two private helpers (`bool_from_i64`, `i64_from_u64`). All repository method coverage depends entirely on integration tests in `teamserver/tests/database.rs`.

Two specific gaps that are not covered by any existing issue or test file:

1. **`AgentRepository::update`** — updating an agent record and then reading it back should return the new values. A subtle bug (wrong SQL column binding) would silently persist stale data.
2. **`AgentRepository::list_active`** — should return only agents where `active = true`. If the WHERE clause were dropped or inverted, dead agents would appear as active, which would affect operator UI and liveness checks.

**Suggested test approach**:
In `teamserver/tests/database.rs`, create two agents (one active, one inactive), call `list_active`, and assert only the active agent is returned. Update the active agent's hostname, re-read it, and assert the new hostname is returned.

title: test: SMB listener duplicate DEMON_INIT must preserve original agent key
description:
**File**: `teamserver/tests/smb_listener.rs`
**Gap**: The HTTP listener has `http_listener_pipeline_rejects_duplicate_init_preserves_original_key` (line 354 of http_listener_pipeline.rs) but the SMB listener has no corresponding test.

**Why this matters**: A second DEMON_INIT from the same agent ID over SMB could overwrite the stored AES key if the SMB path does not check for an already-registered agent. This would break all subsequent communication with the legitimate agent (key clobber), or worse — allow key replacement.

**Functions under test**: SMB DEMON_INIT handler → `AgentRegistry::register` duplicate-detection branch.

**Minimum test scenarios needed**:
1. Complete DEMON_INIT for agent A with key K1.
2. Send a second DEMON_INIT for the same agent ID with a different key K2.
3. Send a subsequent callback encrypted with K1 — it must be decrypted correctly (K1 was retained).
4. Assert no second agent-registration event is emitted.

**Test location**: `teamserver/tests/smb_listener.rs`

title: API key lookup uses non-constant-time comparison — timing oracle
description: In crates/teamserver/src/api.rs:127, the authenticate() method looks up API keys via BTreeMap::get(presented_key). BTreeMap string comparisons are NOT constant-time: they short-circuit on the first differing byte. An attacker with high-resolution network timing (e.g. co-located on the same LAN) could potentially enumerate valid key prefixes character by character. Fix: hash the presented key with HMAC-SHA256 (keyed with a per-process secret) before inserting into the BTreeMap, and hash the presented key the same way before lookup. Alternatively, store a SHA-256 (or bcrypt/argon2) digest of each configured key value in the map and compare digests. The subtle crate's ConstantTimeEq is unsuitable here since map lookup itself branches early; the pre-hashing approach is the idiomatic solution for bearer token comparison.
close_reason: Hashed configured API keys with per-process HMAC-SHA256 digests before lookup and added auth regression tests

title: Implement Python script manager UI
description: In crates/client: script manager panel. List loaded .py scripts with status (loaded/error). Load/unload/reload buttons. Script output console (stdout/stderr from scripts). File picker to load a new script. Error display for import failures. Status indicator showing how many commands each script registered.
close_reason: Implemented the client Python script manager panel with runtime-backed load, unload, reload, status, and output handling.

title: test: add happy-path coverage for SocketRelayManager public lifecycle APIs
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/sockets.rs
**Functions with no tests**:
- `fn remove_socks_server` (line 232) — stops one SOCKS5 listener and closes any clients on that port.
- `fn write_client_data` (line 285) — forwards reverse-proxy bytes from the agent to a connected SOCKS client.
- `fn close_client` (line 357) — removes a connected SOCKS client and shuts down its local socket.

**Why this matters**: current coverage only exercises these methods on missing-agent or missing-socket error paths. The success path is part of the public SOCKS relay contract; a regression here would break data relay or leave local sockets open without any test catching it.

**Minimum test scenarios needed**:
1. Happy path: create a tracked client, write bytes through `write_client_data`, and assert the local SOCKS client receives them.
2. Error path: remove a live server with `remove_socks_server` and verify the method returns the expected close message while client state is removed.
3. Edge case: `close_client` on an established client removes state and closes the writer so subsequent writes fail.

**Test location**: inline `#[cfg(test)]` in `teamserver/src/sockets.rs` or `teamserver/tests/<name>.rs`

**Note**: this complements existing open issues for `finish_connect` and full SOCKS relay integration; it is specifically about the remaining public API success paths.
close_reason: Added three happy-path tests: write_client_data_delivers_bytes_to_local_socks_client, remove_socks_server_returns_close_message_and_removes_client_state, and close_client_removes_state_and_shuts_down_writer. All 533 tests pass.

title: test: kill_date > i64::MAX path in parse_init_agent has no coverage
description:
Introduced by: Codex <noreply@openai.com>

teamserver/src/demon.rs — tests module

## What is missing

The only kill_date test vectors in parse_init_agent tests use 1_893_456_000, which is well
within the i64 range. There is no test for:
1. kill_date == 0 (no kill date)
2. kill_date == u64::MAX or any value > i64::MAX — the overflow path at line 319 is never
   exercised

## Why it matters

The overflow fallback `unwrap_or(i64::MAX)` (red-cell-c2-2njj) is untested, so any fix to
that line would go unvalidated. Tests in this area drive detection of protocol handling
regressions.

## Fix

Add a test case that builds a DEMON_INIT packet with kill_date = u64::MAX and asserts the
current behavior (i64::MAX stored) or the corrected behavior (reject the packet). Add a test
for kill_date == 0 asserting None is stored.
close_reason: Added DEMON_INIT kill_date tests for zero and u64::MAX edge cases.

title: SOCKS relay write_client_data failure silently dropped in socket callback handler
description:
Introduced by: Codex

crates/teamserver/src/dispatch.rs:2955

In the DemonSocketCommand::Read branch:
  let _ = sockets.write_client_data(agent_id, socket_id, &data).await;

Errors from write_client_data (broken pipe, unknown socket ID, relay
task exited) are completely discarded.  The SOCKS client that sent the
request never receives the data and receives no error signal.  The
operator session has no visibility into the delivery failure.

This contrasts with the error handling directly above (lines 2942–2950)
which does broadcast an operator error event when the agent reports a read
failure.

Fix: log a warning (or broadcast an operator error event) when
write_client_data returns Err, consistent with the error path immediately
above it.
close_reason: Dispatcher now logs and broadcasts reverse-proxy relay delivery failures, with regression coverage.

title: test: encrypt_for_agent and decrypt_from_agent return AgentNotFound for unknown agent
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/agents.rs
**Gap type**: error path

`AgentRegistry::encrypt_for_agent` and `decrypt_from_agent` are crypto operations called on the agent's per-session CTR keystream. Both should return `TeamserverError::AgentNotFound` when called with an agent_id that does not exist in the registry (e.g., after the agent is removed mid-session).

No test verifies this error path. A regression that returned garbled data or panicked instead of an error would silently corrupt session state without failing any test.

This matters because `encrypt_for_agent` is called in the hot path of every outbound task delivery — if the error path is broken, a race between agent removal and job dispatch would go undetected.

**Suggested test approach**:
Create a registry, do NOT insert any agent, then call `encrypt_for_agent(0x1234, b"data")` and assert the result matches `Err(TeamserverError::AgentNotFound { agent_id: 0x1234 })`. Repeat for `decrypt_from_agent`.

title: test: AuditWebhookNotifier::shutdown returns true immediately when notifier is disabled
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/webhook.rs
**Gap type**: error path / correctness

When no webhook is configured (`is_enabled() == false`), `notify_audit_record_detached` is a no-op and `pending` stays at zero. Calling `shutdown` in this state should return `true` immediately. This is not tested.

A bug here (e.g., the drain loop accidentally blocking when pending=0) would hang teamserver shutdown in configurations without a webhook — only observable in a clean-shutdown scenario without Discord configured.

**Suggested test approach**:
Create a notifier from a profile with no webhook block, call `shutdown(Duration::from_secs(1))`, assert it returns `true` without waiting for the full timeout.
close_reason: Added test: shutdown_returns_true_immediately_when_notifier_is_disabled verifies no hang when pending=0 and no webhook is configured

title: test: resolve_logging_config whitespace-only RUST_LOG env var is silently ignored — not tested
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/logging.rs
**Gap type**: edge case

`resolve_logging_config` explicitly trims and filters the RUST_LOG env var:
```rust
env::var(EnvFilter::DEFAULT_ENV).ok().filter(|value| !value.trim().is_empty())
```
A whitespace-only value (e.g. `RUST_LOG=" "`) should be ignored and fall through to the profile or default. This edge case is not covered by any test.

Additionally, the no-debug / no-profile / no-env-override baseline case (should produce `filter_directive="info"` and `format=LogFormat::Json`) is never tested directly — it is only implied by tests that set other parameters. A regression changing this default would not be caught.

**Suggested test approach**:
In the `resolve_logging_config_with_override` tests, add: (1) a call with `rust_log_override=Some("  ".to_owned())` and assert it falls back to the profile/default level; (2) a call with `(None, false, None)` and assert `filter_directive=="info"` and `format==LogFormat::Json`.
close_reason: Added logging tests for whitespace-only overrides and the non-debug default config baseline.

title: test: mock_demon_agent_checkin large boilerplate duplication hides which setup step fails
description:
**File**: teamserver/tests/mock_demon_agent_checkin.rs
**Gap type**: quality

Both test functions (`mock_demon_checkin_get_job_and_output_flow` and `reconnect_then_subsequent_callback_remains_synchronised`) contain ~50 lines of identical `TeamserverState` construction code that duplicates what `common::spawn_test_server` already provides.  The code even hard-codes port numbers (40056, 40057) which can cause port conflicts if tests run in parallel.

When the shared setup is duplicated, a panic in the setup phase produces an unhelpful error that does not distinguish a setup failure from the behaviour being tested.  The duplication also means any change to `TeamserverState` must be applied in three places.

**Suggested test approach**:
Replace the inline `TeamserverState` construction in both tests with a call to `common::spawn_test_server()` (already used in `e2e_operator_agent_session.rs`), removing the hard-coded port numbers and collapsing ~100 lines of duplication.

title: Define shared error types and agent/listener structs
description: In crates/common: define the shared domain types used across teamserver and client. AgentInfo struct (all fields from TS_Agents DB schema: AgentID, Hostname, Username, DomainName, IPs, PID, ProcessName, Arch, Elevated, OSVersion, SleepDelay, Jitter, FirstCallIn, LastCallIn, etc). ListenerConfig enum (Http, Smb, External) with full config fields. OperatorInfo. Custom error types using thiserror.
close_reason: Implemented shared common domain types and thiserror-based validation errors

title: test: sweep_records_agent_dead_audit_entry uses yield_now() to await background task — may be flaky on loaded CI
description:
Introduced by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

teamserver/src/agent_liveness.rs — sweep_records_agent_dead_audit_entry test (near line 297)

The agent.dead audit write is spawned as a tokio::spawn background task. The test waits for it using two back-to-back tokio::task::yield_now() calls:

    tokio::task::yield_now().await;
    tokio::task::yield_now().await;

Two yields are sufficient for an in-memory SQLite write under normal conditions, but the number of task switches required is not guaranteed. On a loaded CI worker the spawned task may not complete before the assertion runs, producing a spurious 'expected agent.dead audit entry' failure.

Preferred fix: use a short tokio::time::timeout loop polling audit_log().list() until the entry appears, or accept the database as a parameter from the caller so the write can be made synchronous in tests.
close_reason: replaced flaky yield_now() pair with polling timeout loop (500 ms) in sweep_records_agent_dead_audit_entry test

title: completeness: audit log records operator actions but not agent lifecycle events (registration, death, checkin)
description:
Introduced by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

teamserver/src/audit.rs — AuditLog::record_operator_action() is called for operator logins, command tasking, listener management.

Agent lifecycle events (new agent registration via DemonInit, CHECKIN received, agent marked stale/dead, agent deleted) are broadcast over the EventBus and persisted to the agents table, but do not generate audit log entries with timestamps.

For a red team C2 framework used in regulated engagements, a complete audit trail must include when each agent first appeared, when it last checked in, and when it was decommissioned.

Fix: call record_operator_action (or a new record_agent_event variant) at DemonInit registration, agent death marking, and agent deletion, capturing the agent ID, external IP, and responsible operator (where applicable).
close_reason: Added agent.registered, agent.checkin, and agent.dead audit events at DemonInit registration, COMMAND_CHECKIN callback, and liveness-sweep death marking respectively. spawn_agent_liveness_monitor gains a Database parameter. Three new integration tests verify the audit entries.

title: test: negotiate_socks5 rejection paths are not unit-tested
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/sockets.rs
**Gap type**: error path

The `negotiate_socks5` function has two rejection paths that are not unit-tested:
1. A client sending SOCKS version != 5 (returns `InvalidData` IO error)
2. A client that advertises only auth methods that do *not* include NO_AUTH (0x00) — the server must send `0xFF` and close, but there is no test verifying this.

These are security-relevant: a mis-implemented SOCKS5 negotiation path could allow an attacker to side-step the no-auth requirement or trigger unexpected server behavior. The existing tests (`socks_server_lifecycle_commands_track_state`) only exercise the happy path where a compliant client connects and the full session succeeds.

**Suggested test approach**:
Create a loopback TCP pair (same pattern as `connected_write_half_and_reader`), write a raw byte stream with SOCKS version 4 (or offer method 0x02 only), call `negotiate_socks5` directly, and assert it returns an `Err` with `InvalidData` / `PermissionDenied` kind respectively.
close_reason: Added two unit tests: negotiate_socks5_rejects_wrong_version (SOCKS version != 5 → InvalidData) and negotiate_socks5_rejects_auth_only_methods (no NO_AUTH method offered → PermissionDenied + 0xFF response)

title: Mutex::unwrap() in auth test-only Argon2 cache can panic under test parallelism
description:
Introduced by: Claude Sonnet 4.6

teamserver/src/auth.rs:552 and 559 — The test-only Argon2 verifier cache uses a std::sync::Mutex::unwrap() without poison handling:



The workspace lint rule unwrap_used = deny is configured but this code lives inside a #[cfg(test)] block. Cargo.toml does not exempt test code from the lint. If a test panics while holding the lock, the mutex becomes poisoned and every subsequent test that tries to acquire it will also panic with a different error message, making the root cause hard to diagnose and potentially causing many tests to fail for an unrelated reason.

While this only affects test infrastructure, the mutation can cascade across the full test suite run.

Fix: Use cache.lock().unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner()) to recover from poison, or use parking_lot::Mutex which does not poison.
close_reason: Replaced .unwrap() with .unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner()) on both mutex lock calls in password_verifier_for_sha3_cached to handle poison without cascading test failures

title: test: POST /listeners (create_listener) success body and duplicate-name rejection untested
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/api.rs
**Gap type**: error path + quality

The `create_listener` handler (line 1850) returns 201 CREATED with a `ListenerSummary` body on success. The only implicit check is the assertion `assert_eq!(create_response.status(), StatusCode::CREATED)` inside `update_listener_replaces_config`, which uses it as test setup. There is no dedicated test that:
- Verifies the full 201 response body shape (name, protocol, status fields).
- Tests creating a listener with a duplicate name returns a 4xx error.
- Tests that an invalid config (missing required fields, bad pipe name, etc.) returns 400.

The duplicate-name path writes an audit entry on failure (line 1860-1878); without a test this audit path is also untested.

**Suggested test approach**:
1. POST a valid SMB listener config, assert 201, inspect `body["name"]`, `body["protocol"]`, `body["status"]`.
2. POST the same config a second time, assert 4xx with an error code indicating duplicate/already-exists.
3. POST with an empty `name` field and assert 400.

title: Implement session graph (node visualization)
description: In crates/client: egui node graph visualization of agent topology. Nodes represent agents, edges represent pivot relationships (SMB links). Drag-to-pan, scroll-to-zoom. Node color by status (active/dead). Click node to open agent console. Use egui_graphs crate or implement custom with egui Painter. Show teamserver as root node.
close_reason: Implemented an interactive egui session graph with pivot topology, pan/zoom controls, status colors, and console-opening node clicks.

title: silent 0-progress event for downloads > usize::MAX bytes
description:
Files: teamserver/src/dispatch/filesystem.rs:205,242,517 and teamserver/src/dispatch/transfer.rs:344 (BeaconOutput FileWrite path)
What is wrong: `u64::try_from(state.data.len()).unwrap_or_default()` silently emits a download-progress event with `received_bytes=0` whenever `state.data.len()` exceeds `usize::MAX` on a 64-bit target.  On any real 64-bit system `usize::MAX == u64::MAX`, so the conversion never fails in practice, but the fallback is semantically wrong — it would report 0 bytes received instead of an error.
Why it matters: Operator UI would display 0% progress for a large download instead of the correct figure.  The silent failure also hides if a future architecture change made `usize` narrower than `u64`.
Fix: On a 64-bit build `u64::from` or an explicit cast `state.data.len() as u64` is always safe and removes the dead fallback path.  Alternatively, gate behind `#[cfg(target_pointer_width = "64")]`.
Attribution: Codex (a849a947 — feat: capture agent loot and credentials; b6211d9f — refactor: split dispatch into per-command modules)
close_reason: Replaced u64::try_from(...).unwrap_or_default() with direct 'as u64' cast at all four sites (filesystem.rs:205,242,517 and transfer.rs:344). Removes the silent 0-byte fallback; cast is always lossless on 64-bit targets.

title: test: add unit tests for AgentResponseRepository CRUD and edge cases
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/database.rs (AgentResponseRepository, lines 1055-1165)
**Functions with no inline tests**:
- `fn list_for_agent` (line 1102) — returns all responses for a given agent_id; no test for empty result or unknown agent
- `fn list` (line 1117) — returns all responses across all agents; untested ordering guarantee
- `fn delete` (line 1127) — deletes by id; no test verifying delete of non-existent id is a no-op

**Why this matters**: The agent-response table stores every task output written by the dispatch layer. A silent ORM regression (e.g., wrong column binding) could cause response data loss without any test failure. The `list_for_agent` path is called by the REST API on every loot/session-output query.

**Minimum test scenarios needed**:
1. Happy path: insert a response, list_for_agent returns it, list returns it, delete removes it, get returns None.
2. Error path: list_for_agent with unknown agent_id returns an empty Vec (not an error).
3. Edge case: delete of a non-existent id is a silent no-op (SQLite DELETE WHERE returns 0 rows affected but no error).

**Test location**: `teamserver/tests/database.rs` (inline with existing AgentRepository tests)
close_reason: Added three tests: list_for_agent with unknown agent returns empty vec, list ordering across multiple agents, delete of non-existent id is a silent no-op

title: Audit webhook delivery can stall operator/API actions indefinitely
description:
Introduced by: Codex <noreply@openai.com>

crates/teamserver/src/webhook.rs:31 constructs a default reqwest client with no request timeout, and crates/teamserver/src/audit.rs:300-313 awaits webhook delivery inline after persisting each audit record. The same synchronous path is used from API handlers in crates/teamserver/src/api.rs:2393-2414 and WebSocket/operator flows in crates/teamserver/src/websocket.rs:2311-2332.

If the configured Discord endpoint accepts the TCP/TLS connection but never responds, operator login, chat, listener actions, and API tasking can hang behind the webhook call even though the notifier is documented as best-effort. Correct behavior: outbound audit notifications should be bounded by a short timeout and/or dispatched off the request path so an unavailable webhook cannot block teamserver control-plane operations.
close_reason: Bound audit webhook delivery with a timeout and moved notification dispatch off the control-plane request path.

title: test: handle_net_callback untested subcommand dispatch paths (Sessions, Share, Logons, Group, Domain)
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/dispatch/network.rs
**Functions with no tests**:
- `fn handle_net_callback` (line 7) — handler for DemonCommand::CommandNet

**Untested subcommands** (only `DemonNetCommand::Users` is exercised by the existing test in dispatch/mod.rs):
- `DemonNetCommand::Sessions` (line 47) — parses target + row tuples of (client, user, active_time, idle_time) and calls `format_net_sessions`
- `DemonNetCommand::Logons` (line 31) — parses target + UTF-16 username list; formats inline
- `DemonNetCommand::Share` (line 65) — parses target + row tuples of (name, path, remark, access) and calls `format_net_shares`
- `DemonNetCommand::LocalGroup` (line 82) — parses target + (name, description) pairs and calls `format_net_group_descriptions`
- `DemonNetCommand::Group` (line 97) — same shape as LocalGroup
- `DemonNetCommand::Domain` (line 23) — parses single string; two branches (empty = not joined, non-empty = domain name)
- `DemonNetCommand::Computer` / `DemonNetCommand::DcList` (line 64) — return `Ok(None)`; no test that confirms this

**Why this matters**: Each subcommand has its own binary parsing loop. A regression (wrong field count, wrong read method) would produce garbled or panic output. The existing `builtin_net_and_transfer_handlers_format_operator_output` test only covers the Users path — 7 of 8 handler branches have zero test coverage.

**Minimum test scenarios needed**:
1. Sessions: payload with 2 session rows → assert output contains formatted session table
2. Share: payload with 1 share row → assert output contains share name and path
3. Logons: payload with user list → assert output lists each username
4. Group / LocalGroup: payload with 2 rows → assert output contains both group names
5. Domain: non-empty domain → assert info message; empty domain → assert 'not joined' message
6. Computer / DcList: any payload → assert handler returns None (no operator event)

**Test location**: inline `#[cfg(test)]` in teamserver/src/dispatch/mod.rs alongside `builtin_net_and_transfer_handlers_format_operator_output`

title: quality: AgentInfo name collision — two structs with same name in red_cell_common
description:
Introduced by: Michel Klomp (Claude Sonnet 4.6)

common/src/domain.rs:283 — persistence model, agent_id is u32
common/src/operator.rs:340 — WebSocket wire format, agent_id is hex String

Both structs are named AgentInfo in the same crate. Callers must import one as
DomainAgentInfo (see api.rs:21) to disambiguate. New code written against either type can
silently use the wrong one (e.g. passing the wire type to a DB function expecting the
domain type) and the compiler only catches this at use-sites, not at the import site.

Fix: rename one of these types. Suggestions:
  - domain.rs: AgentRecord (persistence layer)
  - operator.rs: AgentWireInfo or AgentNotification (WebSocket wire layer)
close_reason: Renamed the persisted common agent model to AgentRecord and updated teamserver call sites to remove the AgentInfo collision.

title: dev01-claude completed red-cell-c2-37rn but never closed the issue
description:
Introduced by: Ubuntu-C2-dev01-claude

The three test scenarios required by red-cell-c2-37rn were fully implemented in commit 1720f08 ('wip: interrupted red-cell-c2-37rn'), covering:
- listener_repository_update_persists_config_changes
- listener_repository_update_is_a_noop_for_missing_listener
- listener_repository_exists_tracks_creation_and_deletion

All tests pass (cargo test --workspace: ok). The issue was left in IN_PROGRESS state and never closed.

**Fix**: run `br close red-cell-c2-37rn --reason="All three test scenarios implemented in 1720f08"`
close_reason: red-cell-c2-37rn was closed in commit 7261c2f — issue resolved

title: quality: query_session_activity inverts count/query order vs query_audit_log
description:
Introduced by: Cursor Agent

teamserver/src/audit.rs:445-452 — query_session_activity

query_session_activity calls query_filtered (line 445) before count_filtered (line 452), the opposite order from query_audit_log (count at 393, query at 394). This creates a narrow TOCTOU window where rows inserted between the two DB calls inflate total relative to what was already fetched.

More problematic: after the SQL fetch a redundant in-memory .filter(|entry| actions.contains(&entry.action)) is applied (line 447-449). Since action_in is already enforced in SQL via action_in: Some(actions.iter().cloned().collect()), this filter is dead code. It was almost certainly left over from a prior in-memory-only implementation that Cursor's SQL refactor did not clean up. The dead filter obscures the intent of the function and could mask a future SQL regression where action_in stops being enforced.

Fix: reorder to call count_filtered before query_filtered (consistent with query_audit_log) and remove the redundant in-memory filter.
close_reason: Reordered count_filtered before query_filtered to match query_audit_log; removed dead in-memory filter redundant with SQL action_in clause

title: protocol: pivot list command handler is a no-op — agents receive no response
description:
Introduced by: Codex <noreply@openai.com>

teamserver/src/dispatch.rs — The DemonPivotCommand::List arm in handle_pivot_callback returns Ok(None), producing no response to the agent or operator. Operators issuing a pivot list request will see no result and the UI will hang waiting.

Fix: implement the pivot list handler to query the agent registry for pivot children of the requesting agent and return the list in the Demon protocol format.
close_reason: implemented pivot list callback handler: parses (DemonId, NamedPipe) pairs from agent payload and broadcasts formatted AgentResponse operator event

title: test: HTTP listener does not reject malformed or truncated request bodies
description:
**File**: teamserver/tests/http_listener_pipeline.rs
**Gap type**: error path / protocol parsing

All HTTP listener integration tests send well-formed `DemonEnvelope` bytes.  There is no test for:
- A body shorter than the minimum envelope length (e.g., 1–3 bytes).
- A body with correct length prefix but truncated payload.
- A body with a wrong magic number (not 0xDEADBEEF).
- A zero-length body (empty POST).

If `DemonEnvelope::from_bytes` panics instead of returning an error on these inputs, the listener process crashes — effectively a DoS.  A test confirming the server responds with a non-5xx status (e.g. 400 or 404) rather than panicking would catch this regression.

**Suggested test approach**:
POST each malformed body variant to a running listener; assert the HTTP response is 404 (or 400) and that the listener is still accepting subsequent valid requests (i.e., has not crashed).

title: HTTP listener never captures real peer IP — external_ip always 0.0.0.0
description:
Introduced by: Codex

crates/teamserver/src/listeners.rs:887,900 — Both the HTTP and HTTPS listener variants call server.serve(router.into_make_service()) instead of router.into_make_service_with_connect_info::<SocketAddr>(). Because ConnectInfo is not injected into requests, the http_listener_handler has no access to the peer socket address.

crates/teamserver/src/listeners.rs:2040 — extract_external_ip() falls back to reading the x-real-ip header, which is fully agent-controlled (spoofable), then returns '0.0.0.0' if the header is absent. In practice, all HTTP-connected agents register with external_ip = '0.0.0.0' unless a proxy sets x-real-ip.

Contrast with: DNS listener correctly uses peer.ip().to_string() from recv_from(); the websocket server uses into_make_service_with_connect_info() and ConnectInfo<SocketAddr>.

Fix: switch to router.into_make_service_with_connect_info::<SocketAddr>() in spawn_http_listener_runtime, add ConnectInfo<SocketAddr> extractor to http_listener_handler, and use the socket address as the primary external IP source (with x-forwarded-for / x-real-ip as opt-in fallbacks via behind_redirector).
close_reason: Added HTTP listener regressions for peer IP capture and trusted redirector forwarding.

title: test: add round-trip tests for untested FlatInfo OperatorMessage variants
description:
**File**: common/src/operator.rs
**Functions with no tests**:
- Six `FlatInfo`-typed `OperatorMessage` variants have no round-trip coverage in `flat_info_variants_round_trip`: `ListenerEdit` (SubEvent 3), `CredentialsEdit` (SubEvent 2), `ChatMessage` (SubEvent 1), `ChatListener` (SubEvent 3), `HostFileAdd` (SubEvent 1), `AgentRemove` (SubEvent 2)
- Each variant serializes with a distinct `SubEvent` discriminant; a typo in the dispatch table silently mis-classifies messages

**Why this matters**: The Havoc operator protocol sends these events to the client GUI. If a SubEvent discriminant is wrong (e.g., ListenerEdit ↔ ListenerNew collision), the client silently renders the wrong event type. A round-trip test would catch this at compile time.

**Minimum test scenarios needed**:
1. Happy path: for each of the 6 variants, construct a JSON payload with the correct Event+SubEvent, deserialize to OperatorMessage, re-serialize, re-deserialize, and assert equality.
2. Error path: JSON with a valid EventCode but the SubEvent of a sibling variant must not decode as the wrong variant.
3. Symmetry: serialized form must carry the same numeric SubEvent code as the input JSON.

**Test location**: inline `#[cfg(test)]` in common/src/operator.rs, extending the existing `flat_info_variants_round_trip` test

title: test: DemonInitRateLimiter eviction path (MAX_DEMON_INIT_ATTEMPT_WINDOWS) is untested
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/listeners.rs
**Gap type**: edge case

`DemonInitRateLimiter::allow` includes a capacity-eviction path that triggers when the number of tracked IPs reaches `MAX_DEMON_INIT_ATTEMPT_WINDOWS` (10,000) and a new, unseen IP arrives. It calls `evict_oldest_demon_init_windows` which sorts all tracked entries by `window_start` and removes the oldest half. This path has zero test coverage.

A bug here is security-relevant: if the eviction logic fails (e.g., it removes the wrong entries, or fails to actually reduce the map size), an attacker could flood the rate limiter with unique IPs to push tracked IPs out of the table, effectively resetting other IPs' rate limits and bypassing the init throttle.

The existing tests only cover the per-IP threshold and window expiry — neither fills the table to capacity.

**Suggested test approach**:
Pre-populate the rate limiter's `windows` map with `MAX_DEMON_INIT_ATTEMPT_WINDOWS` unique IPs with distinct `window_start` timestamps. Then call `allow` for a new IP and assert: (a) the call returns `true`, (b) `tracked_ip_count()` is at most `MAX_DEMON_INIT_ATTEMPT_WINDOWS / 2 + 1`, and (c) the oldest entries were evicted (e.g., the IP with the earliest window_start is no longer present).
close_reason: Added demon_init_rate_limiter_evicts_oldest_when_at_capacity test: pre-populates to MAX_DEMON_INIT_ATTEMPT_WINDOWS IPs, verifies eviction fires on new IP, count drops to ≤MAX/2+1, oldest IP removed, new IP tracked

title: test: add round-trip tests for operator message numeric code serialization
description:
**File**: common/src/operator.rs (numeric_code! macro, lines 11-65)
**Functions with partial tests**:
- `fn as_u32` / `fn from_u32` generated by the numeric_code! macro for all 10+ operator enum types (EventCode, CommandCode, DemonSocksStatus, etc.) — existing tests cover EventCode and a few others, but most generated enums are not tested for their as_u32() / from_u32() round-trip symmetry and unknown-value rejection

**Why this matters**: The Demon binary protocol encodes command types, event types, and status codes as u32 wire values. If the macro-generated Serialize/Deserialize implementations are mis-matched (e.g., the wrong numeric literal in one variant), operator messages are silently mis-classified. This breaks agent callbacks in a way that is invisible at the type level but causes incorrect task dispatch.

**Minimum test scenarios needed**:
1. Happy path: for each enum generated by numeric_code!, verify as_u32() matches the declared literal and from_u32(as_u32(variant)) == Some(variant) for every variant.
2. Error path: from_u32 with an out-of-range value returns None (no panic).
3. Round-trip: JSON serialize an OperatorMessage variant and deserialize it; verify the EventCode survives the round-trip with the correct numeric value on the wire.

**Test location**: inline `#[cfg(test)]` in common/src/operator.rs alongside existing serialization tests

title: test: AuditQuery actor_filter precedence: operator field beats actor field when both provided
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/audit.rs
**Gap type**: edge case

`AuditQuery::actor_filter()` returns `self.operator.as_deref().or(self.actor.as_deref())`, meaning `operator` takes precedence over `actor` when both fields are populated. No test verifies this precedence — the field is only tested via the `operator` name in one test (`filtered_query_by_actor_returns_correct_subset_at_scale`).

If the precedence were accidentally reversed (returning `actor` first), an audit query with `?operator=alice&actor=bob` would silently apply the wrong filter, exposing bob's records to a query intended for alice.

**Suggested test approach**:
Seed rows for two actors. Build an `AuditQuery` with `operator = Some("alice")` and `actor = Some("bob")`, call `query_audit_log`, and assert only alice's rows are returned — confirming `operator` has precedence. Also test with only `actor` set to verify that fallback path works.

title: test: add unit tests for filesystem payload parsers (parse_file_open_header, parse_file_chunk, parse_file_close)
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/dispatch/filesystem.rs
**Functions with no tests**:
- `fn parse_file_open_header` (line 359) — parses file download open packet: reads 4-byte file_id, 4-byte size, and UTF-8 path from raw bytes
- `fn parse_file_chunk` (line 385) — parses file download chunk packet: reads 4-byte file_id and remaining bytes as chunk data
- `fn parse_file_close` (line 404) — parses file close packet: reads 4-byte file_id

**Why this matters**: These parsers sit on the hot path for every agent file download. A wrong length check or endian bug would silently corrupt downloads or panic on malformed Demon callbacks — both undetectable in the current zero-test state.

**Minimum test scenarios needed**:
1. Happy path: craft a well-formed byte slice → assert correct (file_id, size, path) tuple
2. Error path (too short): pass fewer than 4/8 bytes → assert `InvalidCallbackPayload` error
3. Edge case (parse_file_open_header): payload exactly 8 bytes (empty path) → assert empty path string
4. Edge case (parse_file_chunk): payload exactly 4 bytes → assert empty chunk vec

**Test location**: inline `#[cfg(test)]` in teamserver/src/dispatch/filesystem.rs

title: test: restore passing red_cell doctests under cargo test --workspace
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/lib.rs
**Coverage quality gap**:
- `cargo test --workspace` currently fails during `Doc-tests red_cell` because rustdoc cannot resolve the generated `pyo3` extern for `teamserver/src/plugins.rs` while testing the crate root.

**Why this matters**: a workspace-wide test run is currently red even though unit and integration tests pass. That makes doctest coverage effectively unavailable for the teamserver crate and can hide future crate-root documentation or API regressions.

**Minimum test scenarios needed**:
1. Happy path: make `cargo test --workspace` pass with `Doc-tests red_cell` enabled.
2. Error path: add a regression check or documented reproduction for the plugin-enabled rustdoc configuration that previously failed.
3. Edge case: confirm the fix works in a clean workspace without relying on stale build artifacts.

**Test location**: crate-root doctest path for `teamserver/src/lib.rs` plus any supporting workspace test configuration needed to keep doctests running in CI.
close_reason: Verified red_cell doctests pass under cargo test --workspace and crate-root plugin regression coverage is in place.

title: Implement pivot management (SMB links)
description: In crates/teamserver: manage agent pivot relationships. When an agent reports an SMB pivot link, store parent/child relationship in ts_links table and registry. Route jobs for child agents through parent agent's job queue (encapsulated in pivot packet format). Handle pivot chain teardown when parent agent dies.
close_reason: Implemented SMB pivot link persistence, routed child jobs through pivot packets, and added recursive link teardown on parent death.

title: test: compute_cache_key has no test for input-distinctness (collision resistance)
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/payload_builder.rs
**Gap type**: existence / security

`compute_cache_key` produces a SHA-256 content-addressed cache key from four inputs: teamserver version, architecture (`x64`/`x86`), output format (EXE/DLL/shellcode/etc.), config bytes, and an optional `BinaryConfig`. There are **no unit tests** verifying that different inputs produce different cache keys.

This matters from a security standpoint: if two different build configurations ever map to the same cache key — due to a bug in the hash construction such as missing a separator byte between fields, swapping field order, or omitting the binary patch from the hash — a stale payload built for one configuration could be returned for a different configuration. For example, a 32-bit payload could be served when a 64-bit one is requested, or a payload with a different C2 endpoint could be returned.

**Suggested test approach**:
Call `compute_cache_key` with several pairs of inputs that differ in exactly one dimension (arch, format, config bytes, binary_patch present/absent) and assert that all resulting keys are distinct. Also verify that identical inputs produce the same key (determinism).

title: consistency: DEMON_MAX_RESPONSE_LENGTH and MAX_AGENT_REQUEST_BODY_LEN are the same cap defined in two places
description:
Introduced by: Codex <noreply@openai.com>

teamserver/src/websocket.rs:45  — const DEMON_MAX_RESPONSE_LENGTH = 0x01E0_0000 (30 MiB)
teamserver/src/listeners.rs     — const MAX_AGENT_REQUEST_BODY_LEN = 0x01E0_0000 (30 MiB)

These are the same enforcement limit applied in different directions (operator console vs agent HTTP handler). If one is updated without the other, the effective cap silently diverges.

Fix: define once in teamserver/src/lib.rs and import in both modules.
close_reason: Defined MAX_AGENT_MESSAGE_LEN once in lib.rs; removed local DEMON_MAX_RESPONSE_LENGTH and MAX_AGENT_REQUEST_BODY_LEN constants from websocket.rs and listeners.rs respectively and imported the shared constant instead.

title: Implement agent response handler (CALLBACK_*)
description: In crates/teamserver: handle all agent callback response types: CALLBACK_OUTPUT (command stdout/result), CALLBACK_ERROR (error message), CALLBACK_FILE (file download chunk with offset/total). Match response to original request ID. Format output and broadcast AgentResponse event to operators. Handle multi-chunk file downloads (reassemble). Persist output to DB for history.
close_reason: Persisted agent callback response history, request correlation, and callback output/error handling.

title: test: add integration tests for AgentRegistry::set_last_call_in agent revival and persistence
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/agents.rs (set_last_call_in, lines 659-696)
**Functions with partial tests**:
- `fn set_last_call_in` (line 659) — existing tests cover the normal update and the dead-agent revival (active=true, reason cleared); NOT tested: (a) a dead agent with a non-empty reason field reviving correctly clears the reason in the SQLite row, (b) calling set_last_call_in on an unknown agent_id returns AgentNotFound

**Why this matters**: The last_call_in timestamp is the sole input to the liveness monitor timeout calculation. If a dead agent's revival does not clear the reason column in SQLite (only in memory), the database and in-memory state diverge. After a teamserver restart, the agent appears dead in the UI with a stale reason, even though it actually checked in.

**Minimum test scenarios needed**:
1. Happy path: insert an active agent; call set_last_call_in with a new timestamp; verify both in-memory and persisted last_call_in match the new value.
2. Happy path: mark agent dead with a reason; call set_last_call_in; verify active=true and reason is empty in both memory and the persisted row (reload via AgentRegistry::load).
3. Error path: call set_last_call_in for an unknown agent_id; verify TeamserverError::AgentNotFound.

**Test location**: inline `#[cfg(test)]` in teamserver/src/agents.rs alongside existing set_last_call_in tests

title: Webhook Discord URL not validated as HTTPS — SSRF via HTTP redirect possible
description:
Introduced by: Claude Sonnet 4.6

common/src/config.rs:239-243 and teamserver/src/webhook.rs:217-228 — The webhook Discord URL is validated only to be non-empty. No check enforces that the scheme is https:// or that the host is discord.com / discordapp.com. An operator or configuration file author can set the URL to:
- http://internal-service/ (unencrypted, bypasses TLS)
- http://169.254.169.254/ (IMDS endpoint on cloud VMs — metadata exfil)
- http://localhost:8080/ (loopback — internal service access)

The reqwest client in discord_webhook_client() has no redirect-follow restrictions either, so an attacker who controls a DNS entry pointed at the webhook URL can redirect to internal services.

This is a Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) risk. While Discord webhook URLs are operator-configured, misconfiguration or insider threat allows the teamserver to be used as an HTTP proxy to internal services.

Fix: Validate that the webhook URL scheme is https and optionally restrict to known Discord hostnames in Profile::validate(). Also set reqwest::ClientBuilder::redirect(Policy::none()) to prevent SSRF via redirects.
close_reason: Validated Discord webhook URL: requires https:// scheme and restricts host to known Discord hostnames. Set reqwest redirect policy to none() to prevent SSRF via redirects.

title: test: LoginRateLimiter lockout and window-reset behavior are untested
description:
Introduced by: Codex <noreply@openai.com>

teamserver/src/websocket.rs — LoginRateLimiter limits to 5 failed login attempts per IP per 60-second window, but no test exercises the lockout condition: submitting 5 incorrect passwords and verifying the 6th attempt is rejected, then verifying the window resets after 60 seconds.

Without this test, a regression that disables the rate limiter or miscalculates the window would pass CI silently.

Fix: add unit tests for LoginRateLimiter covering: (1) 5th attempt allowed, 6th rejected; (2) window reset after expiry; (3) different IPs tracked independently.
close_reason: Added login_rate_limiter_window_reset_allows_after_expiry test covering the is_allowed() expiry code path

title: Implement file system commands
description: In crates/teamserver: file system command handlers. Upload (send file to agent in chunks), Download (receive file from agent via CALLBACK_FILE, reassemble, persist to loot), directory listing, file delete, file copy, directory navigation. Track download progress and broadcast to operators.
close_reason: Implemented Havoc-compatible filesystem task encoding, upload chunk staging, and download callback persistence/broadcasting

title: DownloadTracker accumulates data without a total-size cap — OOM from rogue agent
description:
Introduced by: Codex

crates/teamserver/src/dispatch.rs:787–811 (DownloadTracker) and
lines ~1319–1365 (FileWrite handler)

DownloadTracker.append() unconditionally calls
data.extend_from_slice(chunk) with no check on total accumulated size.
Downloads complete only when the agent sends FileClose, not when
data.len() reaches expected_size.  expected_size is used only for
progress display.

An adversary who controls a Demon agent (e.g., blue-team capture, modified
implant) can:
  1. Send DemonCallback::File with expected_size = u64::MAX.
  2. Send repeated DemonCallback::FileWrite packets (each capped at 30 MiB
     by MAX_AGENT_REQUEST_BODY_LEN per request).
  3. Never send DemonCallback::FileClose.
  4. Over N requests the teamserver allocates N × 30 MiB → OOM.

Since each FileWrite arrives as a separate authenticated HTTP request, the
per-request body cap does not protect the per-download accumulation buffer.

Fix: add a configurable max_download_bytes cap in DownloadTracker.append()
and return an error / drop the download when exceeded.
close_reason: Implemented in existing HEAD by acfa632 (download cap) with coverage verified in current workspace

title: AES-CTR block offset lost on teamserver restart — reconnect probe sends ack at wrong counter
description:
Introduced by: Cursor (Ubuntu-HTB-cursor, working on red-cell-c2-2bm)

crates/teamserver/src/agents.rs:61,69 — AgentEntry.ctr_block_offset
crates/teamserver/src/agents.rs:110-133 — AgentRegistry::load

ctr_block_offset is initialized to Mutex::new(0) in AgentEntry::new(), which is called from AgentRegistry::load(). After a teamserver restart, all agents' CTR offsets reset to 0, but the agent processes have their counters at position N (accumulated across all prior exchanges).

Two failure modes:
1. Reconnect probe (empty DEMON_INIT): server sends build_init_ack encrypted at CTR=0 instead of the agent's expected CTR=N. Agent decrypts garbage as the ack.
2. Direct callbacks (no re-init): server tries to decrypt at CTR=0 while agent sends data encrypted at CTR=N. All decryption fails.

In the Havoc protocol agents normally re-send a full DEMON_INIT when reconnecting (which would reset CTR on both sides), but the reconnect probe path (empty DEMON_INIT body) does not trigger a full re-init and is therefore broken after a restart.

Fix: Either persist ctr_block_offset to the ts_agents table and reload on startup, or explicitly invalidate the CTR state on reconnect to force a full re-init.
close_reason: Added restart regression coverage for reconnect ack and post-restart callback parsing under restored Havoc stateless AES-CTR semantics.

title: test: agent_liveness AgentLivenessConfig::timeout_for when override=0 or override=u64::MAX
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/agent_liveness.rs
**Gap type**: edge case

`AgentLivenessConfig::timeout_for` returns `self.timeout_override_secs.unwrap_or_else(…)`. The existing tests verify a non-zero override (30 s) and the zero-sleep fallback, but do not test:
1. **Override = 0**: A profile with `AgentTimeoutSecs = 0` would give every agent a 0-second timeout, causing them to be marked dead on the very next sweep tick. This is probably invalid but the code doesn't guard against it.
2. **Very large override**: `AgentTimeoutSecs` set to a large value (near `u64::MAX / 3`) could cause the timeout calculation to overflow when multiplied by 3 in `from_profile`. The code uses `saturating_mul(3)` for the default path but the override goes straight through; the sweep interval clamp also uses division by 3, which is safe.
3. **Agent sleep_delay = u16::MAX**: Casting `u16::MAX` (65535) via `u64::from` should be safe, but this is not explicitly tested in `timeout_for`.

**Suggested test approach**:
Add tests for `timeout_override_secs = Some(0)` in `AgentLivenessConfig` — verify the resulting sweep interval is the minimum (1 s) and that `timeout_for` returns 0. Separately test an agent with `sleep_delay = u16::MAX` and verify `timeout_for` returns the expected `65535 * 3 = 196605`.

title: loot export: csv_field doesn't quote values containing bare \r
description:
Introduced by: Claude Sonnet 4.6

client/src/main.rs:3882 — the `csv_field` function checks for `,`, `"`, and `\n` but not `\r`. A loot field value containing a bare carriage return (without a newline) passes through unquoted, embedding a raw `\r` in the CSV that splits the row on Windows-style line-ending parsers.

Reproduced by: a loot item whose `source` or `preview` field contains `\r` but no `\n` — the resulting CSV row is truncated at the carriage return in Excel / LibreOffice.

Fix: add `value.contains('\r')` to the quoting condition in `csv_field`, and add a unit test for that case.
close_reason: Added \r to quoting condition in csv_field and extended the unit test to cover bare carriage return

title: test: add integration tests for dispatch/transfer callback handlers
description:
**File**: `teamserver/src/dispatch/transfer.rs`
**Functions with no tests**:
- `pub(super) async fn handle_transfer_callback` (line 20) — dispatches transfer subcommands (List, Upload, Download) by parsing a binary callback payload; formats a file-transfer status table when subcommand is List
- `pub(super) async fn handle_mem_file_callback` (line 145) — parses a mem-file callback, emits an operator event with the captured bytes, and persists loot
- `pub(super) async fn handle_package_dropped_callback` (line 169) — parses a package-dropped callback and broadcasts a structured operator event
- `pub(super) async fn handle_beacon_output_callback` (line 191) — parses free-form beacon output, persists credentials if present, and emits output and loot events

**Why this matters**: These handlers sit directly in the agent → teamserver callback path. A parse error or off-by-one in the binary payload reader would silently drop or corrupt transfer-related agent responses — the kind of bug that only surfaces during a live operation.

**Minimum test scenarios needed**:
1. `handle_transfer_callback` List subcommand — craft a binary payload with one active download entry; assert the returned `None` (no reply) and that the formatted output contains the file name and progress percentage
2. `handle_transfer_callback` with truncated payload — assert `CommandDispatchError::InvalidCallbackPayload` is returned
3. `handle_mem_file_callback` — supply a valid mem-file payload; assert the operator event is broadcast and loot is stored
4. `handle_beacon_output_callback` — supply output containing a credential marker; assert credential is persisted and the output event is emitted

**Test location**: inline `#[cfg(test)]` in `teamserver/src/dispatch/transfer.rs` using the same mock-agent helpers used by `dispatch/filesystem.rs` tests
close_reason: added five integration tests covering handle_transfer_callback (List subcommand + truncated), handle_mem_file_callback, handle_package_dropped_callback, and handle_beacon_output_callback with credential persistence

title: security: TLS private key stored without Zeroizing wrapper in common/src/tls.rs
description:
Introduced by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

common/src/tls.rs — TlsIdentity struct stores the rustls PrivateKey as a plain Vec<u8> without a Zeroizing<> wrapper.

AgentEncryptionInfo in common/src/domain.rs correctly wraps AES keys with Zeroizing<Vec<u8>>, establishing a zeroization policy for key material. The TLS private key is exempt from this policy despite being equally sensitive.

On a long-running process with TLS cert reloads, the old private key bytes may persist in freed heap memory until overwritten. Consistent application of the key-material policy requires wrapping the private key field in Zeroizing<Vec<u8>>.

Fix: change the private key storage type to Zeroizing<Vec<u8>> in TlsIdentity (or whichever struct holds the decoded key bytes in common/src/tls.rs).
close_reason: Wrapped TlsIdentity::private_key_pem in Zeroizing<Vec<u8>> to apply the same key-material zeroization policy used by AgentEncryptionInfo.

title: dev01-claude stuck in repeated claim/interrupt loop on 15+ test tasks
description:
Introduced by: Ubuntu-C2-dev01-claude

In the review period 73cb663..a73963a, Ubuntu-C2-dev01-claude is repeatedly claiming and immediately interrupting the same test tasks with zero code output. Each task is being claimed-then-interrupted at least twice (some three times) with ~5-minute cycles:

Affected issues (all in_progress, blocking other agents):
red-cell-c2-8gri, red-cell-c2-upwv, red-cell-c2-0eb7, red-cell-c2-y4fs, red-cell-c2-yxty, red-cell-c2-xypk, red-cell-c2-5b4v, red-cell-c2-doop, red-cell-c2-rqen, red-cell-c2-3b4x, red-cell-c2-ldu0, red-cell-c2-enwz, red-cell-c2-vidy, red-cell-c2-zpn2, red-cell-c2-uyk8, red-cell-c2-m47p, red-cell-c2-t4fy

Evidence: e.g. red-cell-c2-8gri was claimed at c1fc789, interrupted at 0875bcd, reclaimed at 877683e, interrupted again at a73963a — all with only log/beads file changes and no test code.

**Impact**: These 17 issues are stuck in_progress and not visible in `br ready`, blocking other agents from picking them up. Dev01-claude is consuming session slots without producing output.

**Fix**: The dev01 agent should reset these issues to open status so other agents can claim them, or investigate what is causing the repeated context-limit interruptions.

title: Teamserver - Core Infrastructure
description: Core teamserver infrastructure: Axum HTTP server setup, SQLite database layer (sqlx), in-memory agent registry, and event broadcast system. This is the backbone that all other teamserver components build upon.
close_reason: Core infrastructure verified complete: Axum server setup, SQLite/sqlx persistence, agent registry, and event bus are implemented and passing workspace quality gates.

title: test: operator_agent_info with no parent pivot leaves pivot_parent empty
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/agent_events.rs
**Gap type**: edge case

`operator_agent_info` maps `PivotInfo.parent: Option<u32>` to two fields:
- `pivots.parent` — should be `None`
- `pivot_parent` — should be `String::new()` (the empty string fall-through from `unwrap_or_default`)

The existing tests only exercise the `parent: Some(_)` path. The `parent: None` case (a standalone agent that is not a pivot child) is never tested.

If the fall-back logic changed from `unwrap_or_default()` to `unwrap_or("root")` or similar, operators would see a spurious parent ID in the session list and no test would catch it.

**Suggested test approach**:
Call `operator_agent_info` with `PivotInfo { parent: None, children: vec![] }` and assert `info.pivots.parent.is_none()`, `info.pivot_parent.is_empty()`, and `info.pivots.links.is_empty()`.
close_reason: Added test for parent: None path; assert pivot_parent is empty, pivots.parent is None, and pivots.links is empty
